a62b87bb68b4d63f: The FormData multipart upload path in the OpenAI video generation provider uses an unguarded `fetchWithTimeout` call instead of the SSRF-protected transport, allowing redirect-based SSRF attacks to private IPs when `baseUrl` points to attacker-controlled infrastructure.
502eec281fb02782: DM pairing store approvals (`storeAllowFrom`) are incorrectly merged into the general `effectiveAllowFrom` list used to authorize room control commands, allowing users approved only for DM access to bypass room command authorization and execute control commands in any Matrix room.
f2009e2ad20a0830: During gateway hot reload, `stopChannel` unconditionally sets the `manuallyStopped` flag, and if the subsequent `startChannel` call throws before clearing it, the channel becomes permanently stuck in a manually-stopped state that blocks health monitoring and auto-recovery until the gateway is restarted.
98c9b3fd99b17fd1: When `providerId` is `undefined`, provider-scoped `ownerAllowFrom` entries (e.g., `discord:owner-123`) are incorrectly added to the owner allowlist by stripping the prefix, allowing unintended owner privilege escalation in contexts where the provider cannot be resolved.
f4bb43ed57fd8de6: When the GLM-4.7 template model is absent from the registry, spreading `null` into the synthesized GLM-5 forward-compat model leaves `baseUrl` undefined, causing the OpenAI SDK to fall back to `https://api.openai.com/v1` and misroute ZAI credentials to OpenAI's servers.
deb5fd3c1142e0a2: In `persistGeneratedOwnerDisplaySecret`, a three-way async merge using RFC 7396 JSON Merge Patch semantics incorrectly treats keys present in the freshly-read on-disk config but absent from the stale `nextConfig` snapshot as intentional deletions, causing concurrent config additions from other processes to be silently lost during the async write window.
8ffd704037e9e714: Interaction-triggered navigations (clicks, form submissions, evaluate) in `navigation-guard.ts` fail to install Playwright route interception handlers, allowing attacker-controlled pages to bypass strict SSRF protections by triggering requests to internal services through redirect chains before any validation occurs.
667f83da18d91da3: When parsing combined POSIX shell flags like `-cl`, the code incorrectly slices the characters after `c` within the same token (e.g., returning `"l"` as the command) instead of reading the command string from the next argv token, causing wrong command extraction and broken approval binding.
68d0448dd20ab9e3: Group JIDs in WhatsApp outbound target resolution are unconditionally authorized without checking the configured `groupPolicy`, `groupAllowFrom`, or `allowFrom` allowlist, allowing an agent to send reactions or messages to any WhatsApp group and bypassing the intended security policy entirely.
61c0da05d564efb3: When `useAccessGroups` is set to `false`, the `authorizeDiscordVoiceIngress` function incorrectly omits the owner allowlist (`channels.discord.allowFrom`) from its authorization checks, allowing any guild member to use voice commands instead of restricting access to only the configured owners as documented.
52b078110c00d888: The `strictInlineEval` security gate can be bypassed when versioned interpreter binaries (e.g., `python3.11`, `node20`) or symlink-resolved paths are used, because `findInterpreterSpec` relies on exact name matching against a static list that omits versioned variants, allowing persistent "allow-always" entries to be created contrary to the feature's documented guarantee.
418cd79092f7d68c: Doctor's `scanExecSafeBinCoverage` uses a fixtures-only profile merge that excludes built-in `SAFE_BIN_PROFILES`, causing `doctor --fix` to falsely flag built-in-covered binaries as unprotected and scaffold empty `{}` profiles that silently override and strip their built-in security constraints.
da79a59b8203e7a9: In `gateway-lock.ts`, prematurely returning `"dead"` when the lock owner's port is free — without verifying the PID belongs to a gateway process — allows a second process to steal the lock from a still-starting gateway, which then crashes with EADDRINUSE and deletes the stolen lock file, enabling a third gateway to start and resulting in two concurrent gateway processes running simultaneously.
44be81b52f55720c: A TOCTOU race condition in `AcpSessionManager.enforceConcurrentSessionLimit` allows concurrent session initializations to all pass the `maxConcurrentSessions` size check before any of them insert their entry into the runtime cache, causing the configured session limit to be exceeded during bursts of simultaneous session creation.
e6050c359cea62e8: The `replaceSensitiveValuesInRaw` function fails to redact secrets in raw JSON snapshots when sensitive values contain special characters (e.g., newlines, backslashes) because it searches for literal characters against JSON-escaped text, causing confirmed credential leaks through the `raw` field.
36aa5e5d85580a79: In `acp-reset-target.ts`, ACP bindings with a missing or empty `accountId` incorrectly match all accounts instead of only the default account, which can cause reset, steer, or cancel operations on non-default accounts (e.g., Slack, WhatsApp, IRC) to target the wrong agent session.
dd54a72685340858: The `hasExplicitNonStringGatewayCredentialForMode` function fails to recognize env-template SecretRefs (e.g., `"${TOKEN}"`) as explicit credentials because it only checks for non-string (object-form) SecretRefs, causing auto-generation to silently overwrite user-configured string-typed SecretRef tokens with plaintext generated values when the referenced environment variable is absent at startup.
ba572f345908147c: The `agent_call` function ignores `SkillDeclaration.timeoutSeconds` and always falls back to a hard-coded 60-second timeout when the caller omits `timeoutSeconds`, rendering the per-skill default configuration field ineffective and causing long-running skills to prematurely return `working`.
30b4e8345cc978de: The configured `embedding.dimensions` value is used to size the LanceDB schema but is never forwarded to the embeddings API call, causing a vector shape mismatch at runtime when a non-default dimension is set.
f646fb9103a74a74: When smart routing reroutes a model, it silently drops the `localFallback` and `orchestratorFallback` targets from `resolveOrchestratorRoute()`, causing orchestrator outages to abort the run entirely instead of falling back to the local model as intended.
918d1bcbaa38c6ee: The `mustWait` check only evaluates queue length and `lastDoneAt` without accounting for an already in-flight send, allowing a second `run()` call to bypass the throttle and execute concurrently while the first `fn()` is still running, defeating the intended serialization and rate-limit protection.
8693d7e1de3c0c81: The `doctor --fix` cleanup incorrectly deletes active session write locks based solely on a 30-minute staleness threshold, even though locks can be legitimately held for up to ~24.8 days, because cross-process cleanup has no visibility into the in-process `HELD_LOCKS` registry, risking concurrent write corruption to session files.
12a1ad9ca178dfd7: The newly implemented MCP manager is never wired into the runtime tool list returned by `createOpenClawTools`, making MCP tools and resource context completely unreachable in production even when `agents.*.mcp.servers` is configured.
1f4b461254ddf446: The output guard is applied only to final payloads after `runEmbeddedAttempt` completes, leaving the messaging tool delivery path unguarded and allowing unsafe content to reach users before any blocking or redaction can occur.
56c124b630fa99e4: The session store is unnecessarily written on every agent run even when no messages were sent and no dedupe state changed, causing avoidable write lock contention and `updatedAt` timestamp churn on the reply hot path.
9848257531bcc8b9: The `normalizeSlackText` function aggressively collapses all intra-line whitespace and trims each line, which corrupts meaningful formatting in Block Kit content such as code snippets, indentation-sensitive configs, and markdown tables.
1acb4c93c17fbb66: Bumping `MenuBarExtraAccess` to 1.3.0 is unsafe because the new version requires `menuBarExtraAccess(...)` to be the first modifier on `MenuBarExtra`, but `MenuBar.swift` still applies `.menuBarExtraStyle(.menu)` first, which can break status-item initialization and menu/session behavior at runtime.
5fa2943ca1c3e34c: The new override that silently corrects a non-loopback bind host when Tailscale serve/funnel is enabled is unreachable in practice because upstream startup config validation still fatally rejects that exact configuration before `resolveGatewayRuntimeConfig` is ever called.
bd5b1479eecd2cc2: The Matrix transport layer strips Authorization headers on cross-origin redirects but fails to also strip request bodies on 307/308 redirects, allowing sensitive POST/PUT payloads (messages, passwords, cryptographic keys) to be forwarded to attacker-controlled origins.
8411d5338f819492: The new `zaiProvider` object is never registered in `buildMediaUnderstandingRegistry`, making it unreachable at runtime and causing audio transcription calls to fail with a "Media provider not available: zai" error.
6411f5296cb80702: The new `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` fallback in `importResolvedPluginSdkSubpath` silently imports source TypeScript files directly instead of the published `dist` targets, causing the smoke test to pass even when the plugin-sdk's export map is broken for downstream consumers.
76ae9dbe0e8b9f49: The `sanitizeUserFacingText` function's generic `model_not_found` rewrite strips out the original error's concrete model identifier and recovery hints (e.g., suggested replacement model names), causing users to lose actionable guidance and see only a vague "The selected model is not available" message.
9b981a8022dd39b3: In `releaseLock()`, the lock file is never closed before `fs.rm()` is called, causing Windows to reject the unlink with a silently swallowed `EPERM` error, which leaves stale lock files behind and forces subsequent lock acquisitions to wait for the full TTL or timeout even after the previous operation has completed.
13e29fa607bf05c1: The `refreshLock` function performs a single `handle.write(...)` call without retrying on partial writes, which can corrupt the on-disk JSON lock payload and cause other processes to incorrectly reclaim the lock after TTL expiry while the original critical section is still running.
5c88cdf22baef0d8: In `mapContainerPathToWorkspaceRoot`, the "already a host path" fast path runs before container-path remapping, so bind mounts with `hostRoot: "/"` cause every absolute sandbox path to match and return unchanged, resulting in incorrect host path resolution where paths like `/host/etc/hosts` are used instead of the correctly remapped `/etc/hosts`.
3728fb04b156fd73: The new `QQbot` plugin entry was appended after `WeChat` rather than inserted before it, breaking the required alphabetical ordering of the community plugins list.
b6004572bdfae3e4: When a named account explicitly sets a token field to an empty string, the new base-credential gate incorrectly suppresses the `default` account ID, causing `listAccountIds()` to omit the valid base account and preventing its gateway from starting at runtime.
c5c863ff02edb2b3: The `baseTokenFields.length > 0` guard incorrectly injects a `default` account even when the base config is only partially authenticated, causing `resolveDefaultAccountId()` to select an unconfigured account over valid named accounts for channels like Slack and Mattermost that require additional fields beyond a token to be runnable.
bcaf88a5515764d9: A new basename guard in `resolveEntrypointFromPackageJson` causes renamed `.cmd` shims (e.g., `qmd-beta.cmd`) to return early before attempting the adjacent `package.json` lookup, silently breaking Windows plugin resolution when `resolveCliSpawnInvocation` is called with `allowShellFallback: false`.
39ed84e250d7598c: The code incorrectly injects an implicit `default` account based solely on the presence of token-like fields in the base config, without verifying that the base account has all required channel-specific settings (e.g., `baseUrl` for Mattermost, `signingSecret` for Slack), which can cause account-less operations to resolve to an unconfigured and non-functional default account instead of a properly configured named account.
9d615c888d65ad38: The predicate that decides whether to inject a `default` account only checks for inline token fields and ignores `tokenFile`-based accounts, causing the base `default` provider to never be started when a Zalo account is configured via `tokenFile` alongside a top-level bot token.
a6db0870b190ecee: After a tab-close action, the fallback logic in `getPageForTargetId()` returns the surviving tab's URL instead of the closed tab's URL, causing the enriched `url` field in close responses to be incorrect and potentially misattributing audit or policy logs to the wrong page.
bc2f1069947b26c4: The `voicewake.routing.set` handler lacks sufficient input validation, allowing malformed payloads to silently overwrite the persisted routing configuration with coerced defaults instead of returning an `INVALID_REQUEST` error.
61be0901c6d5c49e: Malformed `agentId` values in voice-wake route targets (e.g. `"!!!"`) are silently normalized to `"main"` by `normalizeAgentId` instead of being rejected, causing wake-word traffic to be misrouted to the default agent without any validation error.
e619f62951a503da: The `parseAgentSessionKey` function silently accepts malformed session keys containing empty path segments (e.g., `agent:main::`), which pass the `classifySessionKeyShape` validation check and can cause voice-wake routes to silently create or resolve the wrong session instead of rejecting the bad input.
c96e731874b044ea: The mention-stripping regex uses `[:\s]*` as a separator, allowing zero-length matches that can incorrectly rewrite non-command messages like `@bot:example.org/new` into `/new`, potentially triggering unintended command execution.
c422b344356c8a5e: A condition change that requires a colon (`:`) in room ID keys causes bare `!roomId` entries in matrix room/group config to be incorrectly routed through `resolveMatrixTargets` instead of being retained verbatim, silently dropping room-specific allow/deny and prompt settings for those rooms.
ca4323c153d35ae2: Changing `stdio` from `"pipe"` to `["ignore", "ignore", "ignore"]` sets `proc.stderr` to `null`, swallowing Chrome's diagnostic output and turning actionable startup errors into opaque failure messages that are difficult to debug.
ab3d98509d5167b2: When the recovery deadline is exceeded before entries are processed, the MAX_RETRIES check is bypassed, causing entries to accumulate ever-increasing retry counts indefinitely without ever being moved to the failed state as intended.
1bf85dcd8e026bf4: When the recovery time budget is exhausted, the deadline-exceeded branch fails to check `MAX_RETRIES` before processing remaining entries, allowing deliveries that have already hit the retry limit to persist in `pending/` indefinitely instead of being archived to `failed/`.
ace9f533922b9751: The restart deferral logic emits SIGUSR1 immediately without waiting for async pre-restart hooks to complete, causing race conditions where the restart sentinel may not be written before the process restarts, leading to lost restart notifications on startup.
037ec23908f707e1: The forget candidate list displays truncated 8-character IDs, but the delete flow requires full UUIDs, causing `memory_forget` to silently fail when users submit a displayed ID and breaking the two-step forget workflow.
7055435fc7c67b5e: In the smart-capture correction path, the old memory is deleted before the replacement is successfully persisted, meaning a failure in any subsequent async step (e.g., LLM call or DB write) will silently erase both the old and new facts with no way to recover them.
6a6eba4f93d5a9bc: The plugin manifest schema blocks `provider` and `outputDimensionality` fields with `additionalProperties: false`, even though the runtime config parser supports them, causing valid embedding options to be silently rejected before the plugin ever executes.
1ab9e2ba1db70966: The `updated-message` noise gate incorrectly discards media-only events with empty text by routing them to the `return true` (drop) path, causing inbound photo and sticker attachments delivered via BlueBubbles' `updated-message` webhook to never reach `processMessage`.
4271e58a325efe86: The new fallback that infers `messageId` from `toolContext.currentMessageTs` for `react`/`reactions` actions is effectively unreachable because the message-tool execution pipeline never propagates `currentMessageTs` into `toolContext`, causing Slack reaction calls that omit `messageId` to continue failing instead of using the inferred timestamp.
66ce17bbacac386a: Calling `loadAuthProfileStore()` to inspect a secondary agent's credentials unexpectedly triggers `syncExternalCliCredentials()`, which can silently copy unrelated third-party OAuth secrets into the target agent's store before the user is prompted to reuse any credentials.
1100c27361a52424: When multiple custom providers share the same base URL but have different credentials, a first-match lookup in `promptBaseUrlAndKey` may incorrectly reuse and persist an unrelated provider's API key into the new provider being configured.
0009d85307930cd9: Returning `null` when `models.copilotDiscovery.enabled` is `false` inadvertently removes the synthesized Copilot provider for profile-backed authentication setups, causing model IDs like `github-copilot/gpt-4o` to lose their provider backing and fail, because the toggle should only skip the startup token discovery exchange rather than disabling all Copilot usage for existing auth profiles.
bb94b0287c14d7dc: The streamed preview post is deleted before the fallback delivery is confirmed successful, meaning a failure in `deliverMattermostReplyPayload` leaves the user with no visible response at all instead of preserving the partial reply.
8f4c0d990c7fc15b: A UTF-8 BOM character (`\uFEFF`) was accidentally prepended to `docs/plugins/community.md`, causing frontmatter parsers that rely on a literal `---` prefix check to fail to recognize the file's frontmatter, which breaks documentation listing and i18n metadata processing for that page.
e89b037c005cce5e: When `streamMessageId` is still `null` during the first in-flight preview send and `replyTargetDiverged` is true, the final reply is delivered immediately without awaiting or canceling the pending preview, causing a stray orphaned post to appear in the original thread if the in-flight send resolves afterward.
e2996e95e9541940: When a custom endpoint's saved provider ID collides with a built-in provider namespace (e.g., "openai" or "anthropic"), reusing that ID in `ensureApiKeyFromEnvOrPrompt` causes the built-in provider's credentials to be offered and potentially accepted, which can overwrite the custom endpoint's stored config with the wrong key.
b323f34ebbee3412: If a run completes without a final payload before the first 200ms timer tick, the no-final cleanup guard is bypassed because `streamMessageId` and `patchSending` are still falsy, causing the `patchInterval` to fire post-settlement, post a stray preview message to Mattermost, and never be cleared.
799b20a6d5a5e121: When a stored API key references an environment variable marker, the code resolves it to a plaintext secret before persisting it via `setCredential()`, silently overwriting the env-backed configuration with an inline secret and breaking future environment-variable-based key rotation.
afd8ce39b0aed591: After a `patchMattermostPost()` failure, `streamMessageId` is never cleared and `lastSentText` stays stale, causing the 200ms interval to repeatedly retry the failing patch API call for the entire duration of a long response, spamming logs and leaving the preview frozen on outdated text.
5b3e2f2456af70d5: After a patch interval is stopped due to a send/edit failure, `onPartialReply` can still invoke `schedulePatch()` on subsequent deltas, which recreates the interval timer whenever `patchInterval` is null, causing repeated failing API calls and log spam for the duration of the stream instead of halting retries after the first failure.
5db493a3cb0dc580: A synchronous `sendMessageMattermost()` call in `flushPendingPatch()` has no timeout guard, so a stalled or hung Mattermost API response can block final reply delivery indefinitely when a fast model response completes before the 200ms streaming timer fires.
e2c833a30b4729ad: When a streaming preview POST or PATCH request times out via `Promise.race`, the underlying HTTP request is never cancelled, allowing it to complete late and either create an orphaned draft post or silently overwrite the already-finalized message due to stale `streamMessageId` state.
5c8b5bcb178302ae: The health check watchdog that restarts stale connections only calls `stopRunner()` without a `forceCyclePromise` fallback, so a hung update handler can block `runner.task()` indefinitely and prevent the restart from ever completing.
bf3c0e4569606f46: When only one of `notifyChannel` or `notifyTarget` is provided, the subagent spawns successfully but silently skips the completion notification, leaving the caller with no ping and no error to indicate the misconfiguration.
1510f000060e8b35: The memory flush run partially replays stored sender/source metadata but still reads `messageProvider` and `agentAccountId` from the live session context, meaning if the session's channel or account changes between the original user turn and the deferred flush, hook policy and message-tool calls could target the wrong channel or account.
69c713a0b06194a0: The new `sourceProvider` and sender identity fields added to `PluginHookAgentContext` are never populated when `runBeforeReset()` is called in `commands-core.ts`, meaning trust-aware plugin policies are silently bypassed for user-triggered reset commands.
e38149990eb632cf: The `handleCompactCommand()` wrapper does not pass `sourceProvider` when calling `compactEmbeddedPiSession()`, so manual `/compact` invocations will expose `sourceProvider: undefined` to before/after compaction hooks in relayed or cross-channel sessions, causing trust-aware plugins to incorrectly classify manual compactions differently from normal agent turns.
1464c270b4a99feb: The newly added fields (`senderName`, `groupId`, `spawnedBy`, `sourceProvider`) are declared in `EmbeddedCompactionRuntimeContext` but are never actually passed through by the runtime builders in `attempt.ts` and `run.ts`, causing context engines to always see these fields as `undefined` and potentially leading to incorrect trust or policy logic.
0248a40aae8cd2fe: The `context_assembled` hook exposes the live `imageResult.images` array without cloning it, meaning any plugin that mutates `event.images` during the hook will nondeterministically corrupt the actual vision payload sent to the model.
0c1460e8b3a21894: Loop hook payloads omit `runId` and `attemptIndex`, so when `runEmbeddedAttempt` retries after compaction, auth refresh, or truncation, handlers cannot distinguish a fresh attempt's `iteration: 1` from the original, causing per-turn telemetry to be ambiguous or double-counted.
5c4c3c8ba111dc9c: `pendingToolResults` is always initialized to `0` instead of being seeded from existing session history, causing the counter to undercount tool results already present at the tail of history when a retried attempt resumes after tool-result truncation recovery, which breaks retry telemetry and token accounting for plugins on the first model turn.
8a81c37dde813ba2: Removing `resolvePinnedHostnameWithPolicy` from the non-pinned DNS code path inadvertently bypasses all SSRF hostname and IP validation, allowing callers who set `pinDns: false` to reach blocked or private targets without triggering a `SsrFBlockedError`.
f587af337430b611: The updated test now depends on a prebuilt `dist/plugin-sdk` directory instead of building its own output, causing it to fail on clean checkouts or in CI environments where `pnpm build` has not been run before `pnpm test`.
0afba6b6ae989afb: The helper builds session keys using simple lowercasing instead of the canonical `normalizeAgentId` logic, causing `peekSystemEvents` to look up a different key than the one used during enqueueing, which makes `status` silently report an empty queue even when pending events exist for agent IDs containing spaces or punctuation.
e0c2106e234d9ce5: When all documents in a batch resolve to `null` (e.g., due to Telegram's 20MB limit or exhausted retries), the new early-return logic silently drops the entire batch instead of forwarding placeholder turns to the agent as the original per-message path did.
c25be646c547d078: Batched document processing calls `processMessage()` directly instead of routing through `inboundDebouncer.enqueue()`, causing downstream exceptions to be silently swallowed rather than surfaced to the user as error replies in Telegram.
b687cb1f4c05b466: The early return in the document batch handling branch bypasses `inboundDebouncer.enqueue`, causing forwarded bursts containing both text and documents to split into two separate agent turns instead of being coalesced, because the text flushes after 80ms while documents wait up to 1.5s in the `documentBatchBuffer`.
6860280252498eed: Because document messages are buffered for up to 1.5 seconds while subsequent non-document messages (e.g., text commands) are enqueued immediately, a follow-up instruction can reach the agent before the buffered file attachments, reversing message order and causing the agent to process commands without the intended documents.
62501388143edc43: The new `talk.ptt.*` commands invoked by `handlePTTCommand` are never added to the gateway's allowlist, causing all `/ptt` requests to be rejected with "command not allowlisted" on a default configuration, making the feature completely unusable without manual operator intervention.
f51a1eda052650a4: `handlePTTCommand` is wired into the command handler but `/ptt` was never registered in the shared commands registry, causing it to be treated as plain text by inbound debouncers, which can result in burst messages being incorrectly merged, and the command remaining absent from slash-command menus.
a5308848ee56d76c: When all batched documents exceed the size limit, the caption text still incorrectly falls through to the agent queue despite no files being successfully processed, causing the agent to respond to a message without ever receiving its attachments.
ca8c67456fa16f95: When multiple documents with captions are batched together, only one caption is preserved as the `primaryEntry`, causing all other captions or instructions attached to subsequent documents to be silently dropped before reaching the agent.
f766b8b9daf13831: A debug `console.log` statement was left in `enqueueSystemEvent()`, which is called on all inbound traffic, causing user message content and session metadata to be unconditionally leaked to stdout in production, bypassing the repo's logger controls.
70a606a91ccb7e7e: The `documentDebounceLane !== "forward"` guard incorrectly excludes all forwarded documents from the new batching buffer, so forwarded PDFs and other documents without `media_group_id` are never grouped and still arrive as multiple separate turns instead of a single combined one.
1c3d2503be1dc06e: When all batched documents fail to resolve, the fallback path dispatches each failed message once via `processMessage` but then incorrectly enqueues a second caption-only batch turn, causing the agent to receive the same captioned document message twice when batching is enabled.
98008e4c3aa3fbed: Document batching logic incorrectly groups unrelated Telegram channel posts within a 1.5-second window by collapsing them to the same synthetic sender ID, causing distinct messages to be merged into a single agent turn with no way to recover the original message boundaries.
d6fc1edbcf5c6a79: The new `exa` provider is not added to the config schema's allowed provider enum, so users cannot explicitly configure it and must rely on fragile auto-detection, breaking explicit provider selection.
d4fdec9fd6e21fc6: The `runTestCase` function never actually invokes the skill or model, instead hardcoding `actual` from the prompt input, causing `skills test`/`bench` commands to evaluate a fabricated response rather than real skill output—making test results meaningless.
eb43650bd9107348: The Exa plugin is registered via the plugin SDK but never actually reachable in production because the live `web_search` tool path in `web-search.ts` only resolves a hard-coded set of providers (brave, perplexity, grok, gemini, kimi) and does not include Exa, meaning the feature silently never activates even when `EXA_API_KEY` is set.
50e655e74e89cd7c: When eval cases fail, `runSkillTest` and `runSkillBench` do not return a non-zero exit code, causing CI pipelines to incorrectly report success even when test cases have failed.
74bdf47cf54384c6: The `resolveSkillPath` function only looks up skills in `<workspace>/skills/<name>`, failing to check all other supported skill discovery locations (managed skills, `~/.agents/skills`, `<workspace>/.agents/skills`, and configured extra/plugin dirs), causing `skills test|bench|analyze-trigger` subcommands to incorrectly report skills from those locations as missing.
9bc1ea72c27480cd: The regex used to parse the `description:` field from YAML frontmatter only captures the inline value on the same line, so skills using YAML block scalar syntax (e.g., `description: |`) will have their description reduced to the literal character `"|"`, causing `analyze-trigger` to produce incorrect low-relevance or false-negative scores instead of evaluating against the actual description text.
a801c93e2f9e243a: The unconditional regex stripping of collect-mode queue markers in `stripStructuralPrefixes()` allows a user to trigger live commands like `/stop` or `/new` simply by pasting a collect-mode prompt into chat, because the function is also used for inbound command detection without verifying the input actually originated from a trusted collect wrapper.
2f46793e3cd8a48c: The regex in `stripStructuralPrefixes` only removes `---`/`Queued #N` headers but fails to strip overflow summary blocks prepended when the queue exceeds its cap, causing `isAbortRequestText()` to still miss `/stop` commands in the high-backlog scenarios where collect mode is most commonly triggered.
8197e1ee7ada3b4b: The Querit search result mapper silently drops the `page_age` field instead of mapping it to the standard `published` field, causing recency signals to be lost when using Querit on time-sensitive queries.
99a005bfa9b8141a: Stripping `---\nQueued #N` markers from arbitrary inbound text can cause pasted or quoted collect-mode prompts containing inline directives (e.g., `/fast`, `/think`) to be incorrectly recognized and applied as real user directives, unintentionally mutating session settings.
3435d503cb12f78c: Querit's `page_age` field returns full ISO timestamps (e.g., `2025-07-20T16:00:00Z`), but `normalizeToIsoDate` only handles bare dates, causing `published` to always be written as `undefined` and stripping recency signals from API responses.
3dc45d893ba9821d: The updated `ignored` predicate incorrectly filters out all non-`.md` files, silently preventing multimodal files (e.g., `.png`, `.wav`) in `extraPaths` from ever triggering watcher updates and leaving them perpetually stale in the index when multimodal support is enabled.
a4e8a6a291b09136: `toProviderQualifiedModelKey` incorrectly bails out for slash-containing model IDs instead of combining the separate `provider` and `id` fields into a qualified key, causing provider-specific context window limits to be conflated across providers under the same upstream model ID.
748be7f75f6c3717: When `deliverOutboundPayloads` is called with only a `replyToId` and no `quoteAuthor`, Signal group messages silently drop native reply/quote behavior because the Signal send layer requires both fields to be present to attach quote metadata.
f2bf0beb7f95b298: When multiple Signal messages are debounced and batched together, combining all message text but only retaining the most recent `quoteTarget` causes a plain follow-up message to be misattributed as part of a quoted reply, resulting in incorrect `ReplyTo*` context.
9bd59dec57fe4e42: The `announceTimeoutMs` budget is incorrectly derived from the caller-supplied `timeoutSeconds` rather than using an independent, larger window, meaning that short-timeout callers will still have late replies dropped because the follow-up `agent.wait` and announce steps are constrained by the same tight deadline they were trying to escape.
a0b5dd6c9429da49: When a payload has `mediaUrl` set but `mediaUrls` is an empty array, the nullish coalescing operator (`??`) incorrectly treats the empty array as a valid value, causing `resolvedMediaList` to be empty and silently dropping the media attachment instead of falling back to the single `mediaUrl`.
4685b1ddb6935ab2: Removing `workflow_dispatch` from the Docker release workflow eliminates the only way to manually retrigger or backfill GHCR image publishing for existing release tags, leaving no recovery path if a Docker publish fails.
5486cf1033d9618a: The session-saved info log is emitted immediately after the inline write rather than after post-hook actions, meaning it can create a false-positive audit trail and potentially persist sensitive data in logs even when a later hook blocks or redacts the session file.
1222761a33ba95fe: When `blockSessionSave` is pre-set to `true` with a `sessionSaveRedirectPath`, the handler unnecessarily reads the full target file into memory for a rollback snapshot that can never be used, wasting I/O and spiking memory on large files.
dd373848781f72ec: In queued followup turns, auto-compaction incorrectly sends a hardcoded "🧹 Compacting context..." chat message instead of calling the existing `opts.onCompactionStart`/`opts.onCompactionEnd` callbacks, causing inconsistent behavior on Discord and Telegram where normal turns use platform-specific reaction controllers instead of raw chat messages.
529872b3f6157595: Restricted plugins can bypass the `allowPromptInjection=false` guard and modify the messages sent to the model by mutating `event.messages` in place, because the registry strips returned fields but does not deep-clone the event object passed to `before_llm_call` handlers.
5dbbd84ba2cf4d84: Normalizing `sessionKey: "current"` to `requesterInternalKey` before gateway lookup prevents legitimate sessions whose key or ID is literally `"current"` from ever being resolved, silently redirecting those calls to the caller's own session instead.
9eb0b60c5822f707: Replacing the multi-match resolution helper with `Object.entries(...).find(...)` breaks deterministic session lookup, causing `session_status` to arbitrarily resolve to the first matching store entry rather than the preferred one when multiple keys share the same `sessionId`, risking stale or invalid session resolution.
c850a1bb565b766a: Using `replaceAll` with a string replacement for `@_user_N` placeholders in merged-forward messages corrupts display names containing `$` characters due to special substitution pattern handling, and should instead use a callback-based replacement like `normalizeMentions()` already does elsewhere in the plugin.
41b635091dabe25e: The `resolveSessionEntry()` function fails to generate agent-scoped session key candidates (e.g., `agent:support:main`) when resolving `sessionKey:"current"`, causing `session_status` to incorrectly return "Unknown sessionKey" instead of the caller's own session for non-default agents.
74c838f8e2efb68d: The compaction completion notice is unconditionally injected as a chat message even on channels that already surface compaction status via callback-driven reactions, resulting in duplicate UX (both a reaction flow and an extra chat message) instead of being gated behind the same callback check used by the start path.
a0fbe4b074a66c1c: When `rawInput === "current"` in a sandboxed (`restrictToSpawned: true`) context, the literal key lookup resolves before alias fallback but ignores `spawnedBy` constraints, so an unrelated session named "current" in the store can match first and block resolution of the requester's own session, causing `sessions_history`/`sessions_send` to silently fail or return incorrect results.
43da9d4da426bf14: The reordered candidate list causes `session_status("main")` to incorrectly resolve to the stale legacy key `agent:main:main` before the live alias `global`, potentially attaching the status card and model overrides to the wrong session entry when both keys coexist in the store.
01194434f50c2a22: Including `RECOVERABLE_TOOL_ERROR_KEYWORDS` (which contains "missing") in `SELF_CORRECTABLE_TOOL_ERROR_KEYWORDS` causes hard, unrecoverable mutating tool failures — such as Discord "missing permissions" errors — to be silently suppressed as self-correctable, hiding the failure warning from users even though the action never succeeded.
6a22404317d4914d: The desktop splash screen hardcodes the gateway probe URL without accounting for the configured `basePath`, causing the health check to always fail and leaving the app stuck on the loading screen when `gateway.controlUi.basePath` is set.
b0c746d156c64d5d: When a non-default agent resolves the special key `"current"`, the new `sessions.resolve` gateway call canonicalizes it against the default-store agent rather than the requester, causing session operations like `sessions_history` and `sessions_send` to silently target an unrelated session belonging to the default agent instead of the caller's own session.
d689471592030467: The new test calls `transcribeOpenAiCompatibleAudio`, which is not imported in the file, causing a `ReferenceError` at runtime that breaks the test suite and leaves the intended filename-extension behavior unverified.
c29259915e06388f: The duplicate-ID check in `cron.add` unconditionally calls `.toLowerCase()` on every stored job's `id` without guarding against non-string or missing `id` values that can exist in malformed or legacy-corrupt JSON stores, causing a `TypeError` that breaks all add operations until the store file is manually repaired.
0a649488dbc3aba0: When a circuit breaker trips, the wake is dropped from `pendingWakes` without scheduling a follow-up probe after `BREAKER_COOLDOWN_MS`, so recovery can be delayed far beyond the intended cooldown window in interval-driven deployments.
409eb4c693f9146a: Renaming the `append` parameter to `appendExisting` in `openWritableFileWithinRoot` is a breaking SDK change that causes existing callers passing `append: true` to silently truncate files instead of appending, risking data loss.
deedaf25bfc53b3f: Although `registerSubagentRun` now stores `requesterMessageId` on the registry entry, the downstream `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` function never reads and forwards `entry.requesterMessageId` to `runSubagentAnnounceFlow`, causing completion announcements to omit `currentMessageId` and fall back to unthreaded delivery, defeating the reply-binding this change intended to preserve.
684a494cbd32851f: Adding "querit" to the bundled web search ID list without updating the corresponding provider lookup map causes `resolveBundledWebSearchPluginId("querit")` to return `undefined`, breaking production code that relies on this helper to map Querit back to its plugin ID.
414818bf94f1a93f: The updated `stripSpecialMarkupFromText` function fails to remove standalone separator headers (e.g., `#+#+#+#+#` or `#+#+#+#+# assistant to=final`) because only `NO_REPLY` remainders are treated as removable, allowing internal scaffolding markers to leak through to users in normal reply flows.
f5655f6af5748a22: The new `closeAll()` method clears `INDEX_CACHE_PENDING` without awaiting in-flight manager creation promises, allowing those promises to later re-insert live managers with open FSWatcher handles into the already-cleared cache, defeating the existing race-safe teardown logic.
bfd8a27075e3700c: Accepting negative chat IDs (e.g., `-100...`) as valid `allowFrom`/`groupAllowFrom` entries causes ordinary group messages to be incorrectly denied, because the group policy code treats any non-empty `effectiveGroupAllow` as a required sender allowlist and authorization still checks `msg.from.id` rather than the negative chat ID.
5859684b579a7dd3: When a streamed answer falls back to a normal send and sets `answerLaneNeedsBoundaryReset`, a subsequent final-only assistant turn without `onAssistantMessageStart` may incorrectly edit the stale preview message instead of sending a fresh message, corrupting the conversation transcript.
2a8df02c3db5dabd: The `startAutoConnect` function silently drops connection attempts if `gatewayAutoReconnectEnabled` is false, but since `disconnectGateway()` sets this flag to false and it isn't restored until after this guard runs in `applyGatewayConnectConfig`, tapping Connect after a Disconnect results in a no-op and the gateway never reconnects.
fd62e951c6807897: When `readSubagentOutput` returns a non-empty partial progress banner after a timeout, the `!reply?.trim()` guard short-circuits the retry polling loop, causing the system to announce stale intermediate output instead of waiting to retrieve the actual final assistant message.
4ae6ac1538d2e886: The HTTP 429 rate-limit retry wrapper is only applied to the normal prompt path, leaving compaction sessions in `compact.ts` unprotected so that transient rate-limit errors during compaction will still fail immediately instead of backing off.
e5e084d3dc8a50ad: A fresh 3-step 429 backoff wrapper applied on every attempt within a loop of up to 32 retries can compound into multi-minute stalls under persistent rate limiting, because the backoff budget resets each iteration instead of being bounded across the entire run.
c30578ea871effdc: A missing import for `loadConfig` causes the function to always throw and return `undefined`, silently preventing `taglineScriptFile` from ever being read from config and causing the "script" tagline mode to render empty for users.
6abf62ab160cc336: When `preserveWebp: true` is set, WebP buffers returned by `loadWebMedia` are wrapped in an untyped `Blob` with a fallback generic filename, causing Discord to receive them as `application/octet-stream` attachments instead of inline images when no filename or extension is available in the URL.
840d570bdd6a86e0: The optimization bypass logic fails to account for animated PNGs (APNGs), which share the `image/png` MIME type and will still be passed through the image optimization pipeline, causing them to be silently flattened to a single static frame.
d097854796c40419: The new `preserveWebp` path uses the default 6 MB image cap instead of Discord's larger upload cap, causing valid 6–8 MB WebP files to fail in Discord interaction-reply and component-send flows that set `preserveWebp: true` without supplying `maxBytes`.
e5b2e3703a7517d5: Gating separator removal solely on `didStripMetadata` is insufficient because legacy messages lacking an injected `---\n**User Message:**` divider may have user content that legitimately begins with those lines, causing valid content to be silently stripped after an upgrade.
072d195ab480b1aa: Replacing the wildcard `export *` in `runtime-api.ts` with a single named type export drops critical runtime exports (e.g., `createActionCard`, `processLineMessage`, `CardAction`) that `extensions/line/api.ts` depends on, causing LINE plugin loading to fail.
32316b55963927e5: The separator-removal logic fails to strip the `---` and `**User Message:**` markers in threaded or event-prefixed turns because `hasLeadingMetadataWithSep` remains false when `_sep` is encountered after prepended context has already set `seenUserContent` to true, leaving separator artifacts in sanitized history.
fb6dc4a46c1d8912: The `buildMediaUnderstandingRegistryAsync` function omits the `cfg` argument when calling the sync `buildMediaUnderstandingRegistry`, causing user-configured media providers and their settings to be silently dropped when callers use the async API.
3ddc60a15203ba8c: The typo-rejection heuristic runs before plugin provider lookup, causing valid custom memory providers whose IDs resemble built-ins (e.g., `openaix`, `ollama2`) to be incorrectly rejected at config-validation time instead of being matched against loaded plugins first.
5092afc106473f5f: The `requireApiKey` flag is incorrectly derived from whether a provider is a plugin rather than from the actual handler being invoked, meaning a partial plugin override can bypass API key enforcement and cause runtime auth failures when the built-in handler is called with an empty key.
2793cf6d85eba844: A new guard that throws when a provider lacks a `describeImage` handler inadvertently removes the existing fallback path for standard image model providers (e.g., OpenAI/Google) not registered in the provider registry, causing image understanding to fail with an error instead of using the built-in model-based describer.
5b2c695efa28ae15: The plugin TTS registry returns `fileExtension` without a leading dot, causing audio files to be saved with malformed names and breaking downstream MIME type inference that expects dotted extensions like `.mp3` or `.opus`.
24543315a426bd66: In auto mode, custom embedding plugins are collected but never correctly returned as a fallback because `createProvider` is called with their IDs despite only supporting built-in providers, causing an unknown-provider error instead of using the discovered plugin.
976964e642c60001: The trailing-ID fallback in `supportsXHighThinking` incorrectly grants `xhigh` thinking to any model whose last path segment matches an allowlisted ID regardless of provider, bypassing the provider/model gate and allowing unsupported `thinkingLevel: "xhigh"` configurations that will fail at execution time.
df0ea9a016538b21: Plugin TTS provider IDs are normalized (e.g., "z.ai" → "zai") before use, but the config lookup uses exact/alias keys, causing API keys and auth settings to go missing and synthesis to fail when users store provider config under an alias key.
cc6322c6b4fba4e0: `getPluginMediaProviders()` is called without passing the `cfg` configuration object, causing it to run with `config: undefined` and potentially activating a different plugin registry that drops config-scoped providers from capability-based discovery.
a4f42ead68b35aa8: Unresolved Git merge conflict markers remain in `llm-task-tool.ts`, causing invalid TypeScript syntax that will break compilation and prevent the extension from being built or consumed until the conflict is resolved.
ed0da54491cf4ab4: The import path for the bundled web-search registry was incorrectly changed from `../bundled-web-search-registry.js` to `./bundled-web-search-registry.js`, pointing to the wrong directory and causing a module-not-found error at load time.
16cf8a3147607c81: When a user skips search setup, the code still mutates tool policy and fetch settings based on the existing provider value in the config, silently altering search and fetch behavior despite the user's explicit intent to skip.
da92331f3256e3c0: The `sanitizeProfileId` function fails to strip trailing bare ESC bytes (`\x1b`) in profile IDs because the regex requires a character after `\x1b`, leaving a terminal injection vulnerability when unsanitized profile IDs are printed in terminal output.
3bb31a3ba496e5be: When the user selects "Skip for now" in the search setup, the config is still silently mutated by appending `web_search`/`web_fetch` to the allowlist and potentially toggling `tools.web.fetch.parallel.enabled`, because the post-search mutation block executes based on the presence of an existing provider rather than whether the user actually made a new selection.
94e400af65122eb5: Replacing the `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` imports with relative paths causes runtime module resolution failures because the referenced files don't exist at those relative locations, breaking the Discord plugin's ability to process messages after startup.
f119a11efc614a53: When the pending-inbound drain queue reaches its 200-entry cap, dropped messages are only logged at verbose level and Telegram still receives a success response, causing silent message loss that operators cannot detect under normal logging configurations.
97652b12c079e5ed: The refactor removed the `listBundledWebSearchPluginIds` export from `bundled-web-search.ts`, but `src/secrets/runtime-web-tools.ts` still imports it, causing ESM binding failures and TypeScript errors that break web-tools runtime diagnostics.
3e0989cbf4417c92: The empty-config guard checks `configuredProfiles.size` after store-derived IDs have already been added to it, allowing the guard to be bypassed in config-less setups and potentially deleting active store-only credentials that the guard was meant to protect.
1645a2bdb62329d2: The `imageSanitization` config is only applied to `screenshot` actions but not to labeled `snapshot` actions in `executeSnapshotAction`, causing `agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx` to be silently ignored for users relying on labeled snapshots for visual references.
b34694e0d78430bc: The empty-config safety guard checks only `configOnlyProfileIds.size` and misses providers explicitly disabled via empty `auth.order` arrays, causing `models auth clean` to abort cleanup and leave stale credentials for those disabled providers instead of removing them.
347ef4d0b3553ac2: The new unconditional catch-all in `formatAssistantErrorText` swallows recognizable plain-text errors (auth failures, model-not-found, session expiry) and replaces their actionable messages with a generic "Something went wrong," leaving users unable to take the correct recovery action.
09117989dcfaaa78: The cron field parser silently discards valid non-numeric Croner tokens (e.g., MON-FRI, JAN, L, #), causing jobs that use these expressions to incorrectly disappear from the timeline despite still being executed by the scheduler.
946152d6ff5e4a0f: The timeline's day-of-month and day-of-week checks are incorrectly combined with AND logic instead of OR logic, causing combined DOM+DOW cron expressions (e.g., `0 9 1 * 1`) to only plot dates that satisfy both conditions simultaneously, while the actual Croner runtime correctly fires on either condition, resulting in missing run markers and incorrect pending/past status labels.
0401f2cc86ca5116: Defaulting `intervalMinutes` to `15` instead of `0` silently enables periodic background sync for deployments that had explicitly disabled all other auto-sync triggers, causing unexpected CPU and embedding traffic every 15 minutes unless users discover and manually override the new default.
2b3de9b9946ce429: Switching to `splitMarkdownIRPreserveWhitespace` can cause `coalesceWhitespaceOnlyMarkdownIRChunks` to drop a trailing whitespace-only chunk when the previous chunk is at the size limit, reintroducing the very whitespace loss the change was intended to fix.
c5f91cfd87f31a2a: Because `tenant_key` is an optional field in the Feishu event contract, `isLikelyBotMention()` incorrectly classifies real user mentions with a missing `tenant_key` as bot mentions, causing legitimate forwarding flows to be silently dropped.
d3f0b79da45e7d79: RichTextBlock OpenUrl runs write unvalidated URLs directly into anchor `href` attributes, allowing disallowed protocols to be reached via non-primary navigations (e.g., middle-click) that bypass the `openExternalUrlSafe` guard.
92cae74338b1feaa: The `Action.ToggleVisibility` handler queries elements by `data-ac-id` attribute, but the renderer never emits that attribute on card elements, so toggle actions can never find their targets and will always silently no-op.
6c7c701861aea456: Treating a missing `tenant_key` as a bot indicator incorrectly discards legitimate user mentions, causing `@Bot @User` forward requests to silently fail whenever Feishu omits `tenant_key` on a real user mention.
3b65f4a7d04edea8: The `isSafeToRetrySendError` function incorrectly returns `true` for grammY's "failed after N attempts" errors, which can represent ambiguous transport failures where Telegram may have already delivered the message, causing duplicate sends on non-idempotent retry paths.
15ca37e18e6bc377: The `bindTemplateData` function coerces all template substitutions to strings, causing numeric, boolean, and object fields bound via `${...}` expressions to lose their original types and render incorrect state (e.g., a numeric `ProgressBar.value` becomes `"0%"` instead of the correct percentage).
89c4c08d8f690c89: Returning `true` for the generic grammY "failed after N attempts" wrapper in `isSafeToRetrySendError` is unsafe because that envelope can mask ambiguous transport failures where Telegram may have already accepted the message, risking duplicate sends.
04120e85b6713e18: The `isSafeToRetrySendError` function incorrectly returns `true` for grammY's generic "failed after N attempts" error envelope, which can cause duplicate messages by retrying non-idempotent Telegram sends even when the original request may have already been delivered.
32137a30f0fb3852: Marking 429 errors as retryable is unsafe because the retry infrastructure caps delays at 30 seconds with jitter, meaning retries will fire before Telegram's flood-control window expires, repeatedly hit rate limits, and exhaust all retry attempts.
fbafcf06e20df9c7: The new `hasTelegramRetryAfterParameter` retry branch incorrectly allows retrying too early because the retry engine caps delays at 30 seconds and applies negative jitter, meaning Telegram-mandated `retry_after` windows longer than 30 seconds (or those reduced by jitter) are not fully respected, risking repeated 429 errors until all retry attempts are exhausted.
6d2b1a6627b12dd9: Adding `GRAMMY_NETWORK_REQUEST_FAILED_AFTER_RE` as an allowed retry path in `isSafeToRetrySendError` is unsafe for non-idempotent Telegram sends because grammY's "failed after N attempts" error can wrap ambiguous transport failures where the message may have already been delivered, risking duplicate user-visible messages.
2ccc52c6a9d450e6: Treating grammY "Network request failed after N attempts" errors as always safe to retry in `isSafeToRetrySendError` risks duplicate message delivery, because these envelopes can wrap ambiguous post-write transport failures where Telegram may have already processed the original send.
6f6bae69be5cc842: Returning `true` for grammY "Network request failed after N attempts" errors in `isSafeToRetrySendError` incorrectly marks ambiguous transport failures as safe to retry, risking duplicate message delivery for non-idempotent Telegram sends.
80198ccd0b44f613: The `retry_after` flag marks flood-control errors as retryable, but the retry runner caps delays at 30 seconds, causing premature retries when Telegram's required backoff meets or exceeds that cap and exhausting all retry attempts instead of honoring the server-directed wait.
52ebc055ac35a201: A duplicate `expectPreparedResult` function declaration in the Telegram test file collides with an existing one, causing a compile error that prevents the file from being typechecked or executed.
250b921e95794bc6: The new call to `isWorkspaceOnboardingCompleted` in `loadWorkspaceBootstrapFiles` references an undeclared and unimported symbol, causing a `ReferenceError` that will crash all agent bootstrap flows at runtime.
09428e7a27053481: `OPENAI_CODEX_DEFAULT_MODEL` is imported twice from the same module in `openai-codex-provider.ts`, causing a TypeScript duplicate identifier error that breaks the build.
1ec18287baea12bb: The unconditional TTY check in `modelsAuthLoginCommand` blocks the new headless device-code login flow from ever running in non-TTY environments like CI or SSH sessions, even though that flow doesn't require an interactive terminal.
6af839c7549b9667: Raw, unescaped skill names from omitted/truncated skills are being concatenated directly into the prompt via `formatTruncatedSkillNames`, creating a prompt-injection vulnerability where malicious skill names could influence model behavior even though those skills were intentionally excluded from the available skills list.
4709fd77049e877f: The new `skills.priority` config option is not factored into the cached skills snapshot version, so changing priority mid-session will have no effect until a skill file changes or the session is recreated, making the setting appear broken for ongoing sessions and cron runs.
5e332f29db44074b: The `skills list` fallback recovery path is insufficient because it only surfaces a skill's name but not its `SKILL.md` file path, leaving truncated skills permanently unusable since the model cannot provide a `<location>` to `read`.
36ec28a3c121f392: Applying `escapeXml` to skill names before embedding them in the truncation notice causes skills with special characters (e.g., `&`, `<`, `>`) to be displayed and recommended as their escaped equivalents (e.g., `&`), which then fails the exact-match lookup in `formatSkillInfo()`, making those omitted skills undiscoverable via the advertised `openclaw skills info <name>` command.
1c345544d06080d7: Because `streamedTurnCount` is incremented before `patchMattermostPost()` completes, a failure in boundary finalization causes `deliver()` to incorrectly skip the older turn instead of re-delivering it, risking silent message loss.
1b037327372ab6bd: The rewrite of `web-search.ts` hard-codes provider selection to five built-in providers and removes the call to `resolveWebSearchDefinition`/`resolveWebSearchProviderId`, breaking runtime and plugin-owned provider overrides that were previously resolved through `resolveRuntimeWebSearchProviders` in `src/web-search/runtime.ts`.
d4ea76b456c1395f: A hardcoded 5-second timeout deletes the over-limit message preview before the authoritative `deliverMattermostReplyPayload()` resend is confirmed successful, risking permanent message loss if the resend takes longer than 5 seconds or fails entirely.
3a7cc0f2fa80dadb: The `previewSendFailed` latch is never reset between assistant turns, so a single transient network error during streaming permanently disables in-place post editing for all subsequent turns in the same session.
4980d03a4209f16d: When a pre-tool-call assistant turn contains both text and media attachments, an early return in the streaming path silently drops those attachments because the only code that uploads them is bypassed once the turn is marked as already streamed.
31a290d87e9eb620: Using a positional count (`streamedTurnCount`) to skip final payloads instead of tracking the specific streamed assistant turn can cause the wrong turn to be skipped in multi-tool-call runs, dropping a reply and misaligning subsequent finalizations.
1816a09168d8eea0: The new `retry_after` branch correctly marks 429 rate-limit errors as retryable, but because the retry runner caps and jitters delays to a 30-second maximum, retries can fire before Telegram's directed backoff window expires, causing repeated 429s and potentially exhausting all retry attempts.
013cbf2fcbc512d8: An unconditional `shift()` on `pendingOrphanDeletes` during a skipped-turn media delivery can cause a later turn's successful send to delete an earlier turn's queued preview post, leaving users with no visible copy of that earlier turn's content.
65e4d8c49448743b: Because `onAssistantMessageStart` is not awaited before `onPartialReply` fires for the next turn, `streamMessageId` may still reference the previous turn's post, causing `schedulePatch()` to overwrite and finalize the wrong Mattermost message with the new turn's content.
8d1132f6e3a2d360: The Vertex Claude 4.6 models (Opus and Sonnet) are hard-coded with a 200k context window instead of their documented 1M limit, causing premature compaction and prompt rejection for users on long-context workloads.
a4b956b97e3f596a: `hasAnthropicVertexAvailableAuth()` incorrectly returns `true` in metadata-server environments (e.g., Cloud Run, App Engine) even when no project ID can be resolved, causing the `anthropic-vertex` provider to advertise itself as available and then fail at request time when constructing the Vertex client without a required project ID.
264bf04dee2a51c5: The catalog registers incorrect Vertex AI model IDs for Claude 4.5 models (missing the `@YYYYMMDD` date suffix format that Vertex requires), causing all requests that explicitly target those models or trigger the image fallback to Opus 4.5 to fail when sent to the Vertex API.
f75c8d76074dd425: The `anthropic-vertex` reference stripping applied to PDF model config resolution is not also applied in `image-tool.ts`, meaning an explicit `agents.defaults.imageModel: anthropic-vertex/...` config will still reach `runImagePrompt()` and fail at runtime with "No media-understanding provider registered for anthropic-vertex".
10222beaf42d69f0: Routing `anthropic-vertex` through `normalizeAnthropicModelId()` incorrectly maps unsupported 4.5 model aliases (e.g., `sonnet-4.5`, `opus-4.5`) to valid-looking model IDs that will silently pass parsing but fail at runtime, since the Vertex provider catalog only exposes 4.6 models.
14725d3caf7e122e: When both native Anthropic and Anthropic Vertex profiles are configured, `resolveAnthropicDefaultAuthMode()` incorrectly returns the auth mode of whichever profile appears first in `auth.order.anthropic`, causing Vertex sessions to inherit OAuth-based pruning defaults and silently lose the cache-retention and heartbeat behavior intended for API-key/ADC semantics.
df15e9e55efddfc0: The session reset capability is captured once at registry construction time and cached without `coreGatewayHandlers` in the cache key, so a registry built without `sessions.reset` support can be incorrectly reused in a later gateway context where the reset method is available, causing `api.resetSession` to permanently fail.
9a5d403809e94728: After a successful redispatch, the code stores `redirectedToRunId` but fails to complete the original run or clear its resume lock, leaving the parent entry permanently in-flight and causing downstream logic to incorrectly count it as an unfinished pending descendant.
ddcf36b5199b8c6d: When `agent.wait` retries are exhausted after a redispatch, the cleanup callback reschedules `resumeSubagentRun` without preserving `newRunId`, allowing a still-running child agent to be incorrectly classified as `resumable-fresh` and re-dispatched with a new idempotency key, causing duplicate agent executions and potentially duplicate tool side effects or user-visible replies.
bcfad588b3d83457: The redirect mapping between the new run ID and original run ID is registered too late—only after all retry attempts fail—so if the redispatched child run completes quickly, its lifecycle completion event arrives before the mapping exists, gets dropped, and leaves the parent run permanently stuck in limbo.
f0e2d3f3c7b38a53: The transcript matching logic in `rehydrateSessionStoreEntries` relies solely on a 5-minute timestamp window to identify transcripts, allowing an unrelated `.jsonl` file to be incorrectly bound to a missing child session key, and because this mapping is persisted to `sessions.json`, a wrong session association can cause replay and continuation against entirely unrelated conversation state.
cb458e2ca83f4c19: The `model.usage` event is emitted before `deliverAgentCommandResult()` resolves the actual delivery channel, causing the OTEL telemetry to incorrectly attribute the run to `openclaw.channel=unknown` even when it was successfully delivered over a real channel.
8c56cb169a983af0: When a user explicitly enters a cleartext gateway URL on port 80, the display URL is rewritten to drop the port, causing a silent round-trip parsing bug that makes the app reconnect on the wrong port (18789) instead of the intended port 80.
78d9f5db63a3fcae: When `lastCallUsage.total` is missing, the fallback incorrectly computes context size as `input + output`, dropping `cacheRead`/`cacheWrite` tokens and distorting context-window telemetry in the `model.usage` event instead of falling back to the existing prompt-token snapshot.
29032e55a642613f: The `resolveSandboxedBridgeMediaPath()` function only allowlists `params.sandbox.allowedRoots` without including the standard trusted media directories resolved by `resolveMediaToolLocalRoots()`, causing valid attachments and generated files stored under default media roots to be incorrectly rejected in sandbox mode.
bd23cdc42cefb68c: After a successful redispatch, the code stores `redirectedToRunId` but never completes the original run or registers a waiter for the new run ID, causing the parent run to remain permanently in-flight and blocking descendant-settlement and announce cleanup.
edab97061ac04d0a: A new ERNIE extension was added without a corresponding entry in `.github/labeler.yml`, meaning PRs touching the ERNIE extension won't be automatically labeled, breaking triage and reporting automation coverage for that surface.
f7c73e92bd16f23d: The `SENSITIVE_STOP_REASON_RE` regex only matches bare `stop_reason: sensitive` text and fails to handle JSON-formatted variants like `{"stop_reason":"sensitive"}`, causing safety-filter refusals to fall through to the generic error/failover path instead of returning the intended user-facing message.
17c307353b7926cc: The ERNIE provider guide's verification step incorrectly uses the channel-only `openclaw message send` CLI command, which requires channel/target options and does not invoke the ERNIE model, meaning readers following the guide will encounter a usage error or send a channel message instead of testing the provider.
03ecb5dc351fd78b: The ERNIE provider documentation shows incorrect token limits (`contextWindow: 128000`, `maxTokens: 65536`) that don't match the runtime catalog values (`119000` and `64000`), meaning users who copy the config example could trigger context or max-token failures due to overbudgeted requests.
85a5d5c33eb42a18: `allowedRoots` is unconditionally passed to `createApplyPatchTool()` regardless of whether `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is enabled, meaning setting `tools.fs.allowedRoots` alone can silently grant `apply_patch` write access to additional directories outside the intended workspace guard.
31529bad391965dd: Switching from a pre-resolved `CONFIG_DIR` constant to a lazily-called `resolveConfigDir()` causes managed skills to be resolved against the wrong working directory when `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` is a relative path and `process.chdir()` is called before skill loading, silently dropping all managed skills.
929fd4258d9708e5: The `includeAltMemoryFile` flag is evaluated only once at startup, so if `MEMORY.md` is later renamed or deleted, the fallback `memory.md` collection is never registered for the process lifetime, causing the file to stop being indexed until a restart.
77ea766c8a307894: Spreading `resolveAllProxyFallbackOptions()` after the existing proxy options inadvertently overwrites the `noProxy` loopback exemptions set by `applyGlobalProxyDispatcher()`, causing local model endpoint requests (e.g., Ollama on `127.0.0.1`) to be incorrectly routed through the proxy on every call to `ensureGlobalUndiciStreamTimeouts()` during embedded runs.
5b5fd46fd36fa5e3: Importing `bundled-web-search-registry` causes all bundled web-search plugin modules to be eagerly loaded at module initialization time, meaning `listBundledWebSearchPluginIds()` will fail at load time if any single plugin module is unavailable, breaking callers like config validation before the local `try/catch` can intercept the error.
8b5b19962e0bcd64: MiniMax Zen models (`minimax-m2.5`, `minimax-m2.5-free`) are incorrectly classified as `anthropic-messages` instead of the OpenAI-compatible `chat-completions` endpoint, causing any discovered MiniMax model to be routed to the wrong API endpoint when selected.
11eee744d87b365c: Replacing dynamic bundled web search plugin ID resolution with a hard-coded static list causes valid configs to fail validation when a statically listed plugin ID is injected into the allowlist but isn't actually available or discoverable in the current environment, resulting in spurious "plugin not found" errors.
f5f58711472ada01: Adding a re-export of `slack.js` to `channel-runtime.ts` introduces a transitive runtime dependency on `@slack/web-api` into the generic channel SDK, causing import failures in any environment where Slack-specific packages are not installed.
3d625f198a9a4875: Stale plugin metadata entries for `google-gemini-cli-auth` and `minimax-portal-auth` were manually added to the generated file without corresponding extension manifests, causing the bundled metadata to fall out of sync with its generator script and breaking the snapshot consistency test.
83ccd0acf138b253: The protected-directory guard in `resolveExcludePatterns()` only matches exact directory names and fails to block descendant globs like `**/credentials/**`, allowing the tar filter to silently exclude protected contents while leaving an empty folder in the archive, potentially making the backup unrestorable.
5e744ad525ead16f: In `resolvePromptBuildHookResult`, `sessionKey` and `agentId` are unconditionally added to the legacy hook event object even when undefined, breaking compatibility with legacy plugins that check for property presence and violating existing test assertions that expect only `{ prompt, messages }`.
2441553436576299: When re-enabling Cortex without specifying `--mode` or `--max-chars`, the updater silently overwrites previously configured values with hardcoded defaults (`technical` and `1500`) instead of preserving the existing settings.
148f9560d0d0e51a: The inline `/model` switch guard compares only cached token totals against the budget threshold without accounting for the current prompt's tokens, allowing a session near the limit to accept a mixed model-switch message and overflow on that same turn.
8321df0babda76e3: The model-switch guard resolves context window token limits using only the model ID, ignoring the target provider, causing false rejections when switching to a provider with a larger context window that shares the same model ID as a smaller-windowed provider.
6e5e17035d7e2fe3: The `runCortexPreview()` function ignores agent-level `cortex.mode` and `maxChars` settings in favor of CLI defaults, causing the preview to show a different context slice than what is actually injected into replies.
1cd729edbf8eed6e: The new Cortex shortcut handlers directly mutate `state.tab` instead of calling `state.setTab(...)`, which bypasses tab refresh logic and browser URL/session state synchronization, potentially leaving the UI on a stale route.
7d58595c80291440: The `/cortex mode show` command incorrectly resolves only the channel-scoped override instead of using `resolveAgentCortexModeStatus()`, causing it to falsely report no override even when a session-scoped override is actively in effect.
5cad05b1d4e2b51d: Running `openclaw memory cortex enable` unconditionally overwrites existing `cortex.mode` and `cortex.maxChars` settings with hardcoded defaults, silently overriding any custom configuration when the user only intends to re-enable the feature.
ff0b18f2c5afbd0d: A control-flow bug in `runConfigureWizard()` causes `openclaw configure --sections memory` to either skip Cortex/memory setup entirely or accidentally overwrite `gateway.mode` to `local` on a remote deployment, because section-scoped runs still execute the global gateway prompt and its early-return side effects before reaching the memory branch.
c267ba54f869cb2a: The new snapshot path directly indexes the session store with `mainSessionKey` instead of using the `resolveSessionStoreEntry()` helper, which handles legacy/mixed-case keys, causing `mainSessionEntry` to return undefined on older installs and losing real session-specific mode and last-capture state in the Overview Cortex summary.
a5e6371689ef6374: Scoping the `fast` command to `"text"`-only removes it from native command surfaces (Discord, Telegram, Slack), causing it to disappear from slash-command UIs on those platforms even though the handler and documentation still reference it as a native command.
f5ac2998231c2fd3: After a gateway restart, `buildGatewaySnapshot()` relies on an unpopulated in-memory cache for the latest Cortex capture history, causing the Overview card to incorrectly show Cortex as idle and lose the last-capture timestamp/reason until a new capture occurs, even though the data exists on disk.
a740d00467885836: The watchdog LaunchAgent example only forwards `PATH` and omits `OPENCLAW_PROFILE` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, causing health checks and gateway restarts to target the wrong service in non-default-profile setups.
c8bcdb561617d12c: The watchdog gates recovery on `health --json` exit status, but because that command only exits non-zero when the gateway is fully unreachable — not when channels are wedged or stale — the watchdog silently no-ops in the exact "process alive but unhealthy" scenarios it was designed to catch.
5b6ce711648e7618: A race condition between the asynchronous `initWebPushState` subscription detection and the single `reconcileWebPushState` call in `onHello` can cause existing browser push subscriptions to go unregistered on the gateway if the gateway connects before the local subscription state has finished resolving.
baf9a1652b824619: The service worker fails to exclude `/plugins/**` routes from its cache-first interception logic, causing plugin assets to potentially serve stale content across plugin updates since only `/api` and `/rpc` are currently bypassed.
3316d226ffa60b37: When no `ref` is provided, the genome reference resolution unconditionally falls back to a Voltaris V2 fixture directory that is not included in the published package, causing `openclaw genome` commands to fail in packaged environments.
d2571330cea8f774: Removing the multimodal provider validation guard allows non-Gemini providers to silently fall back to embedding image/audio files as plain label strings instead of their actual contents, causing a quiet recall-quality regression for any installation using multimodal memory without a Gemini backend.
6098980cd1e993ab: When `senderId` is missing, using `directIdParent` (a truncated session key) as the sender fallback instead of the full `directId` can cause sender-scoped policy rules to silently fail to match, potentially granting broader tool access than intended.
376ae2b3c13f130b: The genome validation commands load schema files from a `schemas/` directory at runtime, but since `schemas/` is excluded from the npm package's published file list, the commands will crash with an `ENOENT` error when run from an installed CLI rather than a source checkout.
3001cb89e59781ca: The `onPayload` hook is called in `createOllamaStreamFn` but its return value is ignored, meaning any payload replacements returned by the hook are silently discarded and the original unmodified `body` is always sent to Ollama.
f41ad68f568b875a: Adding `tempHandle.sync()` before reading file stats can cause otherwise-successful writes to fail hard on filesystems where `fsync` is unsupported or unreliable, even though the sync is unnecessary for the subsequent `dev`/`ino` identity checks that consume the returned stats.
7045ddd23b4ae7ab: The `memory_forget` function coerces a missing `reason` to an empty string instead of leaving it undefined, which can cause delete requests to fail on APIs that treat `reason` as optional-but-non-empty.
bf7a86066c976f1f: The `autoObserve` feature's `shouldCaptureMemory` function lacks prompt-injection filtering, allowing malicious instruction-like text (e.g., "remember: ignore previous instructions") to be persisted and later re-injected as durable context, creating a persistent jailbreak vector.
09cea255f6eb4465: The `canUseTool` check only validates general tool support but not whether the `image` tool is specifically available, meaning non-vision models may be incorrectly instructed to use an image tool that is absent due to missing configuration or policy filtering, leading to contradictory prompts and failing tool plans.
443bddf5c402e479: Adding "discord" to VOICE_BUBBLE_CHANNELS only sets the `audioAsVoice` flag on the payload but the Discord outbound adapter ignores that flag for routed/queued sends, causing Discord voice bubbles to silently fall back to normal attachments in all but the immediate-reply code path.
435b798fad9d214d: The config parser incorrectly rejects the valid empty array value for `retrieveMemoryTypes` because `parseMemoryTypes` returns `undefined` for `[]`, causing `safeParseMemoriaPluginConfig` to raise a validation error and fail plugin initialization even though an empty array is a legitimate "no filter" input.
8499e83800993e60: The auto-observe hook repeatedly stores the same tail messages on every `agent_end` call without checking if they were already captured in a prior run, causing duplicate memories to accumulate over long conversations and degrading retrieval quality and increasing cost.
901b8c95256c9341: The `memory-memoria` backend changes `memory_get` to only accept `memoria://` URIs, but the core system prompt still instructs the model to use file-path-style calls, creating a contract mismatch that can cause the agent to emit invalid `memory_get` requests at runtime.
8acd574d85373378: The default `coding` profile's `group:memory` allowlist only includes `memory_search` and `memory_get`, so the Memoria plugin's core remember/forget/list tools are silently blocked for all fresh installs unless operators manually override the allowlist.
0bdd5ba62e1b66f7: When a user-scope systemd action fails and falls back to the system scope, if the system-scope action also fails, the original user-scope error is thrown instead of the system-scope error, hiding the true cause of the failure from the caller.
4b9ca6e808b0ea58: The new system-scope systemd availability check incorrectly requires a zero exit code from `systemctl status`, causing OpenClaw unit operations to fail on degraded systems with unrelated failed units, even though the user-scope path already correctly accepts non-zero exit codes in this scenario.
10f84bce67574a75: Recalled memories are injected into prompts without truncation, so long persisted messages can add thousands of tokens to every subsequent prompt, inflating cost and potentially overflowing the model's context window.
66095b87096c5429: On a cache miss, the code falls back to the synchronous `resolveModelWithRegistry()` path instead of the async `resolveModelAsync()` path, meaning OpenRouter models that require `prepareDynamicModel()`/`loadOpenRouterModelCapabilities()` to populate image-support capabilities will incorrectly fail with "Model does not support images" on first use.
5d5d00b4db4cfbca: Using `Reflect.get(target, property, receiver)` in the read proxy binds getter `this` to the proxy instead of the original engine instance, causing `TypeError` crashes when accessing private class fields (e.g., `#private`) through properties like `info` after `resolveContextEngine` wraps the object.
afa244d7ffc666f7: When `agentId` is passed to `readRecentMessages()`, it is silently ignored because `resolveSessionFilePathOptions` and `resolveSessionFilePath` prioritize `storePath` over `agentId` when resolving the transcript directory, causing the function to return the wrong agent's history or an empty array instead of the target agent's conversation history.
