715a4c88ceab1ba8: The session history helper silently discards media-only turns (image/audio/file attachments with no text) because `extractTextContent()` returns an empty string for them and the `trim()` check filters them out, causing plugins to skip those turns and pull in stale context when building the recent-history window.
3e1b5e9747f9677b: Changing `@noble/ed25519` from an exact version (`3.0.0`) to a range specifier (`^3.0.0`) without regenerating `pnpm-lock.yaml` will cause `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` to fail with `ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE`, breaking CI and any clean environment setup.
7f3710a555491c02: Moving the listener registry to a process-global `Map` causes overlapping instances (e.g., during reload or in tests) to incorrectly delete each other's registrations on shutdown, since the older instance's unconditional `setActiveWebListener(..., null)` will wipe out the newer instance's entry, resuracing "No active WhatsApp Web listener" failures.
a7f90b877826402b: Blocking `GIT_SSH_COMMAND` in `blockedKeys` incorrectly strips the operator's legitimate SSH configuration from all child processes, breaking agent git workflows for SSH remotes, when the real security concern is only agent-supplied overrides rather than the inherited host environment.
a301c3ca5bf7a7c5: The `shouldInjectBedrockBearerWrapper` function only checks `authOverride` but ignores profile-based auth resolution, causing `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` to always inject an `Authorization: Bearer` header that can override valid profile-resolved credentials whenever the env var is set, rendering explicit profile selection ineffective.
1172b465d6d2cbb3: The newly added `BROWSER`, `SDKMAN_DIR`, and `NVM_DIR` environment variable restrictions are never enforced for gateway/node host executions because the runtime path only calls `isDangerousHostEnvVarName()` and never checks `isDangerousHostEnvOverrideVarName()`, rendering the security hardening ineffective for those execution paths.
dc08745e79187e5d: The `shouldInjectBedrockBearerWrapper` guard incorrectly gates on profile presence rather than the actual resolved auth path, causing Bedrock requests to be sent without the `Authorization: Bearer` header when a stale or invalid profile exists but auth falls back to `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK`, resulting in authentication failures.
787aa7f8fa60dd32: When an OAuth provider redirects back with an error parameter (e.g., `?error=access_denied`), the code ignores it and waits for a `code` that will never arrive, causing a 60-second hang before throwing a misleading "Missing authorization code" error instead of immediately surfacing the real OAuth failure.
abfe9496f4dc7db8: The refactored profile creation code drops normalization support for the `"existing-session"` driver type, causing any client that passes `driver="existing-session"` to silently fall through and create a wrong managed CDP profile instead of the intended attach-only Chrome MCP profile.
876ecbe858816f93: The `restoreStreamChunk()` function fails to handle `thinking_delta` and `thinking_end` streaming events, causing `pf_*` privacy placeholders to leak unrestored into live reasoning output shown to users instead of the original sensitive values.
59b4baf3005d605b: For existing-session profiles, the new status logic incorrectly reports null/zero/empty values for `pid`, `cdpPort`, and `cdpUrl` because `profileState.running` is never populated for Chrome MCP-tracked sessions, breaking CLI and status-based automation for all preconfigured existing-session profiles.
38b660fa9be53c8d: Any local process can gain fully authenticated operator-level access by connecting to the loopback WebSocket endpoint without providing any credentials, because the `trusted-proxy` branch unconditionally returns `ok: true` for all loopback requests before validating proxy headers or a secret token.
567a7beba391c91b: Home-relative paths (e.g. `~/...`) in `privacy.rules` are not expanded before being passed to `path.resolve()`, causing them to resolve incorrectly, `readFileSync()` to fail, and custom privacy rules to be silently dropped in favor of built-in defaults — meaning user-defined secrets go unredacted.
4d594dbaded82b0f: When no preview URL is provided, `previewImageUrl` incorrectly defaults to the video URL itself, causing LINE API rejections because video messages require the preview URL to point to an image, not a video file.
237c872c6bf00242: Discord accounts with an `agentId` but no token are incorrectly included in synthesized route bindings, causing the agent to auto-select them for outbound message delivery and ultimately throwing a runtime error when the missing token is required to create the Discord client.
2f292047ac365a1a: The tests validate a locally re-declared copy of the empty-state logic rather than importing and exercising the real production code, so regressions in the actual `chat.ts` implementation would not be caught by this test suite.
32090c87c56cbcd9: The newly added `agentId` field is populated in `describeAccount()` but never reaches the primary channel-status API because `buildChannelAccountSnapshot()` only copies `projectSafeChannelAccountSnapshotFields` and does not merge `describeAccount()` output, leaving the main status surfaces without the field despite the commit's intent.
59a846a57f611f1e: Adding `GOOGLE_CLOUD_API_KEY` to the `google-vertex` auth candidates will cause authentication to silently fail in shell-fallback environments because `loadShellEnvFallback()` does not include this key in its import list, meaning the variable is never copied from the user's login shell into the process environment.
76eacac0fc295a93: The `trackingId` field is unconditionally included in video message payloads, but LINE rejects it for group and multi-person chat targets (prefixed with `C...`/`R...`), causing sends to those destinations to fail whenever a `trackingId` is set.
8bfe3cc5c423cf6b: When `OPENCLAW_HOME` points to a legacy-named state directory, the nesting guard causes `CONFIG_PATH` to remain stale and misaligned with the path actually used for config I/O, so operations like `openclaw onboard --reset config` will delete the wrong file and leave the active config intact.
2c2b3c63c9447ca4: The documentation now advertises gcloud ADC as a valid auth path for `google-vertex`, but the underlying resolver in `model-auth.ts` still treats a falsy/empty API key as missing auth, meaning users who follow the documented ADC-only setup will hit a broken auth check at runtime.
6a11b008b82ebc10: The Dockerfile hardcodes the AVX2-optimized `bun-linux-x64.zip` build for amd64, causing illegal-instruction crashes on older CPUs that lack AVX2 support, which the previous installer script handled automatically.
c0eac4869bf3a112: When a 429 response is received during file download, the `retry_after` value from Telegram's API is lost because `fetchRemoteMedia` flattens the response into a plain string error, causing retries to fire after a generic backoff that may be shorter than Telegram requires, resulting in repeated failed attachment downloads.
033119213c55bfd4: Moving the reachability probes before `toBrowserErrorResponse` handling means that if `isHttpReachable()` or `isReachable()` throws a `BrowserProfileUnavailableError`, the error bypasses the proper JSON 409 error mapping and falls through to the generic framework error handler, causing `browser status` to lose its actionable attach-failed response.
5910fb166fe0d274: In `resolveDefaultConfigCandidates`, the nested-home guard calls `resolveStateDir(env)` without forwarding the caller-supplied `homedir`, causing tilde expansion to use the real OS home instead of the injected one, which makes the equality check fail and silently skips valid flat config candidates for non-default home directories.
b1ab62324c901ccf: The new NO_REPLY short-circuit added before delivery path selection causes `dispatchCronDelivery` to exit early without awaiting active descendants or applying fallback summaries, meaning runs where the parent is silent but subagents produce non-silent output will never deliver the descendants' results.
d0e12e46168d6fc3: The inbound chat debounce guard fails to account for active tool-stream state, allowing `loadChatHistory` to be called mid-run, which resets tool streaming state via `maybeResetToolStream` and causes in-progress tool cards to disappear until repopulated by later events.
32153ac1b515fd6f: The tolerant parsing path skips the `normalizeLegacyWebSearchConfig` step that strict validation performs, causing legacy web-search config keys to be misidentified as unrecognized, pruned from the config, and silently dropped during gateway startup.
a6fc2a6244b69c2b: When mirroring webchat messages that use `[[reply_to_current]]`, the helper only passes raw `text` to `deliverOutboundPayloads`, which cannot resolve `replyToCurrent` on its own, causing replies intended for the current external thread to be incorrectly sent as new top-level messages.
0fd4926644b145cf: When a file rename fails with ENOENT (indicating the entry was already acknowledged), the entry is skipped in the rename step but never removed from the `eligible` array, causing it to still be processed in the subsequent delivery loop and potentially re-sending already-delivered messages to users.
dfe334ed205ad459: The webchat mirror receives UI-cleaned text that has already had inline reply tags (e.g., `[[reply_to_current]]`) stripped by `resolveBufferedChatTextState`, causing threaded assistant replies from webchat sessions to fall back to plain top-level posts on the external channel instead of being correctly threaded.
786c845c90210d8a: When restarting, followup queue draining is gated on `flushAllInboundDebouncers()` returning a non-zero count, meaning sessions with pending followups enqueued via `enqueueFollowupRun()` but no active debouncers will skip `waitForFollowupQueueDrain()`, causing those queued messages to be silently discarded on reinitialization.
ae72bfbefe6f134e: A fire-and-forget `mirrorWebchatTextToTarget` call may complete after `dispatchInboundMessage` has already processed the operator's message, causing recipients in the external channel to see the bot's response before the operator message that triggered it, breaking message ordering in the bidirectional sync flow.
3dec912731729d1f: Incorrectly adding `google-gemini-cli-auth` to `LEGACY_REMOVED_PLUGIN_IDS` will cause any config validation failure for that still-active plugin to be silently swallowed as a stale-config warning instead of surfacing a real error, potentially disabling Gemini CLI authentication without any visible indication.
ced00efedef95fc1: After a timeout-triggered compaction, the code unconditionally retries the prompt even if partial replies were already streamed to the user, which can produce duplicate or conflicting messages on surfaces like Discord and Slack.
d38e217b291c64f5: The updated `parseCameraSnapPayload()` function incorrectly requires a `base64` field, causing a regression where valid URL-only camera payloads from older or plugin nodes fail with an "invalid camera.snap payload" error instead of being handled by the existing `writeUrlToFile()` path.
e11e654bd8ecec8e: The socket configuration incorrectly assigns `proxyAgents.wsAgent` to Baileys' `fetchAgent` instead of `proxyAgents.fetchAgent`, causing HTTP-based requests like media and profile fetches to use the wrong proxy agent and potentially fail in proxied WhatsApp setups.
fc0bd0ef95626dc9: When timeout-triggered compaction fails (returns `compacted: false`), the code skips the existing oversized tool-result truncation recovery logic, meaning sessions dominated by large tool results may still spiral into repeated timeouts or unnecessary rotations even though truncation could have resolved the issue.
9e50ea78c3e49472: When a timed-out attempt has already committed external side effects (e.g., sending a message or adding a cron job), unconditionally retrying via `continue` can replay those side effects and cause duplicate actions such as resent messages or duplicate scheduled jobs.
fb37b64e0eabf5b9: In the timeout-triggered compaction path, `after_compaction` hooks are fired before `runPostCompactionSideEffects()` completes, meaning hooks may observe stale pre-compaction state or report success even if subsequent side effects fail, unlike the normal compaction flow where side effects are guaranteed to complete first.
ccce5c06aaf78c57: Using the stale `initialAuthProfileOverrideSource` value (captured before potential `clearSessionAuthProfileOverride` calls) to set `preserveUserPin` can incorrectly re-apply a `"user"` source when persisting `finalAuthProfileId`, effectively recreating a cleared user pin and causing subsequent turns to skip auto-rotation.
6c2b602f75ebf97f: Awaiting the A2A hook chain without a timeout means a single hanging plugin handler can indefinitely block the announce phase, causing users to never receive the final A2A conclusion.
c0c3476d21857c3a: The lockfile incorrectly drops `@lancedb/lancedb-linux-x64-gnu` from LanceDB's optional dependencies, which will cause `import("@lancedb/lancedb")` to fail at runtime on Linux x64 glibc systems due to the missing native binding.
f827dd8ef62fa57b: Returning `false` from `validateLink` causes markdown-it to skip token emission for dangerous URLs (e.g., `javascript:`, `file:`), bypassing the custom image/link flattener and rendering rejected URLs as raw markdown source instead of sanitized label text.
f0f6e73ebeae91f0: Setting `html: false` in markdown-it disables raw HTML token handling, so pasted HTML snippets bypass the custom escape logic and may be incorrectly linkified or markdown-formatted instead of displayed as literal text, regressing the raw-HTML display fix from #13937.
4bf680f21933573a: The custom `www.` autolink matcher uses an ASCII-only character class (`[a-z0-9_-]`), causing a regression where internationalized domain names (e.g., `www.münchen.de`) are no longer converted to links, unlike the previous marked-based renderer.
63fac989adeb052c: Overriding markdown-it's `validateLink()` to allow all non-`data:` URLs bypasses its built-in scheme blocklist (e.g., `javascript:`, `file:`), causing unsafe or unresolvable links to become inert but visually misleading anchors in the UI after DOMPurify strips the href.
6c1fc74153221951: The schema cap on `limit` was lowered to 1000, but the underlying snapshot backends still support up to 2000 nodes, causing valid large-page snapshot requests to fail schema validation before they ever reach the implementation.
28928c2b8dee0526: The new `list-style: none` CSS rules for task list items are scoped only to `.chat-text` and `.sidebar-markdown`, omitting the reasoning block context, so task list items rendered inside reasoning blocks will incorrectly display both a browser bullet marker and a checkbox simultaneously.
15fe1b376682f834: The custom `www` autolink rule uses an overly broad boundary check that linkifies text like `"www.example.com"` or `[www.example.com]` in quoted or bracketed contexts, violating GFM autolink extension boundaries and producing false-positive links in logs and JSON payloads.
53fae6682115380f: The newly added `maximum: 50` constraint on the `limit` parameter in `SessionsListToolSchema` conflicts with existing callers that pass `limit: 200`, potentially preventing the tool from enumerating enough sessions to locate older or less-active targets in larger deployments.
fce360571f84182f: When a user re-enters the chat tab, stale `chatAutoScrollBlockId`/`chatAutoScrollMode` state is not cleared alongside `chatHasAutoScrolled`, causing the forced scroll to reuse the old "clamp" mode and land at the block start instead of the true bottom of the latest output.
3242463913b4ff46: Adding a `maximum: 3600` constraint to `runTimeoutSeconds` in the schema contradicts the runtime's documented support for arbitrary non-negative values (where `0` means no timeout), causing legitimate long-running subagent jobs exceeding one hour to fail schema validation and become impossible to spawn.
cee491f94f199987: The `/set_topic_name` command fails to include `MessageThreadId` when composing session labels, causing two DM topics in the same chat with the same topic name to generate identical labels, which results in a `label already in use` error or ambiguous label resolution on the second `sessions.patch` call.
84a41cf9dce91be5: The `dangerous-exec-python` regex only matches `os.system`/`os.popen`/`os.exec*` calls with literal string arguments, allowing malicious code to bypass this critical security check by passing the payload through a variable instead.
f977edee0eaa4021: The `resetChatScroll()` call triggers unconditionally on any `chatStream` transition from `null` to a string, including queued sends and external delta events, which causes users who have intentionally scrolled up to be unexpectedly yanked back to the bottom when a new reply starts.
f27751f8ec3800d3: The retry callback closes over the original `effectiveForce` value instead of re-evaluating follow state, so a forced scroll can override a manual upward scroll made within the ~150ms retry window and snap the viewport back unexpectedly.
aed8cbb6dbaa3625: Adding `.sh` and `.bash` extensions to the scannable set is insufficient because extensionless shell scripts with shebangs (e.g., `bin/install`, `postinstall`), common in npm packages, are still silently skipped by the extension-based `isScannable()` check, leaving a bypass for malicious code execution.
9c440d9077eb1b83: When `resolveServerChatModelValue` is called with a model ID that already contains a provider prefix (e.g., `openrouter/aurora-alpha`) alongside a matching `modelProvider`, it incorrectly double-prefixes the ID into `openrouter/openrouter/aurora-alpha`, causing the reported and cached model reference to not match the actual session model.
f853837098233a7a: The early return for slash-containing model IDs in `buildQualifiedChatModelValue` strips the provider prefix, causing models like `meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct` to be sent without their provider namespace (e.g., `nvidia/meta/...`), which can route requests to the wrong provider or conflate distinct models with the same slash-containing ID.
3fdc1ef88c561659: The `bluebubbles` platform is missing from the `VALID_PLATFORMS` allowlist in `extractPlatformFromSessionKey`, causing BlueBubbles sessions to fall back to unprefixed sender IDs that can collide with other channels and leak memory across users.
701084b32cd5d25d: Resolving the default timezone on the CLI client side instead of the remote gateway causes cron jobs to be silently scheduled using the operator's local timezone rather than the gateway's timezone, leading to incorrect job timing in remote deployments.
3fa2bf4dd57a52b2: The automatic rollback logic only triggers when the config file is marked invalid at snapshot time, but it fails to handle a later startup failure caused by unresolved required secrets in `prepareGatewayStartupConfig()`, leaving the process to exit without attempting a rollback to a previously working config.
41b2b3d3352572ca: Changing `KillMode` in `buildSystemdUnit()` only affects newly written unit files, so existing Linux users will retain the old `KillMode=control-group` setting on disk until they manually reinstall the service, meaning the targeted bug will persist through a normal upgrade and restart.
546fd83c6cbe6b07: The newly added `(no tz)` warning suffix in `formatSchedule()` can be silently truncated away by `printCronList()`'s 32-character `CRON_SCHEDULE_PAD` limit on longer cron expressions, defeating the purpose of surfacing implicit-timezone schedules to users.
7029fee4c718cdbf: The `!overrides.<provider>.model` guard prevents a later `model=` directive from overriding an earlier `*_model` directive set in a previous block, breaking the expected last-directive-wins behavior and silently ignoring later user-specified model overrides.
5d2af78b18be6774: The fallback to `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone` for auto-detecting the cron timezone can return `undefined` in certain Node.js environments, silently creating the same implicit-timezone job the change intended to prevent, so a hardcoded fallback (e.g., `"UTC"`) is needed before treating the timezone as resolved.
b50d5c242f0f0432: The new conjunction matcher uses `.*?` which doesn't cross newlines, causing `hasUnbackedReminderCommitment()` to miss multi-line reminder promises and silently skip the unscheduled reminder warning that users rely on.
3b9c85034e5e9c25: `resolveKnownContextThresholds` ignores the fallback provider from `props.sessions?.defaults`, so sessions with unqualified model IDs and no explicit `session.modelProvider` silently fail to match provider-scoped entries in `KNOWN_CONTEXT_THRESHOLDS`, causing pricing-threshold hints and model-ceiling metadata to disappear.
1e0051f83ffe153e: The guard in `activatePluginRegistry` that prevents overwriting the active registry also incorrectly blocks legitimate runtime workspace switches, causing subagent runs to retain a stale registry and leading to missing channel tools and "Unknown channel" failures for plugin channels belonging to the new workspace.
6d13eeab628a9bc8: The `memory_refresh` tool truncates memory IDs to 8 characters in search results, but the subsequent replace call requires a full UUID, causing the documented two-step refresh workflow to fail when callers copy the displayed ID into `memoryId`.
7197fe2e91dfcb9c: The thinking-to-text conversion always prepends the synthesized thinking block to the front of the message, corrupting the original text block order and causing downstream providers to reconstruct history with a different context sequence than what the model originally produced.
584920762caafff3: Although the timeout budget is now correctly passed to `resolveProbeBudgetMs`, `probeGateway()` constructs `GatewayClient` without forwarding `requestTimeoutMs`, so internal RPCs still default to 30,000ms and will ignore any user-specified timeout greater than that.
2183c68208c14b56: Using `|| true` to suppress the exit status of `vagrant up` causes `cmd_start` to silently swallow failures and falsely report success, leaving users unaware that the VM never started.
7df3130ccfbeebff: A guard that prevents overwriting the active plugin registry allows a stale registry to persist after plugin configuration changes, causing `config.schema` to silently omit channel entries for newly configured plugins until a restart.
5d0146fd8ce67f98: The ClawAPI provider catalog imports internal helpers directly from `../../src/...` instead of the approved `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-models` boundary, causing a module-not-found error at runtime in packaged installs where the `src` tree is absent and preventing ClawAPI from initializing.
d3aa8d187ccb031f: The installer script hardcodes a different repository (DaevMithran/openclaw-vm) as its clone target, meaning any fixes or updates merged into the current repository (openclaw/openclaw) will never reach end users who follow the documented curl-based install path.
db9699d722860e97: The variable `vagrant_status` is never initialized on the first-create path, causing the script to immediately crash with an "unbound variable" error under `set -u` right after a successful initial provision when `[[ -n "$vagrant_status" ]]` is evaluated.
03820511b412a26f: Sourcing the `.env` file containing a user-supplied `VM_PASSWORD` in `cmd_status` allows arbitrary shell code execution if the password contains command substitution syntax like `$(...)` or backticks, and should be parsed safely instead of evaluated.
c552187d8d7dffd7: The draft stream is not sealed before returning from preview finalization, allowing stale partial callbacks that arrive asynchronously after dispatch to overwrite the already-finalized preview content when the stream is later stopped.
97d899b648631a53: The `ScanControllers` method is missing from `ALLOWED_BRIDGE_METHODS`, causing `invokeBridgeSequence("ScanControllers", ...)` to always be rejected, which prevents virtual controller detection and allows a "real" connection to silently proceed against a virtual target.
b73e253a1d56e319: The `abb_robot_virtual` plugin sends `set_joints`/`movj` commands over a WebSocket connection, but the bridge implementation does not relay inbound frames between sessions, so these commands never reach the browser viewer and the virtual plugin cannot drive the 3D viewer at all.
f53b17a06317676d: The `movj` tool hard-codes a 12-second reply timeout that doesn't account for actual motion duration, causing timeout errors for valid slow or large-angle joint movements that the viewer correctly executes over longer periods.
c7defa31cd0efb90: The `wsConnect` function always hardcodes `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` as the WebSocket connection target, ignoring the `host` value stored in `state.host`, which will cause connection failures in any deployment where the bridge is not running on loopback.
f7060fb1822e455b: The mention-fallback sanitization only strips single-backtick code spans, so bot mention tokens inside multi-backtick code blocks (e.g., ` ```<@botId>``` `) survive into `textForMentionCheck` and incorrectly trigger a bot response when `mentionedUsers` is empty.
d8d26a6cae0d1577: The fallback detection compares against a `provider`/`model` that may have already been overwritten to the fallback values on a prior retry, causing `isFromFallback` to incorrectly return `false` on subsequent turns and allowing the fallback model to be incorrectly persisted as the sticky runtime model.
b4cff313aa88d9f0: The guide instructs users to access WebChat at `http://localhost:18789` on the host, but because the gateway defaults to `loopback` binding, VirtualBox/Vagrant port forwarding won't reach the guest's `127.0.0.1`, meaning WebChat will silently fail until the docs also instruct users to switch to a `lan`/`custom` bind or set up an SSH tunnel.
bd9accee3ae74f2e: The `isFromFallback` flag is never cleared by some runtime-model writers, causing the new guard to silently skip valid model assignments in those code paths and fall back to incorrect defaults for all subsequent requests in that session.
237a60d3dffeae5b: The deduplication key in `buildDirectOutboundDedupeKey` omits session context fields (e.g. `agentId`, session `key`), causing legitimate outbound sends from different sessions with the same target and payload to be incorrectly collapsed within the 1.5s window, which can silently drop sends and misattribute or skip internal hooks.
d4c588a3862738cf: The regex used to strip leaked function-call preludes is too narrow, only matching hardcoded patterns like `callrecipient{...}`, so variations with arbitrary function identifiers or extra spaces will slip through and get incorrectly persisted as assistant text on tool-use turns.
b20c029ba1c49d60: When `applyGigachatConfig()` is called without a `baseUrl` during re-authentication, it unconditionally overwrites any existing custom GigaChat endpoint with the default `GIGACHAT_BASE_URL`, silently redirecting future requests to the wrong server.
a5056a3cd2e00174: The `extractRelevantSnippet` function uses a naive whitespace-split tokenizer that misaligns with the actual search tokenizer, causing snippet anchoring to point at wrong locations for conversational queries and silently failing for short or non-Latin search terms.
41b2305b749b4297: When a user selects the OAuth flow but provides Basic credentials (e.g., "user:pass"), the interactive GigaChat path stores them without validation under `authMode: "oauth"`, causing all GigaChat requests to fail instead of falling back to Basic auth as the non-interactive path correctly handles.
c434ce37ae33ac46: When switching from GigaChat Basic to OAuth onboarding, the old Basic endpoint URL is retained in the provider config instead of being reset to the correct OAuth host, causing all subsequent OAuth requests to fail until the user manually edits their configuration.
9cf86241d8bc1991: Deriving the ZK handoff capsule key from the same gateway RPC token used by all authenticated clients allows any client to forge valid capability proofs for arbitrary agent identities, completely breaking the handoff trust boundary.
2e12659760b73a71: When a user previously configured GigaChat using Basic auth via `models.providers.gigachat.apiKey`/`baseUrl` without a `gigachat:*` auth profile, the OAuth onboarding flow fails to reset the old custom Basic URL, causing subsequent OAuth requests to be incorrectly routed to the Basic endpoint until the user manually edits `models.providers.gigachat.baseUrl`.
5d2e5d611ce31594: In `AccessWitnessRecorder.generateProof()`, the agent is given the `gatewayKey` to sign `gatewayMac`, which collapses the trust boundary between prover and verifier and allows any compromised agent to forge gateway-signed proofs that `verifyAccessProof()` will accept as legitimate.
219c474624117252: Bumping the Kotlin compiler plugin versions to 2.3.20 without also upgrading the AGP-bundled Kotlin/KGP (via a `buildscript` override) causes a version skew that can break Android sync and compilation.
7db209bef74df315: The `resetGigachatBaseUrl` check is hardcoded to only inspect the `gigachat:default` profile, so if an active Basic-auth profile with a custom host is configured via `auth.order` or another profile ID, switching to OAuth during onboarding will incorrectly preserve the stale Basic endpoint, causing all subsequent OAuth requests to be routed to the wrong host.
ea36aff9cda44519: The fallback logic in `applyGigachatNonInteractiveApiKeyChoice()` incorrectly blocks all stored auth profiles, not just legacy Basic auth ones, causing valid stored OAuth credentials to be ignored and forcing users to redundantly re-supply their API key on repeat runs.
996076ea184cd990: The new DeepInfra extension is missing a corresponding entry in `.github/labeler.yml`, which means future pull requests touching DeepInfra files will not receive the expected auto-applied triage label, breaking the maintainer routing workflow.
84b8f8d735381a4c: When the DeepInfra model discovery endpoint fails, the fallback to `buildStaticCatalog()` persists zero-cost pricing entries to `models.json`, causing `resolveModelCostConfig()` to incorrectly report $0 costs for affected model routes until a successful fetch overwrites the stale data.
666da6c8fb7d516b: Adding the "DeepInfra" provider link without a corresponding entry in `docs/.i18n/glossary.zh-CN.json` will cause the `pnpm check:docs` CI step to fail due to missing i18n glossary coverage.
a24f8ff85b6d1259: The fallback static catalog incorrectly marks the DeepInfra GPT-OSS 120B model as image-capable, allowing image inputs to bypass local validation gates and reach the provider only to fail, inconsistent with how other catalogs treat the same text-only model.
c61900d5d712c1c5: The DeepInfra wrapper incorrectly injects `reasoning.effort` for non-thinking models (e.g., `Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct-Turbo`) because the reasoning suppression logic only targets `x-ai/*` models, potentially causing requests to be rejected or ignored by the API.
008e5e5f98be5ae3: The new DeepInfra provider was not added to the hard-coded `envProbeProviders` array, causing `openclaw models list --status` to incorrectly show DeepInfra as unauthenticated even when a valid `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` environment variable is set.
7539bc6b49bd37bc: The `extensions/deepinfra/index.ts` file imports `definePluginEntry` from `"openclaw/plugin-sdk/core"`, which is explicitly banned by the repo's guardrail test suite and will cause the Vitest run to fail before any DeepInfra functionality is tested.
612846bf4ed39a4b: Removing the `room:` prefix from conversation ID resolution breaks ACP reset routing for Matrix inbound contexts, causing `/new` and `/reset` commands in ACP-bound Matrix rooms to incorrectly reset the regular session instead of the bound ACP runtime.
0380793a4337aee8: The session-key error fallback is disabled too early—immediately upon gateway acknowledgment rather than after the relay receives a lifecycle start event—so if the ACP runtime fails on its first lifecycle event, the parent relay can still hang because the real child run ID hasn't been learned yet.
1047897f857f1cae: When `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is set above 10 seconds, client-side RPC commands will still time out prematurely because `callGateway()` and multiple CLI entry points hardcode a 10-second transport timeout that isn't updated to match the new server-side handshake budget.
ba3e18f78b661229: The `discoverDeepInfraModels()` function makes unauthenticated requests to the DeepInfra model catalog endpoint, so private or account-scoped models are never discovered even though the provider already resolves a valid API key that could be forwarded.
8965923b2cfd49e2: Adding "baidu" to the allowlist-compat IDs without a corresponding entry in `BUNDLED_WEB_SEARCH_PROVIDER_REGISTRY` means `resolvePluginWebSearchProviders()` never resolves Baidu, causing Baidu-configured setups to silently fall back to other providers and trigger missing-key failures at runtime.
538f8edbc13af912: In `mode: "session"` spawns, the child relay captures the first `lifecycle start` matching `childSessionKey` regardless of `runId`, so a concurrent follow-up turn in the same child thread can hijack `observedChildRunId` and cause the parent to display the wrong output and completion while the original spawn is still pending.
7d47d8d3f51d918a: The Baidu plugin's version (2026.3.16) is mismatched with the root package version (2026.3.14), which will cause the release validation script to fail and block the release process.
c0563592c2b8095a: The hard-coded thinking level labels in `thinking-utils.ts` are not gated by provider or model, so users are incorrectly shown `xhigh` as a valid option on models that reject it and shown unsupported granular levels on Z.AI models that only support a binary off/on toggle.
d61899d5cb96789e: The `looksLikeShortProfileId` regex only permits lowercase alphanumerics and hyphens, causing Bedrock Application Inference Profile IDs containing valid characters like `.` or `:` to be misclassified as non-Anthropic models, which incorrectly disables prompt caching.
df9ef83bd573f554: The code review replaces the generic `normalizeBaseUrl` call with a Gemini-specific `normalizeGeminiBaseUrl` function, which could indicate that the old function was incorrectly normalizing the base URL for Gemini API endpoints, potentially causing malformed requests.
a84ec600d79d8c63: The `looksLikeShortProfileId` function rejects alphanumeric-only validation, causing Bedrock Application Inference Profile IDs containing hyphens (e.g., `my-claude-profile`) to bypass the Claude-name fallback and be incorrectly treated as non-Anthropic models, leaving prompt caching disabled for those profiles.
7160f595c7197f3f: Fatal gateway errors (e.g., 4014 disallowed intents) emitted during the startup readiness wait window are captured by the early guard but never re-drained before `waitForDiscordGatewayStop` registers its error handler, causing the error to be silently swallowed and reported as a generic timeout or reconnect failure instead of an actionable message.
e7e2e06637089df5: Setting `activateGlobalHookRunner: false` in the plugin CLI registration prevents the active plugin registry from being published, causing the pairing subcli to receive an empty channel list and incorrectly reject valid channels like `discord` as unsupported.
bc18319b5773ab5e: A timeout created for each lifecycle-first `agent.wait` call is never cancelled when the `dedupePromise` resolves first, causing orphaned timers to accumulate under concurrent waits and leading to unnecessary memory and callback churn.
961d0b53a0c7515b: When `activateGlobalHookRunner` is false, plugin loading still mutates global command and interactive registries because `shouldActivate` remains true, causing command routing to fall out of sync with the active registry and hook runner on cache misses.
39d101151e67283b: `waitForIdle()` only awaits a one-time snapshot of `this.tails`, so any tasks enqueued after that snapshot is taken can be silently skipped, causing the method to falsely report idle and allowing teardown/cleanup to proceed while jobs are still pending.
364ccbdbf292f9a7: The injected `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY` is added to the merged `env` but is not forwarded through the node-host code path, causing skill scripts to silently fail to discover the session key during `host=node` executions.
062cf5e568a33061: The new `enqueue` and `abort` proxy wrappers added to `resolvePluginRuntime` expose methods that don't exist on the underlying gateway subagent runtime, meaning any plugin that calls them will crash with a `TypeError` at runtime.
ec380f15fb2ce294: The Azure-specific CLI options (API key, base URL, model ID) are not forwarded when `applyAuthChoice` is called during interactive onboarding in `setup.ts`, causing them to be silently ignored and re-prompted for, making these flags only functional in `--non-interactive` mode.
c16fa5e230fa5c05: When `sessions.usage.timeZone` is unavailable on older gateways, the catch block redundantly fires a second `usage.cost` RPC instead of reusing the already-successful result from the initial `Promise.all`, causing the expensive cost scan to run twice on every fallback to `utcOffset`.
7b72d643932ec24f: When a contact name search yields no matching phone numbers, the code fails to short-circuit and instead queries all SMS messages without an address filter, leaking unrelated SMS history to the caller.
1b273f1ed5a6b703: The fallback authorization logic incorrectly requires both `provider` and `model` fields together, causing trusted fallback plugins that pass only `model` (a valid partial override) to hard-fail before dispatch, breaking previously supported behavior that allows independent `provider` or `model` overrides.
484c83378135df94: The new `sms.search` command is registered and advertised when READ_SMS permission is granted, but it lacks a corresponding entry in the live-test command profiles, causing live Android-node capability tests to abort with an "unmapped advertised commands: sms.search" error on devices with that permission.
91f21b068c084f99: The `controlUrl` guard only catches Headscale incompatibility when explicitly configured in the gateway config, but fails to detect cases where the local Tailscale daemon is already connected to a custom control server, allowing `mode=serve|funnel` to pass validation and produce an opaque runtime failure instead of the intended fast-fail error.
cff268b2734b5aeb: The `validateGatewayTailscaleControlUrl` function incorrectly rejects the official Tailscale control URL (e.g., `https://controlplane.tailscale.com`) in serve/funnel mode by treating any non-empty `controlUrl` as custom infrastructure, breaking configs that explicitly set the default and contradicting the existing `isOfficialTailscaleControlServer()` helper in `server-runtime-config.ts`.
2c7f688d50c96a6e: The cron path correctly skips persisting the fallback runtime model/provider, but still unconditionally writes the fallback `cliSessionId`, which can pin future cron turns to the fallback CLI session and prevent the primary model from being retried.
bf36616852c639fe: The fallback guard only skips `setSessionRuntimeModel` but still persists the `cliSessionId`, so CLI providers will reuse the fallback session on subsequent requests, defeating the intent to retry the primary model after recovery.
304a922a38d8aa3f: The return type was updated to include `"sandbox"` as a valid host, but the underlying parsing logic was not updated to recognize it, causing sandbox approval-pending results to be silently dropped and embedded channels to lose structured approval prompt metadata.
f888e28408563983: The `isFromFallback` check incorrectly suppresses `setSessionRuntimeModel` even when hook-driven model overrides (not true fallbacks) occurred, causing the session's persisted runtime model to drift from what actually executed.
e979cefbac083100: When a superseded in-flight preview send resolves after `forceNewMessage()` has advanced the generation, the snapshot fields `lastSentText`/`lastSentParseMode` are unconditionally overwritten with stale data, causing downstream deduplication and materialization logic to operate on the wrong generation's content.
7a2bea85c17e7e9b: Computing `isFromFallback` by comparing requested model/provider values instead of the actually-executed model from `result.meta.agentMeta` can incorrectly pass `false` in CLI resume sessions, bypassing the safeguard in `updateSessionStoreAfterAgentRun` and permanently pinning the session to a fallback model instead of the configured primary.
0b4890899718b283: The audio branch condition only matches the exact string `audio/ogg` but the outbound flow rewrites the MIME type to `audio/ogg; codecs=opus`, causing voice note messages to fall through to the document payload branch and be rendered as file attachments instead of voice notes.
4d637c05de6ae556: The new `gateway.tailscale.controlUrl` config field is missing corresponding `FIELD_LABELS` and `FIELD_HELP` entries, so it will appear in the settings UI without a label or help text, making it undiscoverable to users.
8a04a80fde29af21: The superseded-preview callback archives the original rendered HTML text instead of the effective text actually sent to Telegram, causing stale snapshots to corrupt regressive-update checks and finalization logic in the `forceNewMessage()` race path.
5b53f4cad6405d4f: The Telegram extension directly imports from `src/infra/errors.js` in violation of the extension boundary rule, tightly coupling the extension to a core internal file path that could break resolution during packaging or refactoring.
b2eacedac45e567b: The max-length check in `sendOrEditStreamMessage` incorrectly uses `renderedText` instead of the effective plain-text fallback (`effectiveText`), which can cause valid messages to be falsely flagged as exceeding 4096 characters after HTML parse-mode degradation, incorrectly halting the stream and delivering a truncated or stale final message.
8124af7568dd984f: Because `normalizeLanguageCode` only accepts 2-letter ISO-639-1 codes, xAI-valid language values like `auto` or `pt-BR` throw an error before they can populate `overrides.xai.language`, causing most xAI language overrides to be silently dropped before synthesis begins.
488fca90621b2668: The fallback to `launchctl load -w` incorrectly treats an exit code of 0 as success without inspecting stderr/stdout, which can cause a false success report even when `launchctl` printed a real failure message, since the legacy `load` command is known to return exit code 0 even on meaningful errors.
c308f561eb6b4e30: The replace-mode provider filter fails to map `volcengine-plan` and `byteplus-plan` variant IDs to their base providers, causing those catalog entries to be incorrectly excluded and potentially hiding the active default model from the dashboard's model selection dropdown.
32c0ea74f2860329: When `GITHUB_TOKEN` is set, the unquoted `$GITHUB_AUTH` variable expands into multiple shell tokens, causing `curl` to receive the `-H` flag, the header name, and the token value as separate arguments rather than a single argument, which breaks authenticated GitHub API requests.
731e341a48981acf: The contract test derives both its expected values and actual outputs from the same `resolvePluginProviders` mock, meaning if providers are silently dropped by that resolver, both sides shrink equally and the test falsely passes, defeating its purpose of catching wizard coverage regressions.
18f7e7356b4222b6: When a Slack approval times out, forcing `decision: "deny"` locally clears the pending state prematurely, so if a real approval event arrives shortly after, it is silently ignored and Slack permanently displays "Denied" even though the gateway may have accepted an allow decision.
282100b1521cd166: The non-interactive onboarding command example in the Fireworks setup documentation is missing the required `--accept-risk` flag, which will cause the command to immediately fail when users copy and run it.
bfd054ffee9b790f: The new `execApprovals` field is added to the TypeScript type for Slack config but is missing from the Zod runtime validation schema, causing it to be silently rejected as an unrecognized key during config parsing and making the feature impossible to enable.
1c89afa6f55f2521: `closeHttpServer` calls `closeAllConnections()` before `server.close()`, creating a race window where new connections can still be accepted, potentially preventing graceful shutdown and causing the 5-second timeout to trigger while the port remains bound.
9995ebc0017870b7: The orphan short-reply reattachment logic runs unconditionally for any missing session key, which can silently override an explicit user session switch by reassigning `sessionKey` to a wrong candidate session when the first message is a short token like "yes" or "ok".
dd74c629a9c559fc: The macOS normalization path drops the newly added `args` and `matchMode` fields when rebuilding `ExecAllowlistEntry`, causing "always allow" entries with argument restrictions to be silently downgraded to path-only approvals after a restart or reload cycle.
a873b73ebf233445: A missing `command` argument in `ExecApprovalsGatewayPrompter.fallbackDecision` causes the new exact-match args comparison to always fail, silently denying previously allowlisted commands in headless or inactive sessions where prompts cannot be shown.
6f8fddcfe510b592: When a command is invoked with no arguments, mapping it to `args: null` instead of `args: []` causes `addAllowlistEntry` to skip setting `matchMode: "exact"`, creating a path-only allowlist rule that incorrectly permits any future invocation of that command—including malicious ones with arguments—without prompting.
d59df536526ed18d: The per-agent `contextTokens` override is only applied when it is lower than the base token count, so agents cannot use their configuration to raise or disable a stricter global default, leaving them artificially capped despite having a higher override value.
ee47caa3a9e579c3: The documentation for multi-agent orchestration patterns is incomplete because it only instructs users to enable `tools.agentToAgent` without also requiring `tools.sessions.visibility` to be set to `"all"`, which is a prerequisite checked first in `sessions-access.ts` for cross-agent session calls to succeed.
094a5242abde6623: An unguarded call to `inspectBundleMcpRuntimeSupport()` during plugin loading can throw on malformed bundle MCP config (e.g. broken `.mcp.json`), causing the entire `loadOpenClawPlugins()` function to abort instead of gracefully downgrading the error to a plugin diagnostic.
4a4f25997d095f1e: The SKILL.md documentation incorrectly claims a newly installed skill is available "immediately — no restart needed," when in reality the CLI agent snapshots skills only at session start, meaning users in an active session must restart before the new skill is usable.
f6c7110f48b40bf1: The bundle MCP runtime is created before lock acquisition but only disposed in an inner `finally` block, meaning spawned MCP subprocesses will leak if `acquireSessionWriteLock` fails or times out.
22c805a4dadf6ef3: Passing `onlyPluginIds` to the second `loadGatewayPlugins()` call triggers a full global reload in `loadOpenClawPlugins()`, wiping out all non-deferred plugins' hooks, commands, and providers from the registry for the remainder of runtime.
239c5f284bfff687: The new regex pattern incorrectly matches Telegram's "bot was blocked by the user" 403 error, causing it to be rewritten as a misleading "bot is not a member of the chat" error message and sending operators down the wrong troubleshooting path.
9f3f545128639112: The build-unsigned.yml workflow skips required toolchain setup steps (such as swiftformat, swiftlint, and xcodegen) that are present in ci.yml, causing the job to fail on a clean macos-latest runner with missing-command errors before an IPA can be produced.
b49123d68cc50e05: Excluding `plugins.installs` from the manifest-registry cache key means that within the 1-second TTL, installs or removals of global plugins that collide with bundled plugin IDs won't be reflected, causing the duplicate-suppression logic to use a stale winner/loser set and potentially load the wrong plugin version.
4e4d4bcf2c5fcdcd: When using the Google embeddings provider, the configured `embedding.dimensions` override is ignored by `GoogleEmbeddings` but still used to size the LanceDB table, causing vector-size mismatch errors at runtime during `memory_store`/`memory_recall` operations.
ce4e54bd01e365bf: The workflow uploads an IPA artifact intended for direct sideloading, but uses an `app-store` export method via `fastlane beta_archive`, which produces an IPA packaged for App Store/TestFlight distribution that cannot be directly installed by end users.
6704acd930cb85c7: The new "ClawPool" plugin entry is inserted after "WeChat" in the community plugin list, violating the repository's documented rule requiring alphabetical ordering for docs lists, which makes the list harder to maintain and inconsistent with established standards.
e5918734763f6528: In the intentional-override path for installed global vs. bundled duplicate plugins, using `continue` silently drops the bundled candidate from `manifestRegistry.plugins` entirely rather than just suppressing the warning, causing downstream plugin-status lookups in `loadOpenClawPlugins` to incorrectly omit the shadowed bundled plugin as an overridden/disabled entry.
fff11d2030f88dcd: When deriving symlink names in the composite workspace, using raw workspace basenames without reserving internal names like "memory" can cause collisions with OpenClaw's reserved paths, leading the memory backend to incorrectly index linked repositories as memory data and blocking normal memory-folder behavior.
76ce7cabd6a5a3a9: `ensureMemoryFlushTarget` unconditionally attempts to open and write the daily memory file on startup regardless of whether memory flush is enabled, which can cause `EACCES`/`EROFS` errors and abort valid runs on read-only workspaces even when the memory flush feature is disabled.
926dbea48e28d779: The `formatReasoningStatus` switch statement has no default/fallback case, so any unrecognized `reasoningLevel` value stored as an unconstrained string in session data will return `undefined` and render as "Reasoning: undefined" in the status output.
47c3edd97ac1e447: In `ensureCompositeWorkspace`, collision detection uses case-sensitive basename comparisons, so on case-insensitive filesystems (macOS/Windows) names like `Repo` and `repo` are treated as distinct during planning but resolve to the same on-disk symlink, causing one workspace to be silently dropped and allowing case-variant reserved names to bypass internal-name guards.
91362a39b49d9853: Removing the reverse-lookup branch causes already-resolved API key values (e.g., the plaintext value of `OPENAI_API_KEY`) to be written directly into `models.json` instead of being normalized back to their environment variable references, reintroducing the plaintext secret persistence bug fixed in #38757.
ce6730ef32dd9da2: Removing the `MOONSHOT_NATIVE_BASE_URLS` and `MODELSTUDIO_NATIVE_BASE_URLS` constants causes a `ReferenceError` at runtime (and a type-check failure) because `applyNativeStreamingUsageCompat` still references them later in the file, breaking provider normalization for Moonshot and Model Studio configurations.
c696e30387c53b96: Pre-reserving every unique basename causes all workspace symlinks to incorrectly trigger the collision-deduplication branch, renaming non-colliding entries like `/repos/api` to `<parent>-api` instead of `api` and producing unstable, unpredictable composite link names.
2039064de8f40132: The `formatReasoningStatus` function's fallthrough default silently maps any unknown or legacy `reasoningLevel` value (e.g. `"low"`) to `"stream"`, causing `/status` to incorrectly report active streaming reasoning instead of surfacing or normalizing the unrecognized value.
084c3322b7555886: Thinking event recipients are registered too late — after `onAgentRunStart` fires — so any reasoning deltas emitted before the first agent event callback (e.g., from `emitReasoningStream()`) are silently dropped for opted-in WebSocket clients.
68c5f651c08c45d1: Replacing exact model ID matching with `modelIdMatchesDotDash` in `applyConfiguredProviderOverrides` can apply the wrong headers, costs, or token limits when both dotted and dashed variants of a model ID exist in `providerConfig.models`, because the fuzzy match may select whichever equivalent entry appears first instead of the intended exact match.
5d3f5aedac7552c9: Thinking events are only broadcast to run-scoped recipients registered at run start, so clients that reconnect or late-join a session mid-run have no way to subscribe to the in-flight reasoning stream until the next run begins.
6458cf02452833ba: The documentation incorrectly instructs users to run `openclaw plugins enable google` instead of the actual plugin ID `google-gemini-cli-auth`, which will cause the Gemini OAuth flow to silently fail because the wrong (inert) plugin is enabled.
622980b022ac0da7: Placing the secret-detection matrix strategy at the top-level job causes all steps (checkout, setup, and audits) to run 104 times instead of only the intended secret-scanning step, massively inflating CI runtime and flakiness.
daee826042259c03: The `thinking-events` capability check is bypassed for session subscribers because `sessions.subscribe` adds clients to `sessionEventSubscribers` without verifying `connect.caps`, allowing clients that connected without the `THINKING_EVENTS` capability to receive chain-of-thought reasoning frames.
92399bf73acb914e: The `@mariozechner/pi-*` package versions were bumped to `0.58.4` in `package.json` but the `pnpm-lock.yaml` was not regenerated to match, causing CI installs to fail with `ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE` due to `--frozen-lockfile` being enabled by default.
08348702e82ff956: The `resolveSlackThreadContextData` function passes raw envelope timezone mode keywords (e.g., `local`, `host`, `user`) directly to `buildImageAnnotation`, which expects valid IANA timezone strings, causing it to silently fall back to UTC and producing incorrect annotation timestamps that can mislead the model's recency reasoning.
eafb3157c66559c2: The new 30-second default hook timeout is lower than the 60-second budget allowed for remote embedding queries, meaning valid memory recall operations in the `memory-lancedb` plugin can be silently killed mid-flight, causing the agent to start without recalled memories even though no actual failure occurred.
0ed264e3ffe1eec0: The `shouldApplyOpenAIAttributionHeaders` function only checks for an explicit `api.openai.com` base URL, missing the case where `model.baseUrl` is unset but the runtime already defaults to native OpenAI, causing valid OpenAI requests to silently skip the `originator`/`User-Agent` attribution headers.
304bd386943c6418: The `hookTimeoutMs` field was added to the Zod runtime schema but the generated base config schema (`GENERATED_BASE_CONFIG_SCHEMA`) was not regenerated, meaning config-schema consumers like the gateway API and editor UIs will be unaware of the new option despite runtime validation accepting it.
5716fb3d8e75087c: Applying a blanket 30-second timeout to all async void hooks via `callHandlerWithTimeout()` will cause `agent_end` hook handlers—such as the `memory-lancedb` auto-capture path, which can require multiple sequential embedding calls within a legitimate 60s–5min budget—to silently drop memory capture on slower providers even when individual operations are within normal system limits.
27db165df84bd7f5: Redundantly remocking the already-mocked `src/auto-reply/dispatch` module overwrites the `inboundCtxCapture` sink, causing Discord (and potentially Signal) dispatch calls to bypass context capture and making those contract test assertions non-deterministic or broken.
837a19f1ce25eb2b: Prepending bootstrap warning text to `params.prompt` for all runs, including heartbeat-triggered ones, breaks the exact-match shape the system prompt heartbeat rule requires, causing the model to return regular responses instead of `HEARTBEAT_OK` and producing avoidable heartbeat noise when bootstrap truncation occurs.
1543ee9217da2da4: When `NU_VERSION` is set inside a bash/zsh login shell, `getShellConfig()` now executes commands with `nu -c` while `detectRuntimeShell()` still reports the login shell, causing the model to generate bash/zsh syntax that will fail under nushell's incompatible command semantics.
a28b4600288e7ab8: `splitLongLineByBytes` splits strings using UTF-16 code unit indices without checking for surrogate pair boundaries, which can corrupt multi-byte Unicode characters (such as emoji) by producing chunks with unpaired surrogates.
cb6f1017204a9f67: The XML closing-tag suffix stripping is applied to all string parameters indiscriminately, which can silently corrupt legitimate file content in write/edit operations where fields like `content`, `oldText`, or `newText` may validly end with `</arg_value>` — the fix should scope the sanitization only to known affected fields like command and path arguments.
e4bce5d5ab1db709: When an account is deleted or renamed during the drain window of a channel hot reload, `postDrainStatus` becomes `undefined` but the code still calls `startChannel`, potentially resurrecting a removed account using stale base config settings instead of honoring the updated configuration.
eef16e480e0d0b10: The audit check incorrectly suppresses a wildcard authorization finding when a top-level `groupAllowFrom` restriction exists, producing a false negative because the Telegram runtime replaces (not intersects) the top-level allowlist with per-group overrides, leaving groups with `allowFrom: ["*"]` effectively unrestricted.
6b1a2254d5163e43: In `simple-chunker.ts`, `splitEntryIfNeeded` checks byte size to decide when to split but delegates to `splitLongLine` which enforces character length instead, causing CJK/emoji-heavy lines to exceed the configured byte budget and produce oversized chunks in multilingual content.
f2023d5e75db51d3: The `deepseek-api-key` auth choice is exposed in onboarding, but the DeepSeek plugin will silently fail to apply because `deepseek` is not added to `BUNDLED_ENABLED_BY_DEFAULT`, meaning the provider runtime remains disabled unless the user manually enables the plugin first.
9d1411021beb7bc4: The new cross-agent global session bypass logic is unreachable because `parseAgentSessionKey` always returns `null` for the `"global"`/`"unknown"` keys that `classifySessionKey` produces, causing `sameAgentScope` to always evaluate as `true` and leaving cross-agent global sessions permanently hidden in the dropdown.
b4d1566d889c7dda: In `group_topic_sender` Feishu topics, the unfocus resolver checks the shared `chat:topic:root` binding before the sender-scoped one, so `/unfocus` can incorrectly remove the shared ACP binding and report success while leaving the sender's actual focused binding intact.
24754f7473bca2ad: The `stop()` method permanently deletes all in-memory Feishu thread bindings without persisting them to disk, causing all `/focus` state to be irrecoverably lost on any monitor restart or transient reconnect since there is no reload path to restore them.
370f003a7683735d: On Windows, `path.relative()` returns backslash-separated paths that never match the forward-slash entries in `LEGACY_ALLOWLIST`, causing all allowlisted offenders to appear as new violations and incorrectly failing the lint check.
87272aa9a4ac2e11: The fallback logic in `resolveDeliveryAttempted` incorrectly returns `true` for any requested delivery, even when execution fails before a delivery attempt occurs, leading to inaccurate `lastDeliveryAttempted` telemetry.
e7b35a2401bcdd4d: When `agentId` is omitted, `filterSkillEntries` silently falls back to resolving the main agent's skill policy, which can cause non-main agent sessions to have skills incorrectly shown or hidden based on the wrong agent's policy overrides.
fa912ae8fd1c911a: In the shared-workspace path, skill filters from multiple agents are merged together, allowing an agent to inherit and expose skills that were only allowlisted by a co-tenant agent, potentially surfacing invalid or unauthorized command entries for each individual agent.
d3a90d36855a5860: The `--theme` CLI flag does not reliably apply the requested theme because importing the theme module before calling `setTheme()` freezes color functions (e.g., `theme.fg`, select-list colors, syntax highlighting) to their pre-override values, causing most UI elements to render with the wrong theme at runtime.
fd0b301aa1d5811c: The `/session idle` and `/session max-age` commands resolve the wrong Feishu binding for `group_topic_sender` conversations by matching the shared `chat:topic:root` record before the sender-scoped `:sender:<openId>` binding, causing lifecycle changes to be applied to the wrong session while live message routing correctly uses the sender-scoped one.
23f43b6edcb59812: The new import block in `channel.ts` reintroduces symbols already imported elsewhere in the file, causing `TS2300 duplicate identifier` errors that prevent the Slack channel module from building.
2d9aa4fb4b8a3929: Adding a second `ChannelPlugin` import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/signal` in `channel.setup.ts` duplicates an existing binding already imported via `./runtime-api.js`, causing a `TS2300: Duplicate identifier` TypeScript error that breaks compilation of the Signal setup module.
d86916a6c70fe025: The required `groups` argument has been removed from both `createWhatsAppPluginBase` call sites in the WhatsApp channel package, causing a missing required property type error that will prevent the package from compiling or loading.
60cc9ce7fcd2dda2: The new `openclaw/plugin-sdk/signal` import duplicates symbols already imported from `./runtime-api.js`, causing duplicate local bindings (TS2300) that will break type-checking and building of the Signal channel package.
d59cdc2ba75456ef: `hasWebSearchKey()` hard-codes known web search providers instead of querying the dynamic plugin registry, causing the security audit to falsely report web search as disabled even when a custom third-party provider is active at runtime.
e6bab975b49db180: The documentation incorrectly promises automatic next-day log recovery for all configurations, when in reality users with a fixed `logging.file` path will experience indefinite write suppression after hitting the file size cap since no daily rotation occurs to reset it.
1bdce0b5a22a0ffb: The `resolveTelegramAllowFromEntries` call in the setup wizard surface is missing the `proxy` and `network` transport parameters, so `@username` resolution during the allowlist setup step will fail for accounts that rely on custom Telegram proxy or network configurations, even though other paths like send/probe/audit correctly use those transport overrides.
86262a9c76a0cd37: The ACP command handler uses a `sendNodeEvent` helper that silently swallows transport errors, meaning failed event deliveries are treated as successes, which can leave runs permanently stuck in recovery with inconsistent replay and cancel semantics.
bbece4115723dfbd: When a model directive like `gpt-4o@20251001` is used without an explicit provider, the numeric-profile fallback resolves against `defaultProvider` instead of the inferred allowlist provider, causing the `@YYYYMMDD` suffix to persist in the model token and breaking fuzzy/allowlist matching for providerless shorthand flows.
a93cef3546b5a335: Changing the anonymous-probe check from inspecting individual auth fields to requiring `opts.auth === undefined` causes loopback probes that pass an empty `auth` object (e.g., from unresolved SecretRef paths) to be treated as authenticated, potentially attaching device identity and masking credential-resolution failures in status output.
576f343cc9775be8: Mutating a nested property of the `console` `@state` object instead of reassigning the top-level field bypasses Lit's reactivity system, causing the detail panel to fail to open or close reliably when a subagent is clicked.
aecd01fb87b1540f: The `tryGatewayCall` fallback indiscriminately replaces all errors—including auth failures, disconnections, and server exceptions—with fake demo data, which can silently mislead operators into trusting fabricated diagnostics instead of seeing that a real console request has failed.
dc1a3eeeb2cf5f5a: The incident is resolved using the wrong key (`${channelId}:${accountId}`) before the new incident is even recorded (which uses `${type}:${channel}`), causing successfully recovered channel crashes to remain permanently marked as active incidents and potentially triggering spurious OAG analysis or rollbacks.
32a1fda8fd537b64: When writing per-channel OAG config overrides, the code incorrectly reads the baseline value from the global resolver instead of the existing channel-scoped config, causing per-channel budgets and retry limits to be overwritten with wrong values and rollbacks to restore incorrect previous state.
d38781c6178e637d: The lightweight dispatch path in `oag-diagnosis-lightweight.ts` calls `recordDiagnosis()` a second time after `requestDiagnosis()` has already appended a pending record, resulting in duplicate `diagnosisId` entries that cause phantom pending diagnoses in OAG history and prematurely evict real records from the 20-slot history limit.
aa106fb52e0f65b0: The Argus recovery retry relies solely on an in-process `setTimeout`, so if the gateway restarts during the delay window, the persisted `recoveryResumeAt` metadata is never scanned or acted upon, leaving the session stuck in `recoveryState: "active"` and silently dropping the promised automatic retry.
2cea844b230c5628: When `seedream_generate` returns an error status, the script only prints a message and exits with code 0 instead of a non-zero exit code, causing automation and wrappers to incorrectly treat failed image generations as successes.
459dc68b891e75dd: A placeholder CI trigger entry ("do not merge") was accidentally added to the release changelog, which if shipped would publish misleading release notes to users.
f8747542743eaaf1: The pathological-input freeze guard only counts code fences anchored at column 0, missing valid Markdown fences with up to three leading spaces, so the exponential-backtracking hang in `marked.parse()` can still be triggered by inputs containing many unclosed indented fences.
1090dcf0b4b79dd0: When `requireMentionInThreads: false` allows the first unmentioned thread reply through, `effectiveWasMentioned` is incorrectly left as `false`, causing downstream directive gating to silently strip inline exec/elevated directives for that turn until the bot receives implicit mentions from thread participation.
a01f102b215c09b3: Because `scheduleChatScroll()` runs and retries before the async Mermaid render pipeline finishes expanding the SVG, the chat view can end up scrolled short of the bottom, leaving the newest content below the viewport until the user scrolls manually.
76873ce8f10ab2f1: The `soulFile` field is never persisted to disk because the strict channel config schemas don't declare it, causing `writeConfigFile()`'s revalidation step to silently drop or reject the value and leaving users with a config-validation failure instead of a saved channel update.
3dbf13b5fbb8131a: The new `soulFile` config key is written and persisted but never read by the runtime — the agent bootstrap and prompt assembly still hardcode `SOUL.md` — so configuring a custom soul file has no effect on the persona used for replies.
656dab4d6c546949: The standalone `src/acp/server.ts` entrypoint calls `routeLogsToStderr()` without first calling `enableConsoleCapture()`, meaning `console.log`/`console.info` are never actually redirected to stderr and can still corrupt the NDJSON stdout stream.
676638c449c33c52: Channel post command authorization ignores per-channel `groupPolicy` overrides, so channels explicitly configured with `groupPolicy: disabled` or `allowlist` can still execute native/plugin commands because the auth check only verifies the presence of a `groupConfig` entry rather than evaluating the channel's actual policy.
316b9f9bf9a50cb2: When `--workspace` is omitted from CLI sync commands, the configured `agents.defaults.workspace` path is ignored and sync operations silently run against the wrong default directory, leaving the actual agent workspace unmodified.
882b89ba8b83f3c5: The webhook handler calls `pullAndApplyWorkspaceSync` without passing the configured workspace directory, causing webhook-triggered syncs to write to the process default path instead of the gateway's configured agent workspace, which breaks sync consistency for deployments that define custom workspace paths.
f57bc71dbf050d4f: `mediaLocalRoots` is wired into `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` but never passed at either call site in `bot.ts`, leaving it `undefined` at runtime and causing agent-scoped local image paths to fail the allowlist check and degrade to raw path text instead of media.
cdf6771b811fcaf5: An unconditional global sort by `attachmentIndex` applied after synthetic placeholders are merged overrides intentional ordering set by `selectAttachments()` and `runCapability()`, breaking configurations like `attachments.prefer: "last"` and disrupting expected capability output sequencing.
92a19eddf2debea9: Defaulting `supportsUsageInStreaming` to `true` based solely on the provider ID is unsafe because vLLM/SGLang providers accept arbitrary base URLs that may point to proxies not implementing `stream_options.include_usage`, risking streaming parse failures or missing responses.
b474ab2fce8aa637: The `resolveDiscordEffectiveRoute` mock returns `undefined` instead of a valid route, causing `resolveDiscordNativeChoiceContext` to fall into its error fallback and making the `/think` autocomplete test unreliable since it no longer validates the intended session override path.
7258122f04e4ea63: Defaulting `supportsUsageInStreaming` to `true` based solely on provider ID (e.g., `vllm`/`sglang`) without verifying actual endpoint capability can silently enable `stream_options.include_usage` for incompatible backends, reintroducing the streaming failures the fallback logic was designed to prevent.
e70e880c7e39fa1c: In `resolveChoiceContext`, both the authorization and choice context resolution paths independently resolve thread metadata, causing redundant network/cache lookups on every autocomplete keystroke that can push response times over Discord's deadline and result in dropped or delayed suggestions.
505f2b46d6fc1e4d: The AIMLAPI "missing/invalid key" guidance is incorrectly triggered by a broad auth error matcher that also catches unrelated failures like model deprecation or subscription expiry, causing users to be misdirected to key setup instead of receiving proper fallback handling.
1ca95a2a84106e21: The `resolveCronCallerOptions` function relies on a `callerSessionKey` from request params, but the cron schema's `additionalProperties: false` blocks that field from ever being passed, causing `callerOwnsJob` to fall back to an allow-all mode and leaving cross-session cron job access fully unguarded despite the intended isolation fix.
54d39990af189f01: The `resolveCronCallerOptions` function fails to populate `callerAgentId` in the gateway caller options, causing agent-owned cron jobs (those with `agentId` set but no `sessionKey`) to be incorrectly rejected as unauthorized during scoped `cron.list`, `cron.update`, `cron.remove`, and `cron.run` requests, even for the rightful owner.
b1a4fcef0ed9ffc3: The OpenAI parameter fallback logic triggers a second request with the legacy `max_tokens` parameter on any non-401 4xx error rather than only when `max_completion_tokens` is explicitly rejected, risking overwriting the true root cause with a misleading `unsupported_parameter` error and making onboarding failures harder to diagnose.
1e075e32f9382355: The `resolveCronCallerOptions` function always returns `callerSessionKey: undefined`, causing the new ownership guard to treat all non-admin requests as if the caller is anonymous and bypass filtering, so non-admin clients can still retrieve all job names via `cron.runs` with `scope: "all"` — leaving the intended job-name information leak fix ineffective.
97ecd1513406b0a2: In `resolveCronCallerOptions`, hardcoding `callerSessionKey` to `undefined` causes `callerOwnsJob` to reject every job for non-admin callers when `denyWithoutIdentity: true` is set, resulting in an empty `jobNameById` map and broken name-based run-log queries for those clients.
0c1f827a57e40b32: Adding `"tavily"` as a valid literal in the Zod schema without wiring it into `resolveSearchProvider` causes any Tavily configuration to be silently ignored and fall back to Brave, resulting in unexpected authentication failures for users who configure Tavily as their web search provider.
2df7cf7f67c21070: A new Tavily extension was added under `extensions/tavily/` but the corresponding auto-labeling rule was not added to `.github/labeler.yml`, breaking PR triage and violating the repo's `AGENTS.md` contribution requirements.
50698db2c6bf0df7: By making the allowlist prompt conditional on `quickstartDefaults` and removing the `forceAllowFrom` fallback in `finalize`, callers that set `forceAllowFrom` without quickstart defaults will silently skip the intended DM allowlist enforcement for the Zalouser channel.
9ed245a54aecc15a: When a fallback model is used, skipping `setSessionRuntimeModel` leaves the session in a mixed state where the persisted `contextTokens` reflect the fallback run but the stored model reflects the primary, causing downstream session math like gateway utilization summaries to be skewed.
57b1a5f787c702f3: The directive-tag stripping logic in `stripRawContentMeta` and `stripInlineDirectiveTagsForDisplay` is too aggressive — it removes `[[reply_to_current]]` and `[[reply_to:<id>]]` tags anywhere in the text rather than only at leading control-tag positions, causing legitimate user/assistant content that mentions these tags inline to be silently deleted and lost from session recall.
59b04dcb94dd1b84: The `value.deliver !== false` guard silently swallows hook failures that are returned as `status: "error"` results rather than thrown exceptions, breaking observability for background automations because no main-session system event is injected when `deliver` is false.
6881d3850c7e9d0c: When a fallback model is used, the cron session correctly avoids persisting the fallback model/provider, but still persists `contextTokens` derived from the fallback model, causing inconsistent session metadata where the stored primary model and stored context window size describe different models.
9b2eeba5eae48956: The new relative import path `../../../src/channels/plugins/config-schema.js` in the Nostr plugin ties the runtime file to the monorepo source tree, causing a missing module error at startup in packaged installs where no top-level `src/` directory exists.
b36bce57ccb58a51: The LINE setup code was changed to import from a relative `../../../src/line/accounts.js` path that only exists in a local repository checkout, causing module resolution failures in npm/distributed installs where the `src/` directory is not present in the published package layout.
408d29745fa5141d: The bundled Codex auth hint guard incorrectly keys off `runtimePlugin.id` (the provider name) instead of `pluginId` (the owning plugin), causing any custom plugin that registers the `openai` provider ID to be mistakenly treated as the bundled OpenAI provider and shown incorrect Codex OAuth credentials.
8f396069c12f7d46: Removing `defaultModel` from Ollama's auth result causes the `--set-default` flag in `openclaw models auth login` to silently fail, leaving the user's default model unchanged instead of updating it to Ollama's recommended default as advertised.
88c4b4937fd62c7d: The new try/catch blocks around cron operations return raw error messages directly to clients via `String(err)`, bypassing the gateway's centralized `formatForLog` sanitization and potentially exposing sensitive information like file paths or internal substrings.
5ed2cea924cd8521: The `allowKeep: authChoice !== "ollama"` change causes `promptDefaultModel` to rebuild the model picker from an unordered catalog, discarding the remote-mode cloud default computed by `promptAndConfigureOllama` and potentially steering Ollama remote-mode users to the wrong default model.
352583da29a3f077: When `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is set to a malformed value, the fallback to `OPENCLAW_TEST_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is silently skipped, causing handshake-timeout tests to use the 10s default instead of their intended short timeout, potentially making tests slow or flaky.
21a829285959b211: The new assertion that no "duplicate plugin id" diagnostic exists is applied unconditionally to all test scenarios, but certain scenarios (e.g., "bundled beats auto-discovered global duplicate") are expected to emit that warning, which will cause false test failures and block the change.
d1babc4dac663e5f: The `debugDrawerOpen` state variable is added but never read or used to toggle debug logging, leaving the debug connection logger permanently disabled and the feature non-functional.
c292f5c3b195133d: When building the synthetic transcript message for `chat.send` requests with attachments, image data from `parsedImages` is omitted and only the text from `parsedMessage` is serialized, causing image-only turns to be broadcast as empty messages and mixed text+image turns to lose their attachments for live subscribers until a full refresh.
738776e7968cb684: The DebugDrawer component does not initialize its log state with existing buffered entries on mount, causing the panel to appear empty when opened after events have already been logged until a new event arrives.
4b0198b9405f92a6: Replacing the `app-defaults.ts` import with `debug-connection.ts` removes `DEFAULT_CRON_FORM` and `DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL_FILTERS`, which are still referenced later in the file, leaving unresolved identifiers that can prevent the Control UI from booting or cause a runtime crash.
ebba627c367136b4: When an in-memory message hasn't been persisted yet, `readSessionMessages(...).length` returns a stale count, causing the broadcasted `messageSeq` to be incorrect (0 for new sessions or a duplicate on later turns), which breaks ordering and reconciliation for `session.message`/`sessions.changed` subscribers.
aa776ba3dae765d4: Applying a shard matrix to the entire `secrets` job causes non-sharded steps like workflow auditing and `pnpm-audit-prod` to run 106 times instead of once, dramatically inflating CI cost and risking pipeline flakiness.
41d8a1787c827bbd: When updating a nested provider field in JSON/value mode, the helper returns `null` for paths that aren't exactly 3 segments, causing `collectDryRunRefs` to skip dependent refs and allowing `--dry-run` to incorrectly report success even when existing refs would become unresolvable after the provider change.
b35b3cfb63d25db1: Because `probeGatewayMemoryStatus()` maps all `callGateway()` failures — including permanent errors like unknown methods or auth failures — to a generic "unavailable" status, the new prefix-only check silently skips verification for `provider: "local"` setups, allowing `openclaw doctor` to falsely report local memory as healthy even when the gateway was never successfully contacted.
