a8ccbf92a293de84: The `supabase.saveInstance` RPC method is called by the new profile save flow but is never registered in the gateway, causing every save attempt to fail with an "unknown method" error.
0ccafd9fca72d5aa: The `resolveLoginProfiles` function renames the profile ID only after the GitHub Copilot provider has already written the hardcoded `github-copilot:github` profile to the auth store and config, resulting in a duplicate credential entry and the original unwanted profile persisting as the default.
5ae31b134d0d785f: The `resolveExaApiKey` helper only handles literal API key strings and ignores SecretRef objects (e.g., `{ source: "env", ... }`), causing configured Exa API keys to be treated as missing and triggering a `missing_exa_api_key` error instead of resolving the reference.
32dbd3e523f517f2: The new Exa API key credential path (`plugins.entries.exa.config.webSearch.apiKey`) is never registered in the secret target registry, so secret discovery, auditing, and resolution tooling will silently fail to enumerate or manage Exa web-search keys.
bb55db50498e1452: When subtracting one month from dates on the 29th–31st, `setUTCMonth(date.getUTCMonth() - 1)` can overflow into the current month (e.g., March 31st becomes March 3rd), causing `startPublishedDate` to be only days in the past instead of a full month, making month-filtered Exa searches return far more recent results than intended.
4966e6411f2da0f8: The Supabase connectivity check hard-codes a query against a `users` table, causing valid credentials to appear invalid for any project that doesn't use that specific table name.
92fa1d39a5790986: Skipping exec refs entirely before `collectDryRunResolvabilityErrors()` runs causes dry-run to falsely report success for broken configurations (e.g., missing provider aliases or source mismatches) that would fail at runtime, because static provider validation that occurs before any exec command is launched is never performed on the skipped refs.
d24c622404c71174: Trigger session settings (`contextMode`, `modelOverride`, `maxTokens`, `thinking`) are saved directly on `NodeData` by the editor but read from a nonexistent nested `trigger.data.sessionConfig`, causing user-configured model and context options to be silently dropped and replaced with defaults at execution time.
ada1ee5a4466032f: The workflow serializer saves Supabase node payloads under UI-only keys (`row`, `updates`, `function`, `paramsData`, `supabaseInstance`) that don't match the keys the executor expects (`data`, `functionName`, `args`, `instance`), causing INSERT/UPDATE/RPC nodes to fail at runtime with missing payloads and always fall back to the default Supabase instance.
bc040d6e83d8353c: The `extensionFastInfraChanged` predicate omits the shared `vitest.scoped-config.ts` file from its hard-coded watch list, meaning changes to that shared config will not trigger the `extension-fast` CI lane, potentially allowing breaking changes to silently skip testing across all extension lanes.
f1c330ed95f45d61: A broken relative link in `docs/design/README.md` points outside the `docs/` directory to a non-existent `../../ROADMAP.md`, causing `pnpm docs:check-links` to fail and resulting in a broken "Companion file" link on the published docs page.
5fdec8a399d8e86a: The mermaid copy button uses an inline `onclick` handler that will be silently blocked by the app's Content Security Policy, which explicitly disallows `'unsafe-inline'` scripts, rendering the copy button completely non-functional in production.
66ad1581f6b055ec: Recursive `executeWorkflow()` calls share the same `tokenTracking` object, so adding `trueChainResult.tokenTracking` back into `this.tokenTracking` at lines 333–339 double-counts token usage for every child step, causing any multi-step workflow to report inflated token and cost totals.
2309c7f9e385b8be: When a PR modifies both infrastructure files (e.g., `vitest.channels.config.ts`) and extension files simultaneously, the `changed-extensions` reason causes the CI matrix to only run extension-specific tests, leaving the modified infrastructure config completely untested.
348148ce16b70620: When the oldest delivered message lacks a numeric timestamp, the cursor is set to null and hasMore is forced false, silently disabling pagination even when more messages exist beyond the current page.
2a36c66590701306: The `before` pagination cursor uses an inclusive boundary (`ts <= beforeTs`), causing the boundary message to be re-included on every page request, which results in duplicate history entries and can trap clients in an infinite non-advancing pagination loop.
348ad5acacbd9b5c: The durable state skip list is built from `.env` key names alone without checking for empty or placeholder values, so variables like `OPENAI_API_KEY=` incorrectly block valid shell values from being copied into the service environment, causing provider authentication failures at runtime.
4ebd41a69c112bf8: The skip-key derivation uses `resolveStateDir` at install time, which may resolve to a legacy or alias directory that differs from the `~/.openclaw/.env` path the daemon actually reads at runtime, causing injected credentials to be silently dropped after installation.
8f868483706bd03a: The `readDurableStateEnvKeys` function resolves the `.env` path using `resolveStateDir` (which may use legacy dirs or `CLAWDBOT_STATE_DIR`), but the installed daemon loads its dotenv via `resolveConfigDir`, causing auth credentials to be incorrectly skipped at install time and missing at runtime when the two paths diverge.
f4dcbf62ec0621b9: The skill file documents non-existent commands (`operon-guard test <path> --quick` and `operon-guard report <path>`) that don't match the upstream 0.2.0 supported commands (`test`, `scan`, and `init`), which will mislead users into running invalid commands.
041aff5bd134423f: The durable state skip list is built from key names alone rather than filtering to non-empty values, so empty placeholder entries in the `.env` file (e.g., `OPENAI_API_KEY=`) will incorrectly suppress valid auth-ref secrets from being copied into the service environment, causing secret resolution failures at runtime.
8157e9347de16501: The SKILL.md frontmatter only defines a `uv`-based install path, causing `installSkill` to immediately fail on Linux/Windows hosts that lack `uv` or Homebrew, even though a standard `pip install operon-guard` method is available upstream.
bed16796f5bf7bd3: The documentation misleadingly implies that `test` accepts arbitrary skill directory paths, but the underlying `load_agent_from_path()` uses `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location()`, which returns `None` for directories lacking an `__init__.py`, causing the command to silently fail before any verification runs.
ff6e30b47269d30d: The `operon-guard` skill's preflight trust verification is self-defeating because its underlying `load_agent_from_path()` function fully executes untrusted agent code via `exec_module()` and class instantiation before any trust score is computed, meaning the security boundary it advertises is bypassed at the very step meant to enforce it.
231924fbec9a3aba: The documented `--threshold` flag doesn't exist in the CLI and the pass threshold is incorrectly stated as 70 when the tool hard-codes passing grades at 75+, which will mislead users into misreading failing scores as passing and attempting to use a flag that will be rejected.
594212647db51fcf: The `operon-guard scan` command always exits with code 0 regardless of findings, so using it as a pre-install gate in scripts will silently pass even when prompt-injection or PII issues are detected, making the safety check ineffective without a documented workaround.
55ce34d0d7a83f40: The `--json` flag is falsely advertised as CI/CD-friendly machine-readable output because the CLI unconditionally prints human-readable preamble lines (e.g., "Using spec", "Adapter: ...") to stdout before the JSON block, making the combined output unparseable by tools like `jq`.
949b4d6e8c439112: The mermaid fence rewriting logic converts all code blocks to `.mermaid-wrapper` placeholders globally, but the `renderMermaidInContainer()` function that resolves them to SVGs is only invoked in `.chat-thread` contexts, causing mermaid snippets rendered via other callers like `markdown-sidebar.ts` and `agents-panels-status-files.ts` to display as broken placeholder UI instead of diagrams or plain code blocks.
63a09d41e80e10dd: The `scan()` function silently sends 47 adversarial payloads through the real agent function during safety checks, meaning agents with side effects like messaging, state writes, or paid API calls can cause unintended spam or quota consumption when the scan is run.
279fc55180b07efc: The skill's documentation only warns about unsafe code execution during `scan` and `test` commands, but omits a warning that `init --agent` also executes unreviewed third-party code via `spec.loader.exec_module(...)` during module inspection, leaving users vulnerable during the documented setup flow.
71fd418051e81bb6: Because the skill's frontmatter lacks an `os` allowlist, OpenClaw surfaces it on Windows despite the example using POSIX-only shell constructs (`set -o pipefail`, `grep`) that will fail in PowerShell/CMD, breaking the advertised JSON workflow on stock Windows hosts.
213b196394cb01af: The `.chat-send-btn` style change pairs a dark gray background (`--muted-strong`) with a dark foreground (`--text-strong`) in light mode, dropping icon contrast to ~2.9:1 and making the send button icon difficult to read.
ab26cf8d701bee0f: The stale active turn recovery logic is non-atomic — a race condition between `readActiveTurn` and `clearActiveTurn` allows a newly written live turn record to be incorrectly deleted, stripping crash-recovery coverage and potentially causing incorrect recovery behavior.
d19a202feda4f2a5: Because `.ci/multipass` is nested inside the root `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, running `pnpm --dir ./.ci/multipass install --frozen-lockfile` resolves the root workspace instead of multipass's own lockfile, leaving multipass without its dependencies and causing subsequent `pnpm dev roundtrip` steps to fail on missing modules.
2ab8659a69d16bbf: The `auth.order` configuration example in the failover docs directs users to the wrong config file path (`~/.openclaw/gateway/config.json`) instead of the actual path OpenClaw reads (`~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`), causing multi-account rotation to silently fail even when the setting is configured correctly.
a04ea2d7d9858ddb: The pre-hook subprocess is not wired to the job's abort signal, so if the cron job times out, the shell command continues running in the background and can overlap with subsequent runs or cause unintended side effects after the job has already been marked as failed.
cc91e5e12ad0eb99: The multipass-openclaw-bridge script fabricates both the sent message and the ACK response locally rather than communicating with a real backend, so end-to-end roundtrip failures in payload mapping, routing, or credentials will never be caught by this test lane.
5eb5828c7502ead6: When `runHeartbeatOnce` receives a partial heartbeat override (e.g., `{ target: "last" }`) from cron wake-now calls, it silently discards the configured default `preHook`, causing resource and health gate checks to be bypassed entirely.
b5d6b597bbda3aba: When a pre-hook command's output exceeds the 64KB `maxBuffer` limit, Node.js throws an error that causes the hook to be reported as a failure even if the command itself exited successfully, meaning a verbose but otherwise healthy hook can incorrectly block execution based on log volume alone.
c8966332e01bb7ab: The pre-hook unit tests use hardcoded POSIX shell syntax that is incompatible with Windows' `cmd.exe`, risking CI failures on the supported Windows test matrix even when the underlying feature code is correct.
b5325461af673949: The `preHook` field was added to the add/update input schemas but omitted from the exported `CronJobSchema`, creating an inconsistency where gateway operations can persist and return `preHook` but consumers using `CronJobSchema` cannot represent or validate it.
acc112f803273a7b: The `isMissingAgentWaitStructuredMetadata` function incorrectly treats any successful snapshot with an undefined `stopReason` as incomplete, causing `agent.wait` to unnecessarily enter a 1-second grace delay even when metadata is already final, introducing avoidable latency for valid runs.
31996ed373d3d11e: The removal of the `shouldRetryTurnWithFreshHandle` recovery path in the catch block means that transient `acpx` process exits no longer trigger a retry with a new session handle, causing users to receive hard `ACP_TURN_FAILED` errors instead of being transparently recovered.
adbb1ab480c41c9f: The `allowExec` guard is only checked in write mode, so dry-run and interactive preflight flows silently succeed even when a plan contains exec references, causing operators to complete the entire wizard before hitting a failure that requires rerunning with `--allow-exec`.
d076c7c74bba62ff: When a `preHook.command` trims to empty during cron job creation, the normalizer sets `preHook` to `null`, but the gateway's create (`cron.add`) schema only accepts `preHook` as an object, causing the request to be rejected — meaning the "clear on empty" logic works for patches but silently breaks create operations.
650bd3c28c6b34e1: Moving `ensureRuntimeHandle` outside the `runTurn` try/catch block causes initialization failures to bypass failure bookkeeping, leaving observability counters and persisted session state inconsistent when early runtime setup throws an error.
e3e06b65a99d59d8: The removal of the `shouldRetryTurnWithFreshHandle` logic from the catch path means transient early `acpx` exits no longer trigger an automatic retry with a fresh runtime handle, causing users to receive hard `ACP_TURN_FAILED` errors instead of recovering transparently on a second attempt.
49d0cd5320db8580: Moving `ensureRuntimeHandle` outside the `try/catch/finally` block means initialization failures skip session error-state updates and completion metrics, potentially leaving stale session metadata and observability gaps.
c9b5893c1e22d723: The new `gateway.staticFiles` config option was added to the TypeScript type but never registered in the Zod validation schema, causing any deployment that attempts to use it to be rejected at config validation time.
ad2ec39e5ff8c813: The `withTimeout` wrapper uses only the global agent default timeout instead of the per-session timeout override, causing premature turn aborts and a behavior regression when a session is configured with a longer timeout than the global default.
eb597b108cc8b2f0: The new `buildToolContext` always injects the current Telegram topic as `currentThreadTs` based solely on chat ID matching, causing cross-topic tool replies that explicitly target a different topic in the same forum chat to have their intended topic overridden and the message silently delivered to the wrong thread.
a64cacafb7aee8b9: The new `static-files` request handler runs before authentication is enforced and never calls `authorizeHttpGatewayConnect`, allowing unauthenticated requests to read files from the configured static files root when gateway auth is enabled.
2204526fbb65c0c1: The newly extracted `threadId` field is never forwarded through `extractMessagingToolSend()` into duplicate suppression logic, causing `shouldSuppressMessagingToolReplies()` to miss Telegram forum/topic sends and incorrectly emit duplicate assistant text when the tool has already posted to the same topic.
a994220dbebf06f5: When a tool call targets a bare Telegram DM chat ID without a `:topic:` suffix, the new `targetExplicit` early return in `resolveTelegramAutoThreadId` discards the active thread ID, causing replies to fall back to the root DM instead of the correct topic and breaking session continuity.
061b1e6f815d716d: The `isSafePath()` function only performs a lexical path check and does not account for symlinks, allowing symbolic links inside the served directory to point to files outside the static root, which can then be read and served via `createReadStream()`.
03fd224a9cdd7cba: The new `"reuse"` cron session target was added to the TypeScript type but was not surfaced in the CLI's `cron add` or `cron edit` commands, making it impossible for operators to create or manage reuse jobs through the supported CLI workflow despite the backend already accepting it.
605ebc0a3aab5a29: When `combinedSignal` aborts during an `onEvent` await, the resulting `AbortError` is incorrectly routed through the generic turn-failure path, leaving the session in an `error` state instead of returning it to `idle` after a successful cancellation.
3d3bb7ff727fdc12: The new `shouldSync` freshness gate skips reading external CLI credentials when the local token isn't near expiry, causing stale or revoked credentials from Codex/Qwen/MiniMax CLIs to go undetected until the token approaches expiration.
29aa7e9d56b9ab69: `isExternalProfileFresh` incorrectly returns `true` when `cred.expires` is missing or non-numeric, causing `shouldSync` to be skipped and leaving malformed or legacy profile entries permanently stuck without ever pulling fresh credentials from the external CLI.
e1000fec7fbc58a6: The OAuth token refresh logic was refactored to only handle hardcoded providers, removing the plugin hook that allowed extension providers with custom `refreshOAuth` implementations to renew their tokens, causing permanent refresh failures for those providers after expiry.
34ab85bc4fca4791: The cleanup logic only removes order entries present in `toRemove` (built from existing `store.profiles` keys), so stale IDs already missing from `store.profiles` are never pruned from `store.order`, allowing `resolveAuthProfileOrder` to treat a dangling-ID-only order as authoritative and return no usable profiles even when valid ones exist.
fc78ef33ba85b127: `isActiveThreadBindingRecord` uses `isConfiguredDiscordThreadBindingAccount` which only checks if a Discord account is enabled rather than verifying `threadBindings.enabled`, causing stale bindings from disabled accounts to be treated as active foreign owners and incorrectly dropping valid thread messages until those bindings expire.
e92c4ddf83189538: The foreign-owner suppression path uses a stale startup config snapshot (`params.cfg`) instead of the live config, which can cause valid thread messages to be incorrectly dropped after hot config changes in multi-account setups.
3721906753c63632: Classifying all `browser act kind="click"` actions as navigation boundaries incorrectly resets the search loop streak on harmless clicks, allowing repetitive search-click patterns to evade loop detection entirely.
c0fc808f9caa8fd8: Removing the `plugin.origin !== "bundled"` guard causes `resolveNonBundledProviderPluginIds()` to incorrectly return bundled providers, bypassing the zero-load fast path and forcing heavyweight bundled provider plugins to be imported in test environments where they should be disabled by default, regressing unit and gateway test stability.
256f609ff4fdc8ef: The prepaint theme script reads the first matching `localStorage` key rather than the active gateway's scoped key, which can apply the wrong theme when multiple gateways have saved settings, causing a visible flash until the app corrects it on hydration.
c34a2343e06e4b5a: When a bundled plugin is explicitly enabled in config, its provider IDs are returned by both the bundled catalog path and the plugin hooks path, causing duplicate model entries to appear in model listings and picker flows.
dd016b7aacb54da1: The browser search loop detector stores history under the pre-navigation tab ID instead of the updated `targetId` returned after a `browser navigate` call, causing subsequent same-tab search actions to be keyed to a stale ID and making `findRecentBrowserTargetContext()` unable to retrieve prior search context, which prevents the `browser_search_storm` detector from catching follow-up in-tab query refinement loops.
98575b005377f3dc: The new provider-inference path only searches `agents.defaults.models` entries but ignores `agents.defaults.model.fallbacks`, so bare model names that are exclusively allowlisted via fallbacks under a non-default provider still fail resolution with "model not allowed" even though those fallbacks are treated as valid selections elsewhere in the codebase.
53d8242fdfa55d91: When inferring a provider from a bare model name like "claude-sonnet-4-6", passing only the parsed model ID to `inferUniqueProviderFromConfiguredModels()` strips upstream gateway prefixes (e.g., "vercel-ai-gateway/anthropic/"), causing the lookup to fail and incorrectly rejecting a uniquely allowlisted model in gateway/openrouter setups.
490d8fb51aca8a5b: The documentation instructs skill authors to register custom slash commands and session hooks (e.g., `/careful`, `/freeze`) that the skill runtime does not actually support, meaning those commands will never be invocable.
8199b36e257f0bff: The provider inference fallback uses a model string already normalized against the wrong default provider, causing allowlisted bare aliases like `sonnet-4.6` or `gemini-3-pro` to fail with "model not allowed" because their canonical IDs are provider-dependent and must be normalized per candidate rather than upfront.
f253608750bf453d: Adding `SignalAccountConfig` to the Signal runtime-api export without updating the corresponding allowlist in `runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts` will cause CI test failures even though the build passes.
f4c04885fc1b4c7c: Storing user-specific configuration files inside the skill directory causes them to be bundled into the distributable `.skill` package, potentially leaking a maintainer's local settings or credentials to downstream users.
c9ffd12cec94c6ff: Broadening the `api_error` JSON match to ignore the error message text causes `classifyFailoverReason()` to misclassify permanent failures (e.g., invalid API key, quota exhaustion) as retryable timeouts, leading to wasteful profile rotation and silent suppression of the real root cause.
0ce7337f0b0acb27: The `.chat-bubble-actions` container is only revealed on hover, so keyboard users who tab into the copy/expand buttons will find them invisibly focused since `opacity: 0` is never overridden on `:focus-visible`, creating an accessibility regression.
c100e69be151169e: Calling `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded()` before `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded()` causes the post-compaction token count to be marked fresh, which prevents the memory flush gate from triggering and silently drops durable note writes to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` in the stale-usage scenario this patch intends to fix.
e0cebe41545ad090: The Discord provider module imports `forgetDiscordManagedBotIdentity` and `rememberDiscordManagedBotIdentity` from `../accounts.js`, but neither symbol is exported from that file, causing ESM import resolution to fail at startup and preventing the Discord monitor from running.
4035ddcd66f88cc5: The Grok provider declares a new `credentialPath` pointing to `plugins.entries.xai.config.webSearch.apiKey`, but its runtime credential accessors still read/write the legacy `tools.web.search.grok.apiKey` path, causing callers that rely on `getConfiguredCredentialValue` to incorrectly treat a properly configured user as unconfigured and skip Grok during auto-detection.
36d33101e73e6cc4: The blind-create fallback is triggered by any `listMattermostCommands` error (timeouts, 5xx, etc.) rather than only 403 permission-denied responses, which can cause transient failures to incorrectly force create-only mode and leave slash callbacks inactive on deployments with existing commands.
272e4a7699402e92: The `agentAbortControllers` field is never passed to `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` at the call site, causing the new loop to iterate over `undefined` and throw a fatal error that can crash the gateway server on the first cleanup tick.
a247aaed168686c4: When saving the slash command cache, the entire on-disk cache is overwritten with only the successfully registered teams, causing any team that failed registration due to a transient error to lose its previously cached tokens and rendering its existing slash command callbacks unable to authenticate after a restart.
851fb159465f9f39: The `modelOverride` field intended to take priority in `resolveModelSelection` is always `undefined` in practice because `listSessions` payloads strip that metadata before returning rows, so heartbeat and compaction events still incorrectly overwrite the status bar model, leaving the fix ineffective outside of mocked tests.
c419759dca16d720: Replacing the `<label>` wrapper with a `<div>` for the raw config textarea removes its accessible name association, breaking screen reader and keyboard navigation support for assistive-technology users.
cb64ae6d22075f7e: Four compatibility-related exports (`buildPluginCompatibilityNotices`, `buildPluginCompatibilityWarnings`, `formatPluginCompatibilityNotice`, and `summarizePluginCompatibility`) were removed from `status.ts` but are still imported by CLI command surfaces, causing ESM module load failures that prevent those commands from starting.
9c4e57f7c76aa2d4: The new preflight compaction call in `followup-runner.ts` can compact the transcript before a queued turn without first invoking `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded()`, causing durable memory notes to be silently skipped for busy sessions that go through the followup queue's stale-usage path.
93912ca94cca5935: The Discord extension now directly imports `DiscordAccountConfig` and `DiscordActionConfig` from the external `openclaw/plugin-sdk/discord` package instead of routing them through the local `./runtime-api` barrel, violating the repository's extension SDK self-import guardrail and risking packaging and import drift.
8d6711ba1d63ff92: Using colon-delimited `serverName:toolName` identifiers for bundled MCP tools causes them to fail the `TOOL_CALL_NAME_RE = /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/` regex validator, which can silently drop assistant tool-call blocks during persistence and history replay, breaking tool-call/tool-result pairing and orphaning MCP tool interactions.
d5319818240f5e86: The test runtime mocks were renamed from `describeMessageTool` to `listActions`/`getCapabilities` before the contract suite's discovery logic was updated to match, causing Telegram and Discord channel tests to return null for actions and capabilities instead of the expected contract surface.
115fbebaee51e174: When an HTTP MCP session fails during `connect`, the error handling path skips cleanup of the partially initialized `Client` and transport, potentially leaking resources across retries — unlike the stdio path which correctly calls `disposeSession` on failure.
18b0eeabc867f0bd: Because `scheduleChatScroll()` is queued before `scheduleMermaidRender()` and completes within ~150ms while Mermaid rendering takes significantly longer (due to lazy import and SVG generation), the chat scroll position is calculated before the Mermaid diagram expands the page, leaving bottom-pinned users with the transcript incorrectly scrolled away from the latest message.
521bb61cbe034636: The code ignores the `bindFunctions` callback returned by `api.render()`, which must be called after inserting the SVG into the DOM, causing any interactive Mermaid diagram features like tooltips and click bindings to silently break.
d424f0e32f4e63fd: When a dynamic model wins the preference check over an already-resolved entry, `applyConfiguredProviderOverrides` rebuilds it from the plugin template without copying the discovered `baseUrl`, silently reverting requests to the default endpoint and breaking deployments that route traffic through a custom proxy configured in `models.json`.
8e64c8e631e25dbe: When chat transcript search filters in or out messages containing Mermaid diagrams, `scheduleMermaidRender()` is never called because the search-driven rerender bypasses the tracked lifecycle fields, leaving newly inserted `.mermaid-block` elements blank until an unrelated update triggers rendering.
f0077225fe233ab0: Mermaid is initialized once without a theme setting, causing diagrams to always render with the default light theme even when the UI is in dark mode, resulting in visual inconsistency and potential low-contrast rendering.
5125de215d7cde05: When `preferResolvedModel()` merges a dynamic model over a discovered one, it never restores the discovered `contextWindow`, causing downstream prompt sizing to budget against the larger template window (1.05M tokens) instead of the backend's actual lower limit, which can result in oversized requests for proxy-backed Codex installs.
8e96e66d7cbb1db4: The Mermaid fence-to-placeholder conversion in `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml()` is applied globally, but since `scheduleMermaidRender()` is only called in the chat lifecycle, views like the markdown sidebar and agent status panel will render a blank box instead of a diagram or code fence.
c8d735e2ae6f3d3e: When the dynamic model wins the preference check, spreading `discoveredHeaders` after `dynamicHeaders` causes stale headers from `models.json` to override configured provider headers, breaking auth and routing for setups that rely on `Authorization`, `User-Agent`, or tenant-routing header precedence.
8cc0723654356675: When `syncPluginRuntimeState` rebinds to the startup registry and cache key, it causes lazily bootstrapped channel plugins to silently disappear because `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin()` memoizes bootstrap attempts by registry key and never re-attempts them, permanently breaking outbound sends to those channels until the process restarts.
9031f0a637059c65: The new `shouldCompareDynamicOverride` guard incorrectly routes explicitly configured model rows (e.g., an inline `gpt-5.4` entry with a capped `contextWindow`) through the dynamic override path, causing `preferResolvedModel()` to select the synthetic high-context model and allowing downstream prompt budgeting to exceed the backend's real context window.
cc427c110048861c: After setting `activate: false`, the snapshot registry returned by `loadOpenClawPlugins()` is never passed to `listChannelPlugins()`, so `channels.*` schema and UI metadata are still built from the stale active registry, leaving channel configuration incorrect until the gateway restarts.
2990b032a560017c: Switching from `transformAzureUrl(baseUrl, modelId)` to `transformAzureConfigUrl(baseUrl)` causes Azure deployment URLs that already contain `/openai/deployments/<deployment>` to have `/openai/v1` incorrectly appended to the full path, persisting a malformed base URL that the runtime cannot call.
4dc3ca5b501aa37a: When re-onboarding an existing Azure custom model, only `contextWindow` is updated while new fields like `reasoning`, image input support, and `compat.supportsStore` are never applied, leaving stale model metadata that breaks dependent functionality.
95dcfcf014f14e3d: When an OpenRouter model reference like `openrouter/healer-alpha` is resolved via the new alternate stripped-ID lookup, the returned model loses its required `openrouter/` prefix, producing an invalid API model ID that breaks OpenRouter-native requests.
17cbbe881dfb2816: When updating an existing Azure custom model, spreading `nextModel` overwrites the entire `compat` object with only `{supportsStore: false}`, silently discarding any previously set compatibility overrides (e.g., `thinkingFormat`, tool quirks) and potentially causing protocol-level failures on subsequent onboarding runs.
3104b6bdc0db0ff9: Hard-coding `compat.supportsStore = false` for onboarded Azure custom models makes the Azure Responses compaction path permanently unreachable, preventing `context_management` injection and regressing large-context session handling compared to the supported Azure Responses flow.
62997d688f494724: The new `hasSameHost()` fast-path in `resolveUniqueEndpointId` incorrectly collapses distinct Azure project endpoints sharing the same hostname into a single provider entry, causing the second project to overwrite the first's `baseUrl` and orphaning any models registered under the original project.
6a99e8bcb78d6571: The Azure custom endpoint verification incorrectly probes the `chat/completions` surface instead of the `openai-responses` API that the runtime config uses, causing valid Azure Responses API deployments to be falsely rejected during onboarding.
77cef0ed34bbd904: The reasoning/image heuristic incorrectly matches the "35" in Azure `gpt-35-turbo` deployment names, causing `applyCustomApiConfig()` to set `reasoning: true` and image input capabilities for GPT-3.5 deployments, which will break those setups by sending unsupported image and thinking settings to the API.
c22078b5948c5ef1: The capability validation logic only checks the primary model or override instead of all configured fallbacks, causing valid image generation requests to be incorrectly rejected before the runtime fallback chain can attempt them with a compatible provider.
02127759497526e7: When re-onboarding an existing Azure model, the stale per-model `api` field is never cleared or overwritten, causing runtime resolution to continue using the old adapter (e.g., `openai-completions`) even after the provider has been updated to a new value (e.g., `openai-responses`).
3f2e0e9243725626: Setting `compat.supportsStore = false` unconditionally for all Azure custom models blocks the Responses compaction path permanently, causing user-configured `responsesServerCompaction: true` to silently have no effect and denying large-context Azure sessions access to a documented workaround.
4bc3b6f51ead9547: The import of `clearSharedPluginRuntimeOptions` from a non-existent `shared-runtime-options.js` module will cause an ESM module-resolution error at load time, breaking gateway startup before any server code can execute.
8b8c8789caf1c110: The `subagent.abort` implementation dispatches an `agent.abort` gateway call that has no registered handler or advertised method, causing all plugin-driven abort attempts to silently fail with an "unknown method" error and leaving subagent runs uncancel-able.
28022bc3f18a5dca: When an agent session's main key alias changes mid-run, abort requests are normalized against the new config value rather than the alias stored at run-start, causing `abortAgentRunById` to silently fail and leaving the active run un-abortable via RPC until it times out or finishes naturally.
357266a04e0f0c37: The configured-binding registry is primed before `loadOpenClawPlugins` activates the new plugin registry, causing the cached prime result to be immediately stale and deferring compile errors for newly loaded channel adapters to runtime instead of surfacing them at gateway startup.
9a58a63a5537b396: The `createGatewaySubagentRuntime.abort` method dispatches an `agent.abort` event that has no registered handler in the gateway method table (the correct handler is `chat.abort`), causing all subagent abort calls to fail at dispatch time instead of canceling the intended runs.
6e8d2d595019eafb: In `flushPendingMessages`, retrying all items in `toSend` after a mid-batch failure causes duplicate messages because `sendMSTeamsMessages` sends sequentially and earlier blocks may have already been successfully delivered before the error occurred.
bade93dd31bc0671: When a slow client is disconnected for exceeding MAX_BUFFERED_BYTES, the per-identity sequence number is not advanced before closing, causing reconnected clients to resume from a stale seq and silently skip gap detection for all missed events.
659d29791b2345d8: Switching from a single global sequence counter to per-client sequence counters causes the counter to reset to 1 on reconnect while clients retain their previous `lastSeq`, silently suppressing gap detection and allowing critical event-driven state (e.g., pending executions, pairing approvals) to be lost after a transient disconnect.
d9d164f4e9a9391a: The new regex for Z.ai error code 1311 is added to `ERROR_PATTERNS.billing`, but `isBillingErrorMessage` skips that pattern for messages longer than 512 characters due to an early length-gate, causing billing errors wrapped in longer payloads to be misclassified (e.g., as rate limit errors) downstream.
15fa4f7fa282b5d3: The per-message retry fallback silently swallows send exceptions and only logs a debug message, allowing `flushPendingMessages` to resolve successfully with an empty result even when all delivery attempts fail, meaning `onError` is never triggered and the turn is falsely reported as settled.
5e36d32dab9ca6d7: When a union schema contains any string variant, the early return on `hasStringVariant` skips boolean coercion for mixed `boolean | enum-string` unions, causing user-entered `true`/`false` text values to remain as strings and fail schema validation instead of being correctly coerced to booleans.
fadef123dffb3f85: A process-global `lastCancelReason` variable is shared across concurrent compaction sessions, meaning under normal multi-session load one session can overwrite another's cancellation reason before it is read, causing `/compact` and overflow recovery to report the wrong root cause.
bf2041191d1c0219: Applying the 93-way matrix at the job level causes all steps — including `detect-private-key`, zizmor, and `pnpm-audit-prod` — to run redundantly across every shard, massively increasing CI time and runner usage without any additional coverage, when only the secret scanning step actually needs sharding.
8feca091c3f4582f: The `withTimeout` helper rejects the promise after the specified duration but does not cancel the underlying async operation, allowing timed-out memory searches to continue running in the background and potentially causing a buildup of overlapping in-flight requests under a slow or unresponsive memory backend.
2c8d8841009c83eb: When Claude's `stream-json` mode emits an empty or missing `result` field, `parseCliJsonl` returns `null` instead of falling back to extract the actual reply text from earlier assistant/message events, causing the runner to display raw NDJSON output to the user.
78b6f5b6a17ed07d: The timeout detection logic only checks for `Error` objects as abort reasons, causing cron-driven timeouts that abort with a plain string (`"cron: job execution timed out"`) to set `aborted=true` but leave `timedOut=false`, silently skipping the timeout-specific handling branches.
7accc9dd71633c27: The new `BindingToolsSchema` allows `tools.allow`/`tools.deny` to be declared on route bindings in the Zod schema, but the policy is never actually enforced because `ResolvedAgentRoute` doesn't propagate it and the tool-filtering pipeline ignores it, creating a silent security bypass where per-route tool restrictions appear valid but have no effect.
4f38d966fdd215a9: The `rotateDeviceToken` function's scope-escalation error return was changed from the established `{ ok: false, reason: "..." }` contract to a new `{ error, message, details }` shape, breaking existing callers and tests that still branch on the `ok` field.
f91ca13fcf408857: Deleting `talk-config-contract.json` causes `talk-config.contract.test.ts` to crash with an ENOENT error at module load time before any assertions run, completely eliminating talk-config contract test coverage.
8d78744b7843bfa6: When block streaming and final-mode TTS are both enabled, the `kind === "block"` guard causes the synthesized audio reply to fall back to the channel root instead of reusing the established thread, splitting a single response across two locations.
6ab540fd53b1273f: HTTP 529 errors are inconsistently classified as "rate_limit" for object-shaped errors but "timeout" for string-shaped errors, causing Anthropic overload failures to be bucketed differently depending solely on the error wrapper format.
bbfca32e0cfa769b: Changing `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS` and `CLAWDBOT_SKIP_CHANNELS` from `1` to `0` in the `gateway:dev` script disables the intended channel-skipping behavior, causing real channel providers to start during local development instead of the lightweight dev mode, which can lead to startup errors and unintended external side effects.
feb2e75f30686c6b: Passing `durationMs` unconditionally to `formatResponseUsageLine()` causes the stopwatch indicator to appear on all usage footer modes, including the leaner `/usage tokens` mode where it shouldn't appear — the parameter needs to be gated behind a `responseUsageMode === "full"` check.
2d576887a0ee37fc: The `symlinkPathWithFallback` fallback on non-admin Windows can copy `node_modules` as a real directory into `dist-runtime`, which the cleanup step in `tsdown-build.mjs` won't remove on subsequent rebuilds (since it only deletes symlinks), causing stale dependency trees to persist and degrading build/watch performance over time.
3d3626597cc7f033: The `describeXiaomiVideo` function incorrectly uses the text provider's base URL (e.g., `https://api.xiaomimimo.com/anthropic`) for video requests, causing them to be routed to `/anthropic/chat/completions` instead of the correct MiMo video endpoint under `/v1`.
6ede5d78442c4b30: The video understanding fallback model (`mimo-v2-omni`) is never used because the media runner always passes the active agent model (`mimo-v2-flash`) as `params.model`, which only supports text input, causing video description to silently use the wrong model on default Xiaomi installations.
14f2fbb517ab87f6: Returning `null` for `"overloaded"` in `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason` prevents `markAuthProfileFailure()` from persisting cooldown state, causing `runWithModelFallback` to bypass the cooldown-aware rotation logic and repeatedly hammer an overloaded provider instead of failing over, which worsens latency and request pressure during sustained outages.
afc1dae48def9037: Registering Xiaomi as an image-capable provider causes image understanding to fail on default Xiaomi installs because the default onboarding model (`mimo-v2-flash`) is text-only and will be rejected by `describeImageWithModel`.
fc0a7cec238053a1: When a best-effort delivery abort occurs inside `deliverOutboundPayloads()`, the error is swallowed and returns `result: undefined`, causing the `isAborted()` guard to be bypassed and `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn()` to incorrectly report `status: "ok"`, which suppresses retries and can mark timed-out one-shot jobs as successfully completed.
f39b59a19da7b555: A new no-config fallback in `resolveCommandAuthorizedFromAuthorizers` unconditionally returns `true` when no authorizers are configured, inadvertently allowing control commands from non-allowlisted DM senders by bypassing the existing DM access group gating contract.
033a7147bd945057: When `session.historyLimit` is set to `0`, switch targets are still built from persisted `sessionHistory` entries, allowing old sessions to remain reachable via `/session` commands instead of being disabled as documented.
02925199cd20d077: The new `contextTotal` calculation in `grouped-render.ts` omits alternative cache-read token field aliases (e.g., `cached_tokens`, `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`) that are normalized elsewhere, causing the grouped chat footer to incorrectly display near-0% context occupancy for Moonshot/Kimi-style sessions that use those alternate field shapes.
9040fe71f5b93706: The new "no authorizers configured = unrestricted" fallback was only added to the TypeScript channel implementation but not synced to the mirrored CommonJS shim used by the root SDK export, causing plugins that import via the root entrypoint to incorrectly deny commands when no allowlist is configured.
db4d58a97f57ed82: Expanding `isToolCallBlock()` to recognize `toolCall`/`functionCall` types causes `validateAnthropicTurns()` to strip OpenAI-style tool call blocks before `sanitizeToolUseResultPairing()` can repair them, orphaning the corresponding `toolResult` messages and causing providers like Moonshot to silently lose valid tool context.
d5cd008fc81ae49e: The `isFeishuServerError` retry predicate only checks for `Error` instances, causing plain-object 5xx errors (e.g., `{ response: { status: 502 } }`) to be misclassified as non-retryable, which bypasses the new backoff logic and surfaces transient media download failures directly to users.
67c83ec0edf70f92: The new video retry heuristic requiring `content` to be passed is never triggered in production because all existing callers of `downloadMessageResourceFeishu` omit the `content` parameter, causing `isVideo` to always be false and iOS video downloads to silently fall back to a single retry instead of the intended three-attempt backoff.
9ce4e8aec6fb3135: The new fallback that returns `true` when no authorizers are configured is too broad — it unintentionally grants command access in DM and group channels where empty allowlists were meant to deny all commands, because those paths produce `{ configured: false }` authorizer entries that now incorrectly satisfy the allow condition.
b92a8b7ed0c5111e: When a `lane: "cron"` run is aborted or expires, cleanup incorrectly targets the raw `"cron"` lane instead of the resolved `"nested"` lane stored in `clearableLanes`, leaving stale active-task state that can permanently block that lane.
eb29d9e80a28b57c: Unconditionally overwriting `context.agentAbortControllers[runId]` with a new `AbortController` when a run ID is reused causes the original in-flight run to lose its abort reference, making it impossible to cancel or expire via `agent.abort` or timeout logic.
ca405b00a2288adb: The dedupe grace timer incorrectly reuses the full original `timeoutMs` instead of the remaining timeout budget after the first `Promise.race`, allowing `agent.wait` to exceed the caller's requested deadline by up to an additional full timeout window.
62154260378bc74c: When `waitForMessageHandlerIdle()` times out during teardown, `forgetDiscordManagedBotIdentity` is called immediately while in-flight inbound jobs may still be running, causing those messages to nondeterministically lose `SenderManagedAccountId` metadata because the identity is cleared before all active jobs have finished resolving it.
f4ff6644bf90839b: The 1-second cap on the metadata grace window in `agent.wait` can cause intermittent loss of `stopReason` and `pendingToolCalls` structured metadata when a run's post-lifecycle work (e.g., session updates) exceeds that timeout before the dedupe terminal snapshot is written.
5947ecb00e12a8fc: The new `timeoutMs` fetch aborts surface as `AbortError` in Node/undici rather than timeout-named errors, causing the retry logic in `runBatchWithTimeoutRetry` to skip retries and incorrectly treat slow-provider timeouts as ordinary batch failures, which will prematurely disable batch mode.
f4b7153f6f58b803: Replacing the shallow spread `{ ...base }` with `structuredClone(base)` causes `DataCloneError` exceptions for valid but non-serializable JS objects (e.g., `URL`, `Headers`, or functions) passed as cron job input, turning what should be clean validation errors into unhandled handler exceptions.
b2c5dc585343bff9: The pre-poll value of `remainingMs` is passed to `readOpenAiBatchError` without being recomputed after the status poll completes, allowing the error-file download to block for nearly a full extra timeout window on failure paths instead of respecting the overall batch operation deadline.
49a4ddf06a8f1715: Each HTTP poll passes the original full timeout value instead of the remaining deadline, allowing the total batch operation to significantly exceed the configured maximum wait time.
672bb3b09251716d: The non-interactive `github-copilot` auth branch fails to set a default model (e.g., `github-copilot/gpt-4o`), causing model resolution to fall back to Anthropic defaults that don't use the Copilot token, resulting in immediate auth failures despite a seemingly successful onboard.
8370491dd63e46c1: Removing `formatMentionForText` (and `buildMentionedMessage`) from `mention.ts` breaks the Feishu package entrypoint because `index.ts` still re-exports both symbols, causing a missing-export module linking error before any runtime code can execute.
cbdef7ad524fb7a9: The GitHub Copilot token is only sanitized with `trim()` instead of the `normalizeSecretInput` function used by other auth paths, meaning embedded CR/LF characters can survive and get persisted to the profile, causing hard-to-diagnose authentication failures.
ec694948fe4ae22b: When `wrapStreamFnSanitizeMalformedToolCalls` removes an assistant message entirely, it forwards the rewritten history to the provider without re-running turn validators, potentially transforming the original malformed tool call error into a different provider-side rejection due to consecutive user turns violating Anthropic's alternating-turn requirement.
237872b5624e0254: The replay path accepts any non-blank, whitespace-free tool call name without validating it against the current tool schema allowlist, allowing unavailable or unknown tool names to be forwarded to strict provider replayers and potentially causing request rejections, while diverging from the existing sanitization policy enforced elsewhere in the codebase.
63f4894a8fb2f45d: The new replay sanitizer incorrectly calls `resolveStructuredAllowedToolName()` after `normalizeToolCallNameForDispatch()`, undoing the fail-closed behavior for ambiguous provider-mangled tool names and allowing replayed tool calls to be rewritten to the wrong concrete tool when multiple candidates exist.
6be1bdcedb100db9: When `preserveErroredAssistantResults` moves a displaced tool result forward via `pushToolResult()`, it fails to set the transcript-changed flag, causing `repairToolUseResultPairing()` to return the original unrepaired message array and leaving providers that enforce strict ordering to reject the still-orphaned tool result.
20179062c057c7b2: The new ContentClaw plugin entry in the community docs omits the required `npm install -g contentclaw` prerequisite step, which will cause first-time users to encounter immediate tool handler failures and mistakenly believe the plugin is broken after installation.
77f0441bba3862e3: The README falsely labels the community-built "OpenClaw Easy" desktop app as "Official," contradicting the project's own documentation and misleading users into treating an unsupported, third-party wrapper as the security-reviewed, first-party GUI client.
d4aabf9616470062: The draft's CONFIRM payload examples are incomplete — one is truncated and another omits required fields (`confirm_id` and `timestamp`) — meaning developers copying the "minimal JSON" examples will produce malformed confirms that can't be correlated to receipts or used to anchor TTL start times.
d9a3c85a7618416e: The documentation defines two inconsistent type string casings ("confirm" vs "CONFIRM") for the same primitive, which would cause runtimes dispatching on literal event-type strings to treat them as different, non-interoperable protocol variants.
23be682db50e277f: The `scope` field in the CONFIRM example is inconsistently typed as a bare string `"purchase"` instead of the structured object defined elsewhere in the spec, creating two incompatible wire shapes for the same field and losing the workflow/step binding that the rest of the document depends on.
d0a8d1d5e03ba5ff: When `stop_scope="step"` is specified, `step_id` is only recommended rather than required, allowing a producer to emit a valid step-scoped STOP without identifying the target step, which creates ambiguity that could cause runtimes to halt the wrong step or the entire chain.
84d8d8ba7f60d12d: The protocol inconsistently defines takeover handling by specifying a `STOP` wire event with an optional `takeover_mode` field but separately listing an `on_takeover` callback with no normative rule connecting the two, leaving runtimes free to implement divergent behaviors that undermine the goal of stable hook points.
ed94f6fa1be9f7e0: The `toolsExpanded` flag is derived solely from `activeSession?.verboseLevel`, which only reflects persisted overrides and not inherited defaults (e.g., `agents.defaults.verboseDefault = "full"`), because `loadChatHistory()` drops the resolved verbose level returned by the backend, causing tool cards to incorrectly render as collapsed for sessions relying on inherited configuration.
d4b2d14375ac58c3: The `toolsExpanded` fallback in `renderChat()` uses only the global `verboseDefault` rather than respecting agent-specific verbose configuration, which can cause tool cards to incorrectly render as expanded even when the agent ran with verbose mode off.
779677918e657918: The new `$value` download branch calls `res.arrayBuffer()` and buffers the entire response in memory before checking `maxBytes`, bypassing the streaming size-limit guards in `fetchRemoteMedia` and risking memory spikes or crashes on oversized hosted content.
5b1b243757f3ab08: Replacing `setImmediate` with a hard-coded 25ms `setTimeout` is an unreliable synchronization strategy for asserting asynchronous media upsert behavior, as it can still cause flaky tests on slower runners where the debounced async processing takes longer than the fixed delay.
252d320881cec383: The updated audit finding misleadingly suggests that mention gating reduces risk in open LINE rooms with elevated tools enabled, when in fact `resolveMentionGatingWithBypass()` only enforces the gate when `canDetectMention` is true and LINE's handler bypasses it for non-text messages and authorized control commands, leaving the prompt injection risk materially understated.
d7188e7226eee6b2: Adding the overly broad `trafficmanager.net` suffix to the bearer-token auth allowlist allows any attacker-controlled `*.trafficmanager.net` subdomain to exfiltrate the bot's Authorization token by triggering a 401/403 challenge on an allowlisted request.
df0e8e3c8a4e4e56: The new `hasInlineImages` gate removes the previous `onlyHtmlAttachments` Graph fallback, causing messages whose media is referenced via HTML attachment tags or non-`` markup to silently return no media instead of attempting the Graph attachment download path.
462bd4d7c0448ce5: Changing the default value of `supportsUsageInStreaming` from `false` to `true` in `normalizeModelCompat()` bypasses the intentional provider-level gating in `applyNativeStreamingUsageCompat()`, causing non-native Moonshot/ModelStudio proxy endpoints to incorrectly receive `stream_options.include_usage` and potentially break requests on proxy backends that don't support it.
1981d1908d15b5af: The `findNearestPluginSdkPackageRoot` fallback to `process.cwd()` no longer validates that the discovered package is an actual OpenClaw root, allowing any ancestor `package.json` that exports `./plugin-sdk/*` to poison `createJiti()` with alias entries from an unrelated workspace, causing `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` imports to resolve to the wrong repo's files.
d56056725ef05a0f: When OpenClaw is launched via a transpiler cache from a different working directory without an explicit `argv1` parameter, `resolveLoaderPluginSdkPackageRoot` fails to fall back to `argv1`-based resolution, causing `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths to go unresolved and breaking plugins that import modules like `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-runtime`.
16ba23fb70e1fda0: `RunnerManager.launch()` calls `createRunner()` synchronously on the main Node thread, meaning the full model load and warmup (up to ~30 seconds) blocks the event loop and can stall unrelated services during cold ExecuTorch startup.
a2e4adc32523fa25: The regex-based source rewrite in `root-alias.cjs` incorrectly mutates non-import content such as template strings and comments that match `import "./x.js"` or `from "./x.js"` patterns, which can silently corrupt runtime string data and should instead be limited to parsed import/export specifier nodes.
4733ed8239919460: The remediation command hardcodes `openclaw config set tools.fs.workspaceOnly true` instead of using `formatCliCommand()`, so users auditing a non-default profile will accidentally update the default config rather than the risky profile they intended to fix.
57841f1bd7594cea: The `rewriteLocalSourceJsSpecifiers` function uses broad regex-based text replacement to rewrite `.js` import specifiers, which risks incorrectly modifying non-specifier content like string literals, comments, or template snippets that happen to match import-like patterns, and should instead operate on parsed import/export AST nodes.
fa4f8e3a80c30e9a: The new `read_config_gateway_token` implementation runs `openclaw-cli` via Docker Compose before volume mounts and image builds are configured, preventing it from accessing the host config file and causing a new gateway token to be generated on every rerun when `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` is unset, which can unexpectedly invalidate existing clients.
763cd9cb4541594b: In isolated mode, `migrate_legacy_host_data` is called before the Docker image is built or pulled, causing the setup script to exit fatally on `docker compose run` if the image doesn't yet exist, preventing setup from completing whenever legacy host config requires migration.
89d9ff798b591dac: With `set -u` enforcing strict unbound variable checks, internal variables like `DOCKER_SOCKET_PATH` are dereferenced before being assigned defaults, causing the script to immediately abort and making the entire Docker setup entrypoint unusable.
86d29cb527514e43: When isolated mode is enabled, `OPENCLAW_HOME_VOLUME` is written directly into Docker Compose volume name fields without validation, so a bind-style path like `/srv/openclaw-home` will be treated as a volume name and cause setup to fail after the costly image build/pull step.
2a647ad1205b6361: The Zalo Personal link in the zh-CN channel index contains a duplicated locale prefix (`/zh-CN/zh-CN/channels/zalouser`) that causes it to resolve to a missing page instead of the correct translated page at `/zh-CN/channels/zalouser`.
9aa6add4a95538c1: The regression test for `resolveSubagentCompletionOrigin` is never executed by CI because the test runner only discovers `*.test.ts`, `*.live.test.ts`, and `*.e2e.test.ts` files, leaving the `.mjs` test file completely ignored and providing no actual protection against regressions.
cef38052420137d3: The new `fetchLatestWhatsAppWebVersionViaProxy` fallback omits the required anti-bot headers (e.g., `User-Agent`, `sec-fetch-site`) that Baileys includes when fetching `sw.js`, causing WhatsApp to reject the request and silently fall back to the stale bundled version, meaning the login failures this change aims to fix will still occur.
914e75904028ae04: The proxy setup in `createWaSocket()` unconditionally routes all WhatsApp traffic through the proxy whenever an HTTPS proxy environment variable is set, ignoring `NO_PROXY`/`no_proxy` exclusion rules and breaking deployments that intentionally bypass the proxy for WhatsApp hosts.
3502d2aa7cb9acb5: The patch fails to populate `options.dispatcher` in the Baileys socket config, causing media downloads and other fetch-based requests (e.g., `downloadMediaMessage` via `getHttpStream`) to bypass the proxy and fail in firewall-restricted environments, even though `fetchAgent` is set.
365201763e3cdc19: The `authenticateGovdossApiKey` function grants full operator-level access to any caller who provides a non-empty API key string without validating it against a store of legitimate keys, allowing an attacker to forge arbitrary credentials and gain unauthorized access.
3d728e5fac16759b: When `HTTPS_PROXY` is set, `fetchAgent` is incorrectly assigned an undici `EnvHttpProxyAgent` dispatcher instead of a Node `https.Agent`, causing media uploads (images, documents, audio) to fail because Baileys' upload path passes `fetchAgent` directly into `http[s].request({ agent })`, which expects a native Node agent.
e04529de4fe43456: The password reuse check only validates against password history but not the current password hash, allowing users to "change" their password to the same value and bypass rotation requirements.
448f3a057526e352: The gateway guard generates an `approvalId` and continuation for approval-required requests but never persists them to `govdossApprovalStore` or the paused executor registry, making every generated approval permanently unresolvable by the `/approve`, `/reject`, and `/resume` endpoints.
2438bba0c2cf8d34: Changing `combinedMedia` to only use the last entry's media instead of flattening all entries causes silent data loss when multiple forwarded media messages are flushed together from the debounce buffer.
aa3d8c024ca6f7c0: On macOS/BSD, `sed -i` requires an explicit backup suffix and will exit non-zero, causing the script to abort prematurely under `set -e` before any dist files are patched, breaking the skill on macOS despite it being documented as a general-purpose workflow.
e7c1258838b1b06c: The script overwrites Alibaba's documented 1M context window values for `qwen3.5-plus`, `qwen3-coder-plus`, and `MiniMax-M2.5` with smaller limits (262144/204800), which actually reduces the available prompt budget and causes long-context chats to compact or truncate earlier than intended.
bb84f7d269d7fd05: The streaming-usage guard checks the entire config file for `supportsUsageInStreaming` instead of scoping the check to Bailian models specifically, which can cause the patch step to be incorrectly skipped, leaving other Bailian models without the required compatibility flag.
209d1ce16b736480: Removing `matrixSetupWizard` and `matrixSetupAdapter` from the plugin-sdk matrix module breaks existing public API contracts, causing import failures for any plugins or tooling compiled against the current published entrypoints.
2ea527207472192d: Making the Fly entrypoint script executable and implicitly the default for all container launches breaks rootless Podman deployments, because the entrypoint attempts to write to paths like `/data`, `/root`, and `/usr/local/bin` that a non-root user lacks permission to modify, causing the container to exit before the application ever starts.
6abcea148baceeac: When only `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set, the provider availability logic incorrectly recognizes only `openai-codex` and not `openai`, causing the startup script to silently overwrite intentionally pinned `openai` models with `openai-codex` defaults in live deployments.
96214ca7205afb3f: Changing the SMS permission check from `&&` to `||` causes the onboarding flow to incorrectly treat SMS as fully granted when only one of `SEND_SMS` or `READ_SMS` is granted, permanently blocking the user from being prompted for the missing permission.
d10c1f8676a85d71: The onboarding UI now advertises SMS search functionality and treats READ_SMS as granted in its toggle logic, but the onboarding flow never actually requests the READ_SMS permission, leaving users who complete onboarding believing SMS search is enabled when it remains unavailable until they separately grant the permission elsewhere.
f1688adfcdcadd49: When one SMS permission is already granted and the other is newly granted, `smsPermissionGranted` fails to trigger a state transition (remaining `true` throughout), so the SMS settings row can remain stuck showing the stale "Grant" UI until an unrelated recomposition occurs.
4168052f796a6fbd: Changing the SMS permission check from AND to OR causes onboarding to incorrectly mark SMS as fully granted when only one of the two required permissions (SEND_SMS or READ_SMS) has been granted, allowing users to skip the flow without ever requesting the missing permission.
aeb5e898c2e728fe: When `READ_CONTACTS` permission is absent and a `contactName` is provided alongside a formatting-only `phoneNumber` (e.g., `"()"`), the new fallback branch accepts the number, which normalizes to an empty string and causes the SMS query to match every thread, leaking messages from all conversations.
42116bfa7d2843de: When `includeMms=true`, `querySmsMmsMessagesByPhone` loads the entire conversation history into memory before applying pagination, potentially materializing thousands of rows for a request that only needs a small number of results, unlike the SMS-only path which correctly pushes `LIMIT/OFFSET` down to the content provider.
1edff81c7fec520d: The `extractRemoteConfigGetPayload` function fails to unwrap the nested config snapshot returned by `config.get`, causing `resolveNodeCommandAllowlist()` to receive the entire snapshot object instead of the actual config, so `cfg.gateway?.nodes` is always undefined and command allow/deny lists silently fall back to platform defaults in remote live-test runs.
030387798dbc80fa: Moving SMS/call-log feature flags into `defaultConfig` removes the `play`/`thirdParty` product flavor variants that existing build scripts, test commands, and release pipelines still depend on, breaking all documented Android build and release workflows.
59c3a6f1168f74a8: The `querySmsMmsMessagesByPhone()` function omits the `status` column from its projection, causing all SMS messages in mixed by-phone searches to lose their real delivery state (pending/failed/delivered) and always report a default status of 0.
b781198239743f86: When `includeMms=true`, the new by-phone MMS search path buffers up to `offset + limit` messages in memory with no upper bound on offset, allowing a caller to trigger an OOM or device hang by requesting a large page offset on a long message thread.
30d635262e34a617: The `conversationReview` flag is only honored when an explicit `phoneNumber` is provided, but not when a `contactName` search resolves to a single number, causing older MMS rows to be silently omitted in the latter case despite identical intent.
c3743847ff93807f: Hard-coding SMS and call log feature flags in `defaultConfig` eliminates the Play-flavor permission stripping mechanism, meaning the Google Play Store build artifact will now include restricted permissions (`SEND_SMS`, `READ_SMS`, `READ_CALL_LOG`) that violate store safety requirements.
47a991d871d664bf: `loadConfig()` is called unconditionally before the remote/local branch check in `resolvePolicyConfigForRun()`, meaning a stale or invalid local config on the test runner will throw an error and abort live remote Android capability runs before they can fetch the policy config from the remote gateway.
2406a5f0d6940a1c: The `conversationReview` flag is effectively a no-op in the mixed MMS query path because it is only used to raise the minimum `limit` to 25 in `effectiveSearchParams()` but never causes `querySmsMessages()` or `querySmsMmsMessagesByPhone()` to perform a broader scan, meaning older MMS rows that the feature is intended to surface will still be omitted.
9e23ba2929c64d28: The regex `\bgot status:\s*internal\b` is too broad and will incorrectly match human-readable HTTP 500 messages like "got status: Internal Server Error", causing those errors to be misclassified as timeout/failover events instead of preserving their original error text.
ce50f60c9a779da1: The `sms.search` command is only declared to the gateway when `READ_SMS` is already granted, so on a fresh install the gateway rejects the command before dispatch ever reaches the new permission-request flow, leaving the intended first-use `READ_SMS` prompt permanently unreachable.
df5901c9b19816cb: Although `loadFreshConfig` is wired in, `resolveTelegramGroupConfig` still closes over the stale startup config snapshot, meaning runtime edits to direct/topic overrides (e.g., `dmPolicy`, `allowFrom`, `requireTopic`, `agentId`) are never picked up without a full bot restart.
e0ca0905bf8b986a: The unconditional `loadFreshConfig()` call during Telegram message context construction can throw if the config file is temporarily unreadable (e.g., mid-edit), causing all inbound messages to fail instead of gracefully falling back to the already-loaded startup configuration.
4ece1f5fca1b2a88: The unconditional call to `telegramDeps.loadConfig()` on every command invocation means a transient config file error will cause all bot commands to throw and drop replies instead of gracefully falling back to the valid startup configuration snapshot.
693748eb82346a87: Replacing `/Query` with `/Query /FO LIST` in `assertSchtasksAvailable()` can cause false "schtasks unavailable" failures on Windows hosts with large task inventories, because the full listing output may exceed Node's default `execFile` buffer limit.
dcc81d725f209425: Within the same `modelCallback` handler, `buildModelsProviderData` is called with the reloaded `runtimeCfg` while other operations like `formatModelsAvailableHeader`, `resolveStorePath`, and `resolveDefaultModelForAgent` still use the stale startup `cfg`, causing a config snapshot mismatch that can display updated model options in the UI but persist the selection to the wrong store or compare against an outdated default model.
327ea8ac4924fb62: Changing the availability probe to `/Query /FO LIST` causes it to buffer the full verbose task listing, which can exceed Node's default `execFile` `maxBuffer` on hosts with many scheduled tasks, incorrectly making `schtasks` appear unavailable.
87cd2575ed1574c7: The fallback to `accountConfig.allowFrom` is only wired into the monitor's allow check but not the rest of the Matrix plugin, so users who set only the top-level key will be silently allowed through monitoring while status reporting, doctor, and onboarding still treat the allowlist as empty and may overwrite it with a separate `dm.allowFrom` list.
83983ae2ca1d5011: The test for the "already pending request" case no longer mocks `upsertChannelPairingRequest` to return `created: false`, so the pending-request branch that suppresses duplicate pairing replies is never exercised and the `expectedSendCount: 1` assertion is invalid.
8cf297e7aa64b2d9: The approval gate for Discord-originated turns can be bypassed because `inputProvenance.sourceChannel` is sourced from untrusted caller-supplied request JSON rather than verified session or connection state, allowing any client to spoof a Discord origin and reach the restricted cross-channel approval path.
a9aa20545719a141: The new `denyCommands` whitespace validation rejects entries with harmless leading/trailing spaces, breaking existing configs that the runtime already handles correctly via `trim()`, and should trim entries before validating instead of failing outright.
90b1c2eb8e7bd55c: The `COPILOT_XHIGH_MODEL_IDS` allowlist was updated to include `gpt-5.4` but omits `gpt-5.4-mini`, meaning users on the `github-copilot/gpt-5.4-mini` model will incorrectly be denied xhigh thinking support despite being on the same GPT-5.4 rollout.
5ff591e870c80fc2: When a stop-then-start sequence occurs during an in-flight startup, the new `startChannel()` call silently waits for the aborted startup promise and exits without ever re-invoking `startAccount()`, causing the channel to remain permanently stopped.
3d43745fd4a561d9: The LSP `initialize` request sends `rootUri: null` instead of the session's `workspaceDir`, causing project-aware language servers like tsserver, pyright, and rust-analyzer to start without a workspace root, which will result in incomplete or broken hover, definition, and references for files in the repo.
4111a6c20f206e36: When ACP inline-delivery spawns a child run without populating `inputProvenance`, the `turnSourceChannel` fallback incorrectly coerces the origin to `webchat` instead of preserving the true channel (e.g. Discord), causing exec approval handlers that check the source channel to reject approvals for those spawned runs entirely.
c5eb888c97febb12: The LSP message parser incorrectly uses `Content-Length` (a byte count) as a JavaScript string slice index, causing JSON parse failures and permanent buffer misalignment whenever server responses contain non-ASCII characters.
4c9886bac696b902: The fallback markdown tool-call extractor fails to apply `allowedToolNames` filtering in the additional-pattern loop, allowing arbitrary JSON objects and `<tool_call>` blocks to be incorrectly promoted to tool calls even when no matching configured tool exists.
18aec0710cfac4cb: The `text_end` event is emitted before fallback tool JSON is stripped from `message.content`, causing raw JSON tool call syntax to be flushed to the user before the tool runs, even when the final event is correctly rewritten to `toolUse`.
482af0c97068713c: The context window cache uses only the model name as its key, so querying the same model name from two different Ollama server instances can return a stale, incorrect context window size for up to 5 minutes, potentially causing invalid requests to be sent to the second server.
81892e028faa9e5e: When a mid-stream failure triggers a retry of the `/api/chat` request, `accumulatedContent` and `accumulatedToolCalls` are never reset, causing the successful retry's full response to be appended to the partial failed stream and resulting in duplicated text and tool calls.
9dff02d0eb4522ab: Chunks arriving within the throttle delay window are accumulated but never emitted as `text_delta` events, causing the `text_end` event to carry no content and potentially dropping entire short replies or the tail of longer ones in `blockReplyBreak: "text_end"` flows.
bf82db8b0cec125c: When passing images to vision-capable embedded models, each image is sent twice because the prompt still contains `[media attached: ...]` lines that aren't suppressed on the "skipped" path, and `detectAndLoadPromptImages` appends image references from the prompt to the already-populated `existingImages` without deduplication, doubling token usage and risking per-request image limits.
5c9114d7d42b3e61: A wildcard allowlist entry like `"*"` is stripped by `normalizeHostnameAllowlist`, producing an empty array that the updated `matchesHostnameAllowlist` now treats as deny-all instead of allow-all, causing all hostname checks to fail for configs that relied on `*` to disable host filtering.
ce01ef623121b3ce: The new vision branch fails to call the cleanup function returned by `MediaAttachmentCache.getPath()`, causing a temp file leak under `/tmp` for every URL-backed image attachment processed on each vision turn until the process restarts.
382fc8419ec2261b: The bare-JSON and `<tool_call>` XML-wrapper regexes in `extractMarkdownToolCalls` both match the same response pattern, causing duplicate tool call entries that result in the tool executing twice downstream in `buildAssistantMessage()`.
91ebdf5c30e686db: The vision support check uses the default model to decide whether to populate image attachments before model overrides are applied, meaning the final model that actually runs may be mismatched — either missing images it supports or receiving images it doesn't support.
ef15032f0c3075e7: The normalized "ok" status is only applied to persisted job state but not propagated to emitted "finished" events, causing operators to still receive erroneous "error" notifications and run-log entries in the delivered-but-follow-up-error case the patch intends to fix.
3c0ba3c6eae59d74: The patch inconsistently applies the normalized `effectiveStatus` to the one-shot job path but leaves the recurring-job backoff logic (lines 420–447) still checking the raw `result.status`, causing recurring jobs to be incorrectly delayed by error backoff even when the run should be treated as successful.
194b43c4e8a60f89: The `getProviderLabel()` function only lowercases provider IDs before lookup but lacks entries for real canonical IDs like `azure-openai-responses` and `openai-codex`, causing those providers to render as raw slugs in the UI instead of user-friendly group names.
b94ecc2e282f85b4: Removing `overflow-x: auto` from `.data-table-container` will cause the sessions table's rightmost columns and action controls to be clipped and unreachable on smaller viewports, since the parent layout already hides horizontal overflow.
0f21dd7f51031132: In QMD deployments, `memory_get` incorrectly uses a status-only manager whose `readFile()` always throws, breaking all memory file retrieval for that backend.
7898b7640d99054c: The new `optionKeys.has(trimmedModel)` branch incorrectly strips the wrapper provider (e.g., "openrouter") from hierarchical model IDs like "anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5" when the catalog contains a matching direct provider key, causing the wrong provider to be submitted in environments where both a wrapper and underlying provider are configured.
7e3e69450c5de7ef: The skill parse warning logs the file path and error message as structured metadata rather than inline in the message string, so in non-TTY compact logging mode operators will only see the generic warning text with no actionable details about which skill was dropped or why.
df951cc27985fbe5: The session picker hard-codes "main" as the primary session key instead of reading the configured `mainSessionKey` from `OpenClawChatSessionsDefaults`, which will cause incorrect session switching and status display for any install that sets a custom main session key.
a752113671981bf9: The new `mediaMaxMb` config limit is effectively unreachable for WebSocket callers because base64-encoded attachments expand by ~33%, causing any value above ~18 MiB to exceed the 25 MiB WebSocket frame cap and get dropped before the attachment parsing logic ever runs.
895c67a47559aeda: The edit tool's description unconditionally advertises `apply_patch` as a fallback, but `apply_patch` is only added to the tool list under specific conditions (feature flag enabled and OpenAI provider/model), meaning non-OpenAI and default sessions will have the model instructed to use a tool that doesn't exist, likely causing infinite retry loops.
e331d0da77626c32: The fallback order for channel identity incorrectly prioritizes `Surface` over `Provider`, causing replies relayed through the web UI to be misrouted as internal webchat traffic instead of being attributed to the true source channel (e.g., Feishu).
c76ab5f8744feff0: Switching to `listEnabledFeishuAccountConfigs()` can incorrectly suppress `feishu_chat` tool registration in multi-account setups where an earlier (alphabetically) account has `tools.chat: false`, because the first-account chat check now sees a non-chat-enabled account that was previously filtered out, hiding the tool even when a later account has chat enabled.
a0ae3936d76c8167: Switching `queueBodyBase` from `bodyWithEvents` to `effectiveBaseBody` drops prepended system events (e.g., model-switch, node-connected notices) from deferred/queued follow-up prompts in followup/collect/steer modes, causing queued turns to run with less context than immediate turns.
72e70eaae1713b69: A missing `executeLocal` dispatch check causes slash commands like `/clear` and `/model` to be sent as literal chat text instead of being handled locally, breaking history reset and session patching functionality.
1df5f3931de51f63: Removing `senderLabel` from the grouping key causes consecutive messages from different users to be merged into a single `MessageGroup` and incorrectly attributed to "You", misidentifying who sent what in shared or imported chats.
b8ddac3eca504f19: The `Promise.race`-based timeout in `resolveScriptTagline` cannot interrupt synchronous blocking work in the imported script, because the event loop is blocked and the timer callback never fires, potentially hanging the CLI indefinitely.
6eca37c7c66aa8ab: The delivery log incorrectly records the send mode as "reply" based solely on whether `sendReplyToMessageId` is set, even when the underlying Feishu API silently falls back to sending a top-level message due to the target being withdrawn or deleted, making reply-target failures harder to diagnose.
c951a594f56bb03f: The `hasHelpFlag` function incorrectly identifies `-h`/`--help` as a help intent even when these strings appear as option values rather than flags, which can cause false positives that trigger unnecessary script resolution and leak output in piped workflows.
