ba1f90d2e31b4a70: The example implementation hardcodes `DISCOVERY_PLATFORM_URL` to a placeholder value instead of reading it from environment configuration, causing service registration to fail at the wrong URL unless users manually edit the source code.
19f14ee3de8cd5fe: The root-help fast path uses `emitCliBanner` to prime the script tagline cache, but since `emitCliBanner` exits early in non-TTY environments, piped or CI help output will silently render an empty tagline instead of the expected text.
0b7bc6f1e43c6022: The new `emitCliBanner` priming call in `runCli` is ineffective for the most common `--help` path because `src/entry.ts` short-circuits root help before `runCli` is ever reached, leaving the script tagline still rendering as empty in practice.
8a6c6a08385f7119: The new `!raw.startsWith("![")` guard is overly broad and incorrectly routes valid shell commands like `![ -f .env ] && echo yes` to chat instead of the local shell handler, breaking common `[`/`test` syntax for TUI operators.
2bbef68bf891908c: When `replyToMode` is `first`, Slack channel IDs matched case-insensitively by the auto-thread resolver can populate `threadId` for `[[reply_to_current]]`, but the `hasRepliedRef` flag in the action runtime only flips on an exact-case match, so the first-reply budget is never consumed and subsequent sends in the same turn incorrectly auto-thread again.
0e9e5959cb5d5dc8: The new `!command.commandBodyNormalized.startsWith("![")` guard intended to exclude Markdown image syntax is overly broad and incorrectly blocks valid bash commands like `![ -f package.json ] && echo ok`, breaking the documented `!` alias for shell `[`/`test` expressions.
19130bb470d00a66: Writing `runningAtMs = undefined` and `lastStatus = "error"` before `applyJobResult` completes creates a race window where a process crash can leave a timed-out job in a state that bypasses backoff logic and allows it to be immediately re-queued or re-executed on restart, skipping intended delay or disable behavior.
77840fee4e8cfff9: The `normalizeImagePayloadForModel` function fails to re-validate attachment size after converting HEIC images to JPEG, allowing transcoded images that exceed the configured size limit to pass through to the model pipeline.
d3109b6e5226fc6e: When `usePty` is true on Windows, the `windowStyle` option is lost because the PTY code path returns early with only `ptyCommand` and the supervisor later reconstructs the shell with a plain `getShellConfig()` call, ignoring the user-configured `tools.exec.windowStyle` setting.
3daec80295a24c88: The owner allowlist cache is keyed only on the raw array reference and plugin/account/provider IDs, so hot-swapping a plugin with a new `formatAllowFrom` implementation but the same plugin ID will silently reuse stale normalized owners and grant or deny commands incorrectly until the `ownerAllowFrom` array reference changes.
4b2bcd8b3a1efd28: `resolveNodeCommandAllowlist` is incorrectly used to validate `denyCommands` entries, causing legitimate deny rules for valid but non-allowlisted commands (e.g., `camera.snap`, `screen.record`) to be falsely flagged as unknown, undermining operator attempts to harden node access.
9a11d92316bdc56e: The fallback guard only suppresses duplicate final text replies for routed turns, causing normal ACP turns to always send a redundant final text reply after streamed block replies when TTS yields no media, resulting in duplicate output for users.
99732069c7f9dc2f: When deriving the REST base URL, using only `parsed.hostname` drops any non-default port from `apiUrl`, causing REST calls to target the wrong origin and potentially leaking bearer tokens to an unintended service.
00c94edc7b3b25d0: When `resolveTelegramReplyId()` fails to parse an explicitly supplied reply target ID, the new `?? params.defaultReplyToId` fallback silently redirects the reply to the current inbound message instead of leaving it unthreaded, causing the bot to reply to the wrong message.
af58b05e6685256d: Updating `lastSeen` before reply generation or message sending succeeds causes unread DMs to be silently dropped on transient failures, since the next poll incorrectly treats the conversation as already handled.
8ad0fd5ecaa0f64f: The poll loop fails to recheck the `aborted` flag after `client.getChats()` resolves, allowing message handling and outbound replies to continue executing even after shutdown, causing unexpected side effects during restarts or gateway shutdowns.
53a2000263ac7641: The `fetchJson` function in `warroom.ts` makes HTTP requests to SCid and Ollama without an `AbortSignal` timeout, meaning a stalled upstream service can cause the requests to hang indefinitely and block agent turns instead of failing fast.
3a29ac3100d826f1: The poll loop fails to re-check the `aborted` flag after `await generateReply(...)` completes, allowing an in-flight reply to be sent via `client.sendMessage(...)` even after a restart or shutdown has been triggered.
028e1003c47ced90: The `autoFollowAll` task does not check the `aborted` flag during its async flow, causing it to continue issuing `followUser` mutations even after the channel has been shut down, resulting in unintended post-stop account actions.
b3fbaeca52f733e1: The `safeFetch` function is vulnerable to DNS rebinding SSRF attacks because it validates a URL's resolved IP in a separate DNS lookup from the actual `fetch()` call, allowing an attacker-controlled domain with a short TTL to pass the blocklist check but resolve to a private/internal IP during the real request.
2a4882c0dd837925: The `soundchain_platform_docs` tool fetches from `https://soundchain.io/skill.md` without an `AbortSignal` timeout, meaning a stalled connection can cause the tool call to hang indefinitely and block the invoking turn.
874e35e7a4100a31: The `maybeAttachGif` function fetches from Giphy without an `AbortSignal` timeout, causing the entire DM response to hang if the request stalls, which can back up polling responsiveness under intermittent network failures.
c702cbd7c88a1d50: Setting `replyInThread` solely from `Boolean(threadId)` ignores explicit `replyToId` values, causing messages that should be inline replies to a specific message to instead be incorrectly re-threaded as topic replies.
7e2365db5df14011: When `ensureApiKeyFromOptionEnvOrPrompt` returns a `SecretRef` object instead of a plain string, `buildFoundryProviderConfig` skips the Azure API-key auth wiring (the `authHeader: false` flag and `api-key` header), causing runtime requests to fall back to bearer-style auth and fail with 401/403 errors for ref-backed credentials.
7b0a211254de7956: In the manual Entra fallback, `modelNameHint` is always set to the deployment ID instead of the actual model name, causing `resolveFoundryApi` to misroute GPT-5 deployments with custom names (e.g., `prod-chat`) to the wrong endpoint family.
084ca151618c8f62: Including `AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT` in `providerAuthEnvVars` causes `resolveEnvApiKey()` to incorrectly treat the endpoint URL as a valid API key, bypassing auth checks and leading to confusing 401/403 failures at runtime.
0f1129d5c0ac162e: The fuzzy path resolution fallback incorrectly initializes the filesystem walk with an empty root on POSIX systems because splitting an absolute path like "/tmp/foo" on `path.sep` yields an empty first segment, causing the walk to resolve against `process.cwd()` instead of the filesystem root and leaving normalized QMD paths unresolvable on Linux/macOS.
b01f063bcf3bcf82: The `resolveLlmLogFile` function calls `loadConfig()`, which throws on invalid configurations, causing `openclaw logs` to fail for `--source llm` and `--source all` precisely when users need log access most — during troubleshooting after a broken config edit.
76900deadb6c6178: Third-party bundles can exploit the new SSE transport branch to trigger arbitrary outbound HTTP connections simply by including a `{ "url": ... }` entry in their `.mcp.json`, bypassing the existing stdio-only trust boundary without requiring any explicit owner opt-in.
03e480c300534e28: Passing `envelopeOptions` into inbound metadata formatting causes `Conversation info.timestamp` to be unintentionally suppressed when users set `envelopeTimestamp: "off"`, a setting meant only to control outbound message envelope headers, not inbound conversation timing context in prompts.
05aafdab80ac38ae: The bundle MCP inspection logic still flags any non-`command`-based server as unsupported, so SSE/URL-backed bundles will be incorrectly reported as having an unsupported transport in warnings, status output, and CLI inspection even when they are functioning correctly.
471c0c771da90fc7: Removing `!hasRequestScopeClient` from the condition allows the fallback model-override authorization path to execute even when a real gateway client is present, enabling non-admin, request-scoped callers to bypass normal override restrictions via plugin trust settings.
eeffd78e5eb1fc26: Newly forwarded fields (`clientTools`, `disableTools`, `streamParams`) in the agent RPC payload will cause runtime validation failures because the backend `AgentParamsSchema` uses `additionalProperties: false` and doesn't recognize them, silently breaking all structured subagent runs.
24b0a82626a9cd63: Unlike `subagent.run()`, which rejects unauthorized model override requests, `subagent.enqueue()` silently strips the `provider`/`model` params and dispatches the job anyway, creating an inconsistent auth enforcement gap that can queue runs against an unintended default model without surfacing an error.
601aa85d73f54b1d: The newly added `reasoningDefault` config field is never surfaced in `session_status` output because `buildStatusMessage()` isn't passed a `resolvedReasoning` value and the status fallback path still hardcodes `reasoningLevel ?? "off"`, causing the status display to incorrectly report reasoning as off even when `reasoningDefault` is set to `"on"` or `"stream"`.
bc4e8c1610fe1a33: The mail button callbacks (e.g., `mb:archive:<threadId>`) are never routed to a real handler, so clicking Archive, Reply, or Delete on Telegram silently falls through to the generic message path and sends the raw callback string as a text message instead of performing the intended Gmail action.
4ba7fe7f5e539bd3: When a transparent wrapper command (e.g., `nice`, `nohup`) is unwrapped recursively, the `allow-always` decision gets stored against the inner executable rather than the outer wrapper, causing approval reuse to break for subsequent invocations that still present the wrapper as the first token.
aef706ebe0829d14: Calling `clearPluginCommands()` at the start of each reload now wipes the process-global command registry immediately, causing any plugin commands that arrive during the rebuild—or after a partially failed reload—to go unmatched until the new registry is fully populated.
2c16584367737476: When the same environment variable key exists in both `sandboxEnv` and `skillEnv`, the intended skill-specific value is silently overwritten by the sandbox default because `sandboxEnv` is merged after `skillEnv` in `buildSandboxEnv()`, causing sandboxed execution to diverge from gateway execution and potentially use the wrong credential.
31aca188dc9065e2: When `agentCfg.reasoningDefault` is set to `"off"`, it is not treated as an explicit override, so `resolveDefaultReasoningLevel()` later upgrades reasoning back to `"on"`, making it impossible to disable default reasoning on reasoning-capable models via configuration.
c745805e6509b1e7: The Arabic locale file is missing the `languages.ar` key, causing the language selector to fail to resolve the Arabic language's own name when the UI is set to Arabic, leading to a fallback or raw key display and a failing registry test.
3fe604e850479950: Unconditionally applying `tryRecoverLatin1AsUtf8` to all `filename=` header values corrupts legitimately Latin-1-encoded filenames (e.g., `Ã©.txt`) by misinterpreting their bytes as UTF-8, breaking round-trip filename fidelity for downloads.
751daf08b6646559: Replacing the `@tloncorp/api` dependency with a `file:`-relative path will break installation of the published `@openclaw/tlon` plugin for any consumer outside the monorepo, since npm will attempt to resolve the tarball at a non-existent path relative to `node_modules/@openclaw/tlon`.
74282ca34be09e09: The tray icon name `openclaw-icon` is hardcoded in the AppIndicator setup, but no corresponding icon asset is installed by the build system, meaning the tray entry will render with a missing or default icon on fresh installations.
89bbe556cfa6d2a4: The code hardcodes `openclaw-gateway.service` as the target systemd unit instead of respecting user-defined profiles or the `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT` environment variable, causing the companion app to monitor and control the wrong gateway instance in multi-profile setups.
e6580c4169d1ec5c: The abort cleanup path calls `removeChatRun` using the client/idempotency key instead of the agent `runId` used by `addChatRun`, causing a key mismatch that can strand registry entries and incorrectly remap future agent events to an already-aborted chat run.
0b842cfd99dc139b: The OpenRouter media understanding provider hardcodes the global `fetch` instead of using the proxy-aware fetch utility, causing image analysis requests to bypass any configured `HTTPS_PROXY`/`HTTP_PROXY` and fail in proxy-dependent environments.
6eacd2de7000ebce: When `state_update_systemd()` increments the health generation mid-flight, a completing `gateway probe` exits early without calling `state_set_probe_in_flight(FALSE)`, permanently locking the deep-probe lane and freezing all subsequent diagnostics until the app is restarted.
f53ff600854e2ae5: A stale health completion callback clears the `in_flight` lock before checking its generation, creating a race window where a second concurrent probe can be spawned and its slower result can overwrite fresher health data with stale degraded/running state after restarts or unit switches.
e15f24bf9cc542cb: If `systemd_init()` fails before `tray_init()` is called, the error state is silently dropped and the tray menu permanently displays "Status: Unknown" instead of the expected "User Systemd Unavailable" message.
80b192904726c03f: When a `gateway probe` completes stale after a restart or unit switch, `health_run_deep_probe()` clears the in-flight lock but silently drops the queued eager replacement, leaving Diagnostics stuck displaying the previous probe result until the 60-second timer or a manual refresh.
82f80d096d84f32e: When launched from the Meson build directory, the relative path traversal to locate the OpenClaw CLI resolves incorrectly to `/dist/index.js` instead of the intended `<repo>/dist/index.js`, causing local dev testing to silently fall back to a wrong or missing CLI version.
4ca473e6aadd8ee4: When a newly targeted gateway unit is installed but unloaded, the old `unit_proxy` is never torn down before returning a synthesized `inactive/dead` state, allowing stale `PropertiesChanged` signals from the previous unit to corrupt tray state while Start/Stop actions already target the new unit.
5089b672d8205342: When a gateway service transitions to a stopped or uninstalled state, stale health and probe snapshots are never cleared and new samples are blocked from replacing them, causing the diagnostics window to indefinitely display misleading "healthy" results from the last active run.
72aa8c340eabfb3f: The hard-coded 62.5s disconnect retry window in `deliverWebReply()` ignores the configurable reconnect backoff from `resolveReconnectPolicy()`, meaning reply retries can be exhausted and throw errors while `monitorWebChannel()` is still sleeping between reconnect attempts, causing message loss whenever operators tune reconnect delays beyond that fixed threshold.
adac9fbb59cf878e: If a socket disconnection is detected after one or two send failures, the retry logic exhausts its standard 3-attempt window and throws before it can switch to the extended reconnect-aware retry strategy, causing message loss in a stale-socket race condition.
7bb8cfb69ef82260: The exported `gen_ai.prompt` telemetry field uses `commandBody` (the queue payload) instead of the actual prompt sent to the model, causing it to potentially include content the model never saw and omit content it did see when `diagnostics.otel.includeContent` is enabled.
b8176b85a8e15609: After a successful reconnect, in-flight retry attempts in `deliverWebReply()` are not woken from their backoff sleep because `sleepWithAbort()` only listens for terminal shutdown signals, causing up to 30+ seconds of unnecessary reply latency even though the new socket is already ready.
5da6033fcf7ccd55: The OTEL output text is captured from `replyPayloads` before later mutations (e.g., unscheduled-reminder guard, verbose notices, `appendUsageLine`) are applied, meaning the exported telemetry content will not match the actual user-visible reply when `diagnostics.otel.includeContent` is enabled.
c411568667ac12fb: When the selected unit changes generation while a health probe is in-flight, an early return fails to release the in-flight lock via `state_set_health_in_flight(FALSE)`, permanently blocking all future health probes for the session and causing the tray to silently display stale status instead of reporting degraded or config-warning states.
a52ce2f2c6537591: The `truncateToBytes()` function appends a truncation marker (e.g., `"…[truncated]"`) after already trimming to `maxBytes`, causing the final string to exceed the intended byte limit and potentially causing oversized attributes to still be rejected by collectors.
407bc38891b425b9: The global transport state only tracks a single `activeLogger`, so any child loggers (e.g. per-channel or plugin loggers) constructed before the OTEL diagnostics service starts will never have the OTLP transport late-attached, causing their logs to be silently dropped from OpenTelemetry in multi-channel and plugin setups.
39f71e88ac8f58aa: When block streaming completes, the fallback to raw `payloadArray` instead of sanitized `replyPayloads` means OTEL telemetry can capture control tokens and unsanitized directive text that was never actually shown to the user.
60e41a2f2a46ace5: When the gateway RPC send succeeds, the code exits early before `messageCommand()` can emit the success summary or `--json` payload, resulting in empty stdout and breaking the CLI contract for callers that parse or display send results.
f612487d19603122: When `includeContent` is enabled but a provider returns no usage data, the OTEL instrumentation silently skips emitting both usage and prompt/completion content spans, causing content tracing to fail invisibly for backends that don't report token counts.
eaa685839f0d1d78: Instead of recomputing `promptTokens` by summing raw `usage.input + cacheRead + cacheWrite`, the code should use `agentMeta.promptTokens` so that OTEL prompt-token spans reflect the accurate final-call prompt size rather than incorrectly accumulated totals across multiple API calls in tool-use or compaction runs.
a6228cb9576b27c4: When only total token count is known, defaulting `gen_ai.usage.input_tokens` and `gen_ai.usage.output_tokens` to `0` produces misleading span data that can skew dashboards and cost calculations, rather than omitting those split fields entirely.
ec726f59242a1f3b: The new gateway RPC send path silently drops `--thread-id` and `--gif-playback` CLI flags when forwarding messages, causing messages to land in the wrong thread or videos to be sent without GIF playback instead of correctly passing these fields to the gateway's `send` method that already supports them.
f969d494e2084c75: The `gen_ai.prompt` field is recorded using `followupRun.prompt` instead of the actual `commandBody` passed to the agent, meaning the logged prompt may omit context the model truly saw (e.g., thread history, system events, or media) and include text it did not, resulting in a misleading audit trail.
ec6de2d5b4c74819: The dashboard header subtitle is missing `min-width: 0` on its flex item, preventing `text-overflow: ellipsis` from working correctly for long localized strings and causing layout overflow instead of clean text truncation.
c98fa837de53d6d0: The `wrapOutbound` function calls detached method references without preserving their original `this` binding, which will cause runtime failures for any listener implementations that rely on `this` context, and should instead use `.apply()` with the receiver to safely wrap outbound calls.
ff3dec575add0937: The issue creation command never passes `--label` to `gh issue create`, so issues filed through this skill will be missing the canonical `bug`/`enhancement` labels defined in the templates, breaking triage and searchability.
30c013cba8fb57f0: The health-ping logic forces a hard reconnect when a periodic presence update fails, inconsistently treating what the initial connect-time code handles as a non-fatal, best-effort error, which can trigger reconnect loops and unnecessary downtime when presence updates are flaky but the underlying connection is still functional.
eea28aaa8e33b610: The `cmdExeCommandLine` computation and its unsafe-argument validation are now applied unconditionally to all `runExec` calls instead of only within the `.cmd`/`.bat` wrapper branch, causing legitimate arguments like URL query strings or percent-encoded values to incorrectly throw `Unsafe Windows cmd.exe argument detected` and prevent process execution.
e2b1a527d6f5fe66: Removing the CSS rule that hid `.content-header` on mobile causes chat session/model controls to render twice on screens narrower than 1100px — once in the content header and again in the topbar gear menu — wasting vertical space and breaking the mobile layout.
1121b9388d050c60: The new `shouldPreferNativeJiti` branch replaces `getJiti().import()` with a raw dynamic `import()` for certain helper paths, which will cause `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` failures for Matrix plugins that ship source-style `.js` wrapper files relying on Jiti's module resolution fallback to handle unbuilt `.ts` sibling references.
1296f5941033fefa: In `trusted-proxy` mode, the browser control auth setup unconditionally overwrites `gateway.auth.token` with a new random plaintext token when no credential is resolved, which can silently replace a SecretRef-backed credential in `openclaw.json` and cause downstream flows to drift from the operator-managed secret.
1d7d2f8f7caeba34: The new branch incorrectly increments the shared-secret rate-limit bucket for device-token-only authentication flows where no shared secret was ever attempted, which can cause legitimate clients to be rate-limited on unrelated logins after enough reconnects.
fa7fe558a4e16780: When coercing a direct `dispatcherPolicy` into env-proxy mode, `proxyTls` options are not mirrored from the caller's transport hints, causing proxied HTTPS paths (e.g., Telegram media downloads) to lose IPv4/Happy-Eyeballs fallback behavior in proxy-enabled deployments.
6efcc1136a25c197: Routing all strict fetches through the environment proxy when `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY` is set — without exempting callers that use `allowedHostnames`/`allowPrivateNetwork` for loopback or LAN hosts — will break local integrations and leak internal hostnames to the proxy in deployments where those hosts lack a matching `NO_PROXY` entry.
5c3972c53a2eb2ed: The new call to `hasEnvHttpProxyConfigured` always uses the HTTPS lookup path regardless of the actual request protocol, causing `http://` fetches to incorrectly enter env-proxy mode when only `HTTPS_PROXY` is set, resulting in direct-connect failures in proxy-only environments.
89c3aa96a0e2570a: The skill only collects a bare branch name for PR creation, but `gh pr create` requires the `<user>:<branch>` format when the head branch lives on a fork, making it impossible to reliably create PRs from forked repositories.
e2ddad25ed907be9: The proxy-selection branch fails to honor the `dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork` policy flag, causing callers who rely on that alias to be incorrectly routed through `EnvHttpProxyAgent`, which can leak private/local network requests to the proxy or cause failures unless `NO_PROXY` is explicitly configured.
532c6320ce048d20: Adding `src/config/doc-baseline.test.ts` to the `unit.isolated` bucket when it already exists in `singletonIsolated` causes the expensive test file to be executed twice concurrently, regressing runtime and reintroducing the memory spike the patch intended to fix.
12cbabce710f3c3b: The workflow fails to delete the `latest` git tag before recreating the release, causing subsequent pushes to `main` to publish a new release still anchored to the old commit rather than the new HEAD.
a4045c72f46dd70c: Adding `export type * from "../infra/json-files.js"` is ineffective because the source module exports only functions and no named types, meaning any downstream consumers failing due to missing JSON-file types will continue to fail after this change.
d728558f183904d7: When the first message in an unknown group is a slash command, the `unknownGroupAction` logic is bypassed entirely because command middleware runs before and independently of the group-check handler, meaning bots configured to warn or leave unknown groups will instead silently remain in them.
71cb04a43df48d2b: The `isUnknownGroupPolicyBlock` function incorrectly classifies `group-policy-allowlist-empty` as an unknown group block even when the group is intentionally covered by a wildcard (`"*"`) allowlist entry, which could cause the bot to erroneously warn or leave groups that are legitimately configured.
b44681016ba4c262: Clearing the draft and resetting `hasStreamedMessage` during preview edit fallback causes the subsequent `deliverNormally()` call to lose its thread timestamp (because `replyPlan.markSent()` was already called), resulting in the fallback message being posted to the main channel instead of the correct thread.
c7d18e0694b24e9f: The new unknown-group warning path passes `resolvedThreadId` directly without filtering out the value `1`, which Telegram rejects, causing 400 errors in General-topic forums when `unknownGroupAction` is set to `"warn"` and silently skipping the warning before leaving when set to `"leave"`.
4d778859e3521aef: The new branch unconditionally clears and resets any previously streamed message as if it were a draft, but because `hasStreamedMessage` is never reset after a draft is finalized into a real message via `editSlackMessage`, subsequent payloads in a multi-reply sequence will incorrectly delete already-posted final replies before sending the next one.
fa09d4060ca346ee: During compaction, the session transcript path is unconditionally overwritten with a plain `<newSessionId>.jsonl` filename, which will silently break history, export, and status reads for any session that uses a non-canonical path such as timestamped fork files or topic-specific thread paths.
80f077a41cc8e799: Passing the rotated `newSessionId` only to `incrementCompactionCount` leaves the in-flight `followupRun` still bound to the pre-flush session, causing the same request to be split across two session files — the store points to the new transcript while the assistant reply is appended to the old one.
2a90c714d7c2d5ea: Swapping the fallback priority to prefer `label` over `displayName` can cause raw session keys or query tokens to be displayed instead of human-readable names when a user has saved the session key as the label, directly undermining the change's intended goal.
1a279f13c48084fa: The cleanup command hardcodes the resource group name `rg-openclaw` instead of using the configured `${RG}` variable, which could cause users who deployed with a custom resource group name to either fail to clean up their resources (leaving unexpected costs) or accidentally delete the wrong resource group.
4670de28e38c8ac4: Removing the normalization of `sessionEntry.thinkingLevel` means that a persisted `xhigh` value is no longer downgraded to `high` before `/compact` runs, causing an unnecessary failed API call and retry when compacting on a model that doesn't support `xhigh`.
dbc09882b6cbd8d6: The fix only addresses directive-only requests, but the normal reply path in `get-reply-run.ts` still overwrites a persisted `xhigh` thinking level down to `high` on non-directive messages, meaning session state will still be corrupted in the common targeted cases.
886bd7c5ca644c7d: Suppressing `lastExitStatus` and `lastExitReason` fields when a service is in the "running" state at the data layer causes downstream diagnostic flows to lose critical crash exit codes for services that have already auto-restarted back to active, masking real failures; this suppression should instead be applied only in the display formatter.
1bf5b65fd05e25e9: The slash-model-ID fallback logic only searches `cfg.agents.defaults.models` for candidate providers, so it fails to resolve bare Ollama model IDs (e.g. `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B`) in `allowAny` configurations where providers are discovered solely from the live gateway catalog, causing valid models to be incorrectly rejected as "not allowed."
24f8e0ed71018dd6: The new reply hint instructs agents to use `sessions_send` for private replies but omits the required `deliveryMode: "private"` or `announce: false` flags, so agents following the hint will default to public announcement mode and inadvertently leak private replies into public channels or trigger nested announce loops.
0de35f33695dceed: The sender-session hint is incorrectly shown to the requester agent on its own turn, causing it to target its own session key and potentially stall or infinitely recurse instead of replying to the other agent — the hint should only appear on target turns.
7426f76c0226dd18: The retry loop silently resolves ambiguous slash-prefixed model IDs by binding to the first matching provider instead of rejecting the ambiguity, potentially switching auth and capability behavior without any user signal.
bcbfd29d11910a8f: The duplicate-registration branch uses a shared numeric refcount per key instead of tracking individual adapter owners, so when one of multiple registrants unregisters, the map may retain a stale adapter instance while ignoring the still-live owner, causing `bind/list/resolve` operations to route through invalid state.
424397ea53941e3c: The `unregisterSessionBindingAdapter` function's new adapterless fallback incorrectly uses `pop()` to remove the most recently registered adapter instead of deterministically removing the calling owner, which can leave a stale adapter active and cause session binding operations to route to the wrong manager.
b296ec11d5bd22ab: `shouldUseAnthropicAuthorizationHeader()` only checks user-supplied provider configs and ignores implicit providers injected by `resolveImplicitProviders()`, so built-in Anthropic-compatible providers like `minimax` will never have the required `Authorization: Bearer` header applied.
751ea9198d68a7a0: The auth header lookup uses a strict byte-for-byte URL comparison to determine whether to reuse a saved provider's `authHeader: true` setting, so semantically equivalent URL variants (e.g., `https://host` vs. `https://host/v1`) will fail to match and cause onboarding to incorrectly fall back to `x-api-key` instead of `Bearer`, breaking verification against Bearer-only gateways.
1625748f87460029: The new `authHeader` parameter is only passed to `requestAnthropicVerification` in the explicit Anthropic compatibility branch, but the automatic unknown-endpoint detection path omits it, causing previously configured Bearer-auth Anthropic-compatible endpoints to fail auto-detection and incorrectly re-enter the retry flow.
c4ecba115f85baae: The `authHeader` fix is only applied to the explicit Anthropic compatibility branch, leaving the "Unknown (detect automatically)" probe path still calling `requestAnthropicVerification` without `authHeader`, which means Bearer-only Anthropic-compatible endpoints will continue to fail auto-detection and trap users in a retry loop.
62a7016682cd2e64: The `createAnthropicAuthHeaderWrapper` relies on `authHeader` being set in the explicit provider config, but for built-in Bearer-only providers like `minimax`, this flag is only injected by `resolveImplicitProviders()` and is absent when users have no explicit `models.providers` entry, causing those endpoints to incorrectly use Anthropic-style auth instead of Bearer auth at runtime.
1c1b7a8e1fbe74d2: The `think: false` flag is incorrectly nested inside `payload.options` instead of being set at the top level of the request body, causing Ollama's chat API to ignore it and continue returning unwanted reasoning-only chunks even when `thinkingLevel` is set to `"off"`.
b3a10e4a606a89eb: The `shouldUseAnthropicAuthorization` helper hardcodes `true` for `minimax`/`minimax-portal` provider IDs without consulting the merged provider config, so an explicit `authHeader: false` override set via `mergeProviders()` is silently ignored and the wrapper still strips `x-api-key` and forces `Authorization: Bearer` auth, breaking any proxy endpoint that was intentionally reconfigured to use API key authentication.
27745bd2ce4404b2: The `createOllamaThinkingPayloadWrapper` is incorrectly applied for every `thinkingLevel === "off"` run (which is also the default), sending an unsupported boolean `think=false` to Ollama's GPT-OSS model instead of the required `low|medium|high` values, making it impossible to disable reasoning in that supported configuration.
ff8c1eb88ed3d075: When `thinkingLevel` is set to `"off"`, the `think: false` flag is incorrectly nested inside the `options` object rather than being placed at the top level of the Ollama `/api/chat` request body, rendering the thinking-suppression feature ineffective on the native Ollama path.
f0763cc911205689: The new `onPayload` hook inadvertently allows the Moonshot thinking wrapper to mutate native Ollama `/api/chat` request payloads with a non-native `thinking` object, breaking default Kimi cloud model requests that use the Ollama API.
fbb0eab1e3941b68: Adding the `onPayload` hook to the native Ollama request path unintentionally activates Moonshot compatibility wrappers that inject OpenAI-style fields (e.g., `thinking`, `tool_choice`) into native `/api/chat` payloads, potentially malforming requests for Kimi Cloud models that use `provider="ollama"`.
f7c030c18c92b9c0: The newly added `fromCronLane: true` flag intended to prevent self-blocking in the cron lane is silently dropped by intermediate layers (`CronServiceDeps.runHeartbeatOnce` and the gateway adapter) before reaching `runHeartbeatOnce`, causing manual cron runs with `wakeMode: "now"` to still hit the `cron-in-progress` block, time out, and fall back to queued wake instead of firing an immediate heartbeat.
bfcdb5c4ced05180: The unconditional `CommandLane.Cron` queue check causes `wakeMode: "now"` manual cron runs to immediately return `"cron-in-progress"` and get permanently skipped, because the busy-wait retry loop in `timer.ts` only retries on `"requests-in-flight"`, not `"cron-in-progress"`.
92ef99962f736fe5: The fast-xml-parser security bump to 5.5.7 is only placed in `pnpm.overrides`, which is ignored by npm, leaving npm-based installs of the published CLI still vulnerable to resolving the older version through transitive dependencies.
ab936a0f361f8f97: Using `trim()` on incremental thinking delta chunks strips meaningful boundary whitespace before diffing, causing spaces and newlines between tokens to be lost in the accumulated reasoning stream output.
a9bcf437346bd4a7: Because `currentConfig` is advanced even when plugin changes are skipped during hot reload, a subsequent unrelated hot-reload can silently apply stale plugin policy changes (such as model allowlists) without the required restart, bypassing the intended restart boundary.
b66585b6a0e22a83: The new logic incorrectly downgrades terminal failures to success when any payload precedes the last error, causing cron jobs with failed final actions to suppress retries and alerts while recording misleading success status.
2da33a2a72116860: Thinking events are only sent to recipients registered at run start, so a control UI that reconnects or joins a session mid-run will never receive thinking updates because there is no late-join or session-scoped fallback subscription path.
3d9ea0b11506fa3e: When promoting the `channel` field to `target` during normalization, the original `channel` value is never cleared, leaving an invalid provider identifier that causes `resolveMessageChannelSelection` to reject it and the send action to continue failing instead of recovering.
ffb55e72babe1492: The `sendSmsViaNode` function always selects the first available Android node rather than the node that received the original inbound SMS, which in multi-node/multi-SIM deployments can cause replies to be sent from the wrong phone number or a node without service, breaking conversation continuity.
e0e583ab5853e432: The header context display was updated to use `totalTokens`, but the `/usage` slash command still calculates usage from `inputTokens`, leaving two visible usage surfaces in WebChat reporting inconsistent token counts for the same session.
c257c023f9152a42: Passing `sessionKey` to `requestHeartbeatNow` breaks global-scope setups by turning unscoped hook wakes into targeted wakes that resolve to the absent `main` agent, causing `hook:wake` and `hook:<jobId>` events to be silently skipped even though they were already enqueued.
696a30fc0dc5da9e: Using broad `includes` substring matching for exec completion event strings (e.g., "exec completed", "exec failed", "exec killed") can falsely classify unrelated system events like cron reminders as exec completions, causing `resolveHeartbeatRunPrompt` to misroute or suppress those messages with the wrong prompt handler.
9f3b1111bbdbcc3f: Using `isExecEventReason` to gate the `forceLastTargetWhenNone` override is too broad, as it is true for non-completion exec events like `exec.started` and `exec.denied`, causing heartbeats with an explicit `target: "none"` to incorrectly fall back to the last delivery channel and produce unintended user-facing notifications.
28a238372b997d70: Returning `wakeOptions` without `sessionKey` for cron sessions removes agent scoping, causing heartbeat wakes to execute across all heartbeat-enabled agents instead of only the originating one.
d7ba08f7195694f3: Sorting reset transcript files lexicographically by full filename instead of by timestamp suffix can cause an older transcript to be incorrectly restored during session recovery when multiple session IDs are present in the sessions directory.
bacd0cb860ec13ca: In the timed-out `/new` recovery branch, the fallback logic searches for the newest `*.jsonl.reset.*` file across the entire `workspace/sessions` directory without scoping the lookup to the current session, meaning a more recently reset unrelated conversation could be copied into the wrong memory note, causing cross-conversation data corruption or leakage in multi-chat workspaces.
f339f00e9e347a2f: In summary-first compaction sessions, legacy assistant messages without timestamps bypass both the timestamp-based and index-based stale usage checks, causing pre-compaction usage to incorrectly persist and inflate the post-compaction context counter.
d603924b22f7896c: The hotspot updater's default `--lane unit-fast` doesn't match the CI-emitted lane names `unit-fast-1` and `unit-fast-2`, causing it to silently filter out all CI summary lines and write an empty hotspot set unless the caller manually overrides the lane flag.
9e0931c44e949f89: Memory-heavy unit files are not excluded from the timed-heavy selection pool, so tests that qualify as both duration-heavy and memory-heavy will be scheduled in multiple lanes simultaneously, defeating the isolation goal and risking OOM pressure in CI.
3bb1c521d0591eb5: Removing the `completed` field from the compaction `phase: "end"` event payload breaks multiple downstream listeners that rely on `evt.data.completed === true` to recognize successful compactions, causing attempt counts to stop incrementing and memory-flush dedup to no longer latch.
b79abc195bd504c7: The streamed `/v1/responses` path omits `trustedProxies` when calling `runResponsesAgentCommand`, causing `resolveIngressSenderIsOwner` to always return `false` for streamed requests from trusted upstreams, while the non-stream branch correctly passes `trustedProxies` — creating a behavior mismatch that breaks owner-only capabilities for streamed responses.
dcf95578fd3a5e5e: In summary-first transcripts, pre-compaction assistant messages with missing or unparsable timestamps bypass both stale-usage-clearing checks, leaving inflated usage counts in the TUI context counter because the logic only handles assistants with valid timestamps or positional ordering relative to the compaction marker.
47168d124a12f275: When a legacy engine rejects `sessionKey` in an early method like `bootstrap()`, the fast path in `wrapContextEngineWithSessionKeyCompat` bypasses `invokeWithLegacyCompat()` for subsequent calls like `assemble()`, causing the engine to throw if it also rejects other new fields like `prompt`.
5af64ea3ebff3c43: Switching the `--json` output from filtered `paired` entries to `NodeListNode` objects silently drops pairing metadata fields (`lastConnectedAtMs`, `createdAtMs`, `approvedAtMs`), breaking any automation that relies on those timestamps from `openclaw nodes list --json`.
e99aabed04258014: Inlining `runtimeContext` during overflow recovery drops critical fields (e.g., `currentChannelId`, `bashElevated`, `reasoningLevel`, `extraSystemPrompt`) that compaction relies on, causing delegated compaction to run under a different context than manual or after-turn compaction and potentially producing incorrect summaries or missing actions.
99853a255b95c1bc: When a node appears as paired in `node.list` but is missing from `node.pair.list`, it falls through the cracks of both `filteredPaired` and `filteredConnected` arrays and is silently dropped from `--json` output, even though it is still rendered correctly in text mode.
e9a483f6c55fc290: Passing `agentId` into `syncSkillsToWorkspace()` for a shared sandbox causes whichever agent initializes last to overwrite the shared `skills/` directory for all agents, meaning other agents can lose their allowed skills or inherit the wrong set.
db0babd3ba2f14d6: An unconditional `node.list` call added to the unfiltered `nodes list` command now requires `operator.read` scope in addition to `operator.pairing`, breaking tokens that were intentionally scoped to pairing-only access and causing unauthorized errors where previously they would succeed.
a7779526eecc8786: New Copilot reasoning models are registered in `resolveDynamicModel` but omitted from the shared model catalog, causing them to incorrectly default to thinking `off` instead of the expected low-thinking default, which silently degrades behavior for users who switch to those models.
63df116f35ace20f: Setting `input: ["text", "image"]` on Copilot models is insufficient because `loadModelCatalog` doesn't include GitHub Copilot rows, causing `src/media-understanding/runner.ts` to incorrectly route vision-capable models like `gpt-5.4-mini` through the OCR/vision preprocessing fallback instead of passing images natively, adding unnecessary latency and cost.
94f2e92f21328615: During a forced reload triggered by an external edit to `jobs.json`, a missing `try/catch` around `computeJobNextRunAtMs()` means an invalid cron expression or timezone will throw an unhandled exception, causing the entire reload to fail and halting processing of all unrelated cron jobs until the file is corrected.
34517193a05878af: The updated anchor fragment `#agent-sandbox` in `sandboxing.md` points to a heading that doesn't exist in `docs/install/docker.md`, leaving Docker users with a broken link that fails to navigate them to the correct bootstrap steps.
c133d463ecb17033: The updated anchor link in the Anthropic 429 runbook incorrectly points to the unrelated "quick-start-and-first-run-setup" FAQ section instead of the correct entry addressing HTTP 429 RateLimitErrors, leaving users unable to find relevant troubleshooting guidance.
5fdf7dfa1d479005: The raw comparison of `everyMs` and `anchorMs` fields ignores the floor/coerce normalization applied elsewhere, so a job with a fractional interval like `60000.9` will be falsely detected as changed, triggering a next-run recomputation that can silently skip an execution even though the effective schedule is unchanged.
0038f360a2fe1f30: The timer-driven post-run path in `onTimer()` still calls `recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance` with the default `consumeReloadRepairSkip: true`, unlike the manual run fix, which can cause a concurrently-edited invalid job's skip marker to be prematurely consumed and its `scheduleErrorCount` incorrectly incremented an extra time, potentially auto-disabling an unrelated job one maintenance cycle too early.
249d768bbce830c3: When `jobs.json` is reloaded from a stale snapshot, an outdated numeric `runningAtMs` marker is not overwritten in memory, causing the stuck-job cleanup logic to falsely mark an active job as idle and potentially launch a duplicate concurrent execution.
6b10c79f4f452126: By passing `consumeReloadRepairSkip: false` in the post-run maintenance call, a reload-skip marker for a malformed job is never consumed under a continuously busy scheduler, causing `scheduleErrorCount` to stall at 1 indefinitely and preventing the job from ever reaching the 3-strike auto-disable/alert threshold.
abf6fbf435dc2313: The `signalRecoverableOutboundNetworkError` method aborts `#activeFetchAbort`, which is shared across all Telegram API calls (not just `getUpdates`), meaning it can cancel in-flight `sendMessage` or `editMessageText` requests before the replacement poller is ready, causing the final reply to still be lost despite polling restarting.
953423d0e103aeff: Aborting the shared fetch signal during an outbound restart will cause any in-progress Telegram API calls (e.g., `sendMessage`, `editMessageText`) to fail with an unretryable abort error, silently dropping replies even when the network has recovered.
c3d7d4409f518d1d: The retry wrapper is applied at the entire `sendPayload` level rather than individual Telegram requests, so when a payload fans out into multiple chunked or attachment requests, a mid-delivery failure will cause already-delivered chunks or media to be re-sent, duplicating content on retry.
094822bb75cb2a74: The `pollStallThresholdMs` config option cannot be honored for values below 30 seconds because the watchdog timer that checks for stalls still wakes on a hardcoded 30,000 ms interval, making any configured threshold shorter than 30 seconds effectively impossible to trigger in time.
3a133ed1122bb84d: The `onRecoverableSendChatActionNetworkFailure` callback is bound directly to the shared `TelegramPollingSession` without being scoped to its poll cycle, allowing a stale bot instance that outlives a restart to invoke `signalRecoverableOutboundNetworkError()` against a newer, healthy `activeRunner` and trigger a spurious restart.
e644032b03979081: The fetch wrapper incorrectly resets the consecutive network failure counter on all 4xx responses, including proxy-generated errors like `407 Proxy Authentication Required` that never reach Telegram, preventing the fast polling-restart path from triggering during the exact proxy outages it was designed to recover from.
090deb6063757ec6: The minimum poll stall threshold of 31 seconds (30s long-poll + 1s buffer) is too tight, as it leaves insufficient margin for network latency, causing healthy polling sessions on slow or high-latency connections to trigger false "polling stall detected" restarts.
3e08ca7952731159: The `shouldMarkTelegramNetworkHealthy` function incorrectly returns `false` for all 5xx responses, even JSON 5xx responses that confirm Telegram was successfully reached, which prevents `noteNetworkHealthy()` from being called and can cause the outage counter to spuriously trigger a polling restart on an otherwise healthy network.
a3bcfbf205e15b60: The negated CLI flag `--no-device-identity` is read using the wrong property name `opts.noDeviceIdentity` instead of `opts.deviceIdentity`, causing the flag to always evaluate to `false` and making the token-only auth path unreachable.
91260c980081d09c: Moving `startTtlTimer()` before `await fireStart()` introduces a race condition where a slow first start can allow the TTL to fire `fireStop()` and set `closed = true` mid-await, yet `keepaliveLoop.start()` is still called unconditionally afterward without re-checking `closed`, causing keepalive pings to resume after an explicit auto-stop.
5ca51a9dc09fb2dc: When a force-cycle restart occurs, the `finally` block skips awaiting `runner.stop()`/`bot.stop()`, allowing a new bot instance to start before in-flight update handlers from the old cycle finish, which causes duplicate update processing and duplicate user-visible replies due to re-fetched updates from the last persisted offset.
1251c55445d50574: `stripImageContentForPersistence` eagerly removes toolResult image blocks at write time before they are guaranteed to have been consumed by an assistant turn, meaning that if a run is interrupted mid-session, resuming from persisted JSONL will be missing critical tool output images and the model cannot recover them.
47a5a5cb91333042: Applying `stripImageContentForPersistence` during persistence transformation immediately removes image content from the latest user turn before an assistant response is received, which breaks retry/resume scenarios by preventing the model from re-reading the original image input.
18575b86caab62a9: A lost-wakeup race condition in `waitForActiveRetryRunsToFinish` can cause `drainPendingDecryptions()` to block forever if the last retry completes after the `activeRetryRuns === 0` check but before the resolver is added to `retryIdleResolvers`, preventing the Matrix monitor from shutting down.
3612c6e4247188cd: If an exception is thrown during shutdown in `drainPendingDecryptions()` or `threadBindingManager.stop()`, `releaseSharedClientInstance()` is never called, leaving the shared client state marked as started despite the underlying Matrix client being stopped, which can cause a long-lived gateway process to reuse a stale, non-syncing client on the next monitor start for the same account.
ae1f5a0fc4f0be58: `drainPendingDecryptions` silently exits after a fixed number of retry attempts even if encrypted events remain unresolved, causing the caller to incorrectly persist a clean shutdown and permanently drop events that were still awaiting decryption keys.
42a19b47ac6bf9da: The tombstone guard only clears reused run keys on `lifecycle/start` or `seq === 1`, so if the gateway first sees a reused key at seq 2 or later (e.g., the start event was lost), all subsequent events for that run are silently dropped, causing the chat to never stream or finalize.
d21beb0a253bd572: Because `chat.send` now seeds `minEventTsByEffectiveRunKey` for every request but non-streaming/no-agent-run paths never call `clearEffectiveChatRunState`, each completed idempotency key leaves a permanent map entry, causing an unbounded memory leak on long-lived gateways.
8634893597e84dba: When an agent run reuses an idempotency key that was previously finalized, stale-packet guards can incorrectly drop the new run's packets if the first observed sequence number is greater than 1, causing the run to never stream or finalize until the tombstone expires.
ed00af2435526898: The timestamp fence that guards against stale events is never seeded for the `agent` method, so delayed events from a previous run with a reused idempotency key can appear fresh and corrupt chat state before the new run begins.
1e04106c5082f21f: When a debug `[TUI] evt=...` line arrives as a `delta` event before a hidden `final` message, the streamed fallback path persists the unfiltered debug text into `chatMessages` because `state.chatStream` is never sanitized of such lines, allowing debug output to render in the UI.
a87ba4af2f09052e: The early `kind: "final"` return for rate-limit errors bypasses the normal `buildReplyPayloads()` deduplication logic, causing a second failure message to be appended to users who already received content via streaming or a messaging tool.
6f677a490678633c: Returning `kind: "final"` for context overflow errors bypasses the post-run bookkeeping logic that only executes for `kind: "success"`, causing session metadata (usage, model, provider, system-prompt) to go stale and potentially skewing future token-based decisions and fallback behavior.
b31761a5ea431b13: When `reasoningLevel === "on"`, incomplete turns that produce only an `isReasoning` payload are not caught by the `payloads.length === 0` guard, so no `meta.error` is set, the fallback logic is never triggered, and reasoning-enabled sessions silently return no reply to the user.
eb0205d155bc7368: The `didSendViaMessagingTool` flag incorrectly suppresses the 429/529 mid-turn fallback reply for the originating conversation when a cross-target message was sent, because the coarse boolean doesn't distinguish between same-origin and cross-target sends, causing the originating conversation to silently receive no reply.
a90c06dc173ef1ec: The cooldown logic for exhausted auth profiles is a no-op in the premature-resolution branch because `lastAssistant.errorMessage` is empty at that point, causing `classifyFailoverReason` to return null and leaving the exhausted credential eligible for reuse on the next turn in multi-profile setups.
284af0765b3dca71: The incomplete-turn fallback incorrectly synthesizes an error message ("⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response") for same-target message sends that already succeeded, causing embedded callers to surface a visible failure even though the real reply was already delivered to the user.
15e46c4ecdfe15a8: The fallback error handler incorrectly displays a generic "API rate limit reached" message for all matching errors, even when the failure is specifically a 529 overload error, misleading users with wrong diagnosis and recovery guidance despite distinct overload copy already existing in the codebase.
1ee42c528043e5c9: The automatic reset hook (`session:*_reset`) is emitted after `archiveSessionTranscripts` renames the old transcript file, causing legacy session entries without `previousSessionEntry.sessionFile` to generate empty memory notes because `findPreviousSessionFile` can no longer locate the archived conversation.
aaf8c1eaf69b3cda: The onboarding wizard resolves search providers from a static list without checking plugin enablement rules, allowing users to select and persist a provider that will silently be blocked at runtime, resulting in a non-functional web search configuration.
e145221c60649cbd: The `hasPayloadText` check incorrectly counts reasoning-only payloads as valid user-facing content, causing 429/overload error rewrites to be skipped and leaving reasoning-enabled turns with no visible reply after reasoning payloads are dropped during delivery.
d4397fc44a6e639d: When the setup wizard runs within the Gateway/Control UI, `listWebSearchProviders` incorrectly resolves providers from the already-active plugin registry instead of the newly updated `nextConfig` snapshot, causing a provider just enabled by the wizard to appear unavailable and skipping key detection until after a restart.
907f75e098c5933b: The Feishu plugin removed handler implementations for several advertised actions (pin, unpin, list-pins, member-info, channel-info, channel-list, and reaction actions), causing users who invoke those actions to always hit runtime failures despite the plugin still advertising them as available.
2125de11fcbb69ca: When ACP thread bindings use a `"current"` placement for DM or current-surface spawns, the bootstrap delivery target is incorrectly reconstructed using `resolveConversationDeliveryTarget`, producing malformed recipients like `channel:user:<id>` that can cause the initial bootstrap message to be misrouted or fail.
a2136cd16739b92a: Removing the session-store ancestry check (`getSubagentDepthFromSessionStore`) means legacy subagent sessions without a `subagent:` key prefix will be misclassified as external, causing internal orchestration completion messages to be incorrectly delivered to user-facing channels.
d2f11bad1b61e213: When re-authenticating with LiteLLM, newly discovered `contextWindow` and `maxTokens` limits are never applied to existing model entries because `applyProviderConfigWithDefaultModel()` skips updates when the model ID is already present, leaving users permanently stuck with stale limits from their initial onboarding.
7ba64b4888871059: Widening `allowedSensitiveKeys` to include all skill env keys causes a silent credential leak where a second skill's subprocesses incorrectly inherit the first skill's sensitive values (e.g., `GITHUB_TOKEN`) due to the first-wins behavior in `acquireActiveSkillEnvKey()` when two enabled skills share the same sensitive key name.
476f02d058574210: The DuckDuckGo client uses a direct `fetch` call instead of the shared trusted web endpoint wrapper (`withTrustedWebSearchEndpoint`/`withTrustedWebToolsEndpoint`), bypassing the standard proxy/guarded networking path and centralized outbound guardrails used by all other web-search providers, which can cause failures in proxy-restricted deployments.
7d0ae727ab642eb6: Using `100vh` for the modal's max-height is unsafe on mobile browsers where dynamic toolbars cause the layout viewport to exceed the visible area, potentially keeping action buttons clipped off-screen; `100dvh` should be used instead to match the rest of the UI's mobile-safe sizing pattern.
1f8bd905f52d469a: Relay targets are matched by display name instead of unique Feishu ID, so any mention of a shared display name can incorrectly trigger message dispatch to the wrong bot account.
fc243fdf7d09d03a: The relay cleanup timer in `cross-bot-relay.ts` is not `unref()`'d, causing the Node.js process to stay alive for up to 120 seconds after message delivery due to the live event loop handle.
7ae3a77e5fe836b1: Using `withCheckedContinuation` for the contacts permission request ignores Swift task cancellation, meaning that when an invoke times out, the suspended task will remain blocked until the user dismisses the OS prompt, allowing stuck tasks to accumulate and degrade node responsiveness.
38a4583bdbccbc41: The `mentionedJids` values are passed directly into `contextInfo` without being normalized through `toWhatsappJid()`, meaning non-standard identifier formats like `+15551234567` or `whatsapp:+15551234567` will never be converted to valid WhatsApp JIDs (e.g., `15551234567@s.whatsapp.net`), causing mentions to silently fail.
9c18dd52101e5642: When constructing a quoted reply, the code incorrectly builds the `contextInfo.quotedMessage` from the new outgoing message text rather than fetching the original target message, causing every reply to display a quote bubble of itself instead of the intended message being replied to.
681cdd5a08d4b5c7: The `deliverWebReply()` function only passes `replyToId` in its options object and never extracts or forwards `mentionedJids`, so any `@mention` targets in AI-generated replies sent through WhatsApp groups will silently render as plain text instead of triggering proper mention notifications.
f01dcfb09756eafc: The newly added `replyToId` and `mentionedJids` fields are never read by the underlying `sendMessage` runtime, so extensions passing reply/mention metadata through the new signature will silently send plain messages instead of quotes or @mentions.
977ea33f247286a0: The resize-handle hover code pushes `NSCursor` without a corresponding `pop()` on view disappearance, which can leave the resize cursor permanently stuck if the window closes or the view is replaced while the pointer is still hovering over the handle.
2e02955b75348307: The `resolvedAssistantBubbleColor` function now returns a dark bubble color in both light and dark macOS appearances, causing very low contrast against dark assistant text in light mode and making chat messages unreadable.
316d7fe2dffc856a: The `mentionedJids` values passed to the inbound reply helper's `contextInfo` are not normalized through `toWhatsappJid()` like the outbound sender does, so non-standard formats like `+15551234567` or `whatsapp:+15551234567` will be treated as literal strings instead of real @mentions.
164954e2d759ac63: The `expectFalJsonPost` helper function was deleted from `fal.test.ts`, but remaining tests still call it, causing an unresolved reference error that breaks the entire fal provider test suite.
8150dfe07aa69da6: The documentation incorrectly claims that SecretRef objects are supported for media provider `apiKey` configuration, but the runtime code only resolves plain string values, meaning SecretRef configs will be treated as missing credentials and silently skipped.
68e9dfe61ed0dbca: The shared test helper file imports only `vi` from Vitest but calls `expect(...)` throughout its exported functions, leaving `expect` undefined at runtime and breaking the entire image-generation test suite since Vitest globals are not enabled in the project config.
c884472e43d88627: Tool result content is inserted verbatim inside triple-backtick code fences without escaping, so if a tool's output contains triple backticks (e.g., from markdown or code snippets), the fence closes prematurely and corrupts the exported transcript.
36e94476123c1705: The `extractAssistantText` helper in `session-export.ts` only handles assistant message content as an array of typed blocks, so legacy sessions where `msg.content` is a plain string will export as empty assistant turns, silently dropping conversation text from older transcripts.
fba7d8e503a46777: The new `withCheckedContinuation` wrappers in `requestEventAccess` and `requestWriteOnlyEventAccess` do not observe Swift task cancellation, so timed-out calendar permission requests will remain suspended until the user responds to the OS prompt, potentially accumulating stuck tasks and degrading node responsiveness in headless sessions.
6518ca8d9bc9765d: The LINE runtime barrel in `extensions/line/runtime-api.ts` uses a relative import that resolves outside its extension package to `src/plugin-sdk/line-core.js`, violating the rule that extensions must only depend on public SDK seams, making it fragile to any repo restructuring.
fa48a22811b074c7: The `toWorkspaceRelativePath()` function incorrectly identifies paths as outside the workspace by checking if the relative path string starts with `..` rather than checking for actual `..` path segments, causing valid filenames beginning with `..` (e.g., `..env`, `..reports/file.txt`) to be rejected even when they resolve within the workspace.
c2fe5dbff838726f: When `preferWorkspaceEntries` is true, the code bypasses the stored skill snapshot (which was filtered by `resolveAgentSkillsFilter`) and reloads the entire workspace skill catalog, causing sandboxed agents that were intentionally restricted to a subset of skills to advertise and provision environment variables for all eligible workspace skills instead.
38cffd61d8794551: The new TUI `/model status` handler short-circuits to a minimal `provider/model` string from local state instead of forwarding the command to the gateway, which already returns richer status information including runtime model, defaults, auth profiles, and per-provider details — making the output incomplete and potentially misleading when fallback models or auth overrides are in effect.
488f9d261d38ff54: The gateway service staleness check incorrectly flags services with custom NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS paths (e.g., corporate CA bundles) as needing a refresh, causing unnecessary reinstalls that drop the custom CA bundle and can break TLS connections.
d4315329d8246427: Using `listChannelPluginCatalogEntries` with workspace discovery in `loadBundledConfigSchemaResponse` allows local developer overrides to take priority over bundled metadata, making the docs baseline nondeterministic and risking the publication of non-bundled channel labels/blurbs.
cf1bd6fca0ef1457: When a CLI-primary agent (e.g. `claude-cli`/`codex-cli`) configures a non-CLI compaction model override, the soft-threshold memory flush path short-circuits early on `!isCli` before ever reaching `resolveCompactionModelFallbackOptions`, rendering the compaction model override ineffective.
4ede28ae66b8972f: The `/interrupt` command sends an `agent` RPC to the gateway without the required `idempotencyKey`, causing the request to fail validation and silently drop the re-queued turn while the user incorrectly receives a success message.
335ac13bfd8bcb0d: Calling `resolveMatrixInboundRoute()` (which invokes `sessionBindingService.touch()`) before early-exit checks causes ignored messages like chatter, no-mentions, and unauthorized commands to reset `lastActivityAt`, preventing Matrix thread idle expiry and keeping sessions pinned to stale bindings indefinitely.
a1d823aeab1edd44: Because `skills-status.ts` calls `loadWorkspaceSkillEntries()` without passing `agentId`, the new per-agent policy filtering is never applied during status lookups, causing agents that share a workspace to receive identical skill status reports regardless of their individual policy configurations.
878bb8ac2ae422f4: Once `isLegacy` flips true after an `assemble.runtimeContext` rejection, the compatibility wrapper never retries `invokeWithLegacySessionKeyCompat` for subsequent lifecycle hooks (`afterTurn`/`compact`), causing older plugins that validate different hook params differently to either lose `runtimeContext` entirely or surface unhandled `sessionKey` validation errors.
3b36ec733cd5b972: Calling `runContextEngineMaintenance()` after `contextEngine.compact()` returns means that for legacy and delegated engines, post-compaction side effects like session-memory indexing and `after_compaction` hooks fire against the pre-maintenance transcript, leaving downstream consumers with stale data.
1d6122ffd6a72126: The alias-fallback logic in `filterSkillEntries()` bypasses exact-name policy misses for skills whose names are canonically equivalent (differing only by `_`, `-`, or `.`), causing both skills to pass through and trigger a collision error in `workspace.ts`, making it impossible to selectively enable one of two canonically-colliding skills via `skills.policy` even when distinct `skillKey` values are assigned.
336e2a54e0155de5: When a bootstrap truncation warning is prepended to the prompt at runtime, the budget fields `currentPromptChars` and `reservedContextCharsEstimate` are calculated from the raw prompt without accounting for the extra warning text, causing context engines to under-reserve tokens and potentially assemble too much history, leading to overflow or compaction.
88407ddd3d5ce617: The runtime context estimation omits token costs for images loaded via `detectAndLoadPromptImages`, causing the context engine to over-assemble history on multimodal prompts and resulting in avoidable token overflow or compaction.
32b90a30b89c894f: The new lean daemon status branch emits synthetic `installed: null` / `loadedText: "unknown"` service entries when config is missing, replacing previously unconditional real daemon state reads and causing `status --json` to falsely report an unknown service state on fresh installs or reset machines that scripts and UIs rely on to decide whether to install or start the service.
7b5006cb4c90e822: For shell-wrapper `system.run` requests, invalid or blocked environment variable keys are silently stripped by `sanitizeSystemRunEnvOverrides()` before `sanitizeHostExecEnvWithDiagnostics()` can reject them, causing the new fail-closed hardening to be bypassed and leaving behavior inconsistent with `createExecTool()`.
1c76c07be27c4350: The refactored `sanitizeHostExecEnvWithDiagnostics()` inadvertently drops non-portable Windows environment variables like `ProgramFiles(x86)` by rebuilding the base environment through `listNormalizedPortableEnvEntries()`, potentially breaking commands and toolchains that rely on those variables to locate SDKs or installers.
a722c45a6ce09ad9: When `blockStreamingDefault` is set to `"on"`, the new logic enables block streaming for Feishu plain-text replies, but because Feishu drops non-card block payloads and the agent runner suppresses the final payload after a successful block stream, ordinary non-card replies are silently lost.
9ea6f75d3dd9e9f3: When `editDiscordComponentMessage` fails for an approved decision, the new code silently returns with no user-visible confirmation, whereas the previous flow always attempted a follow-up or reply fallback to ensure the user received feedback.
79d93acfee51894e: Setting `defer: false` removes the early Discord acknowledgment, meaning any component callback that takes longer than ~3 seconds to respond (e.g., due to network or disk I/O in a plugin handler) will exceed Discord's interaction timeout and show users "This interaction failed."
0d11d1d9bff799e2: The Qmemory plugin setup snippet is missing a required `openclaw gateway restart` command, meaning users following the instructions on a live gateway will silently end up with the plugin unactivated until a manual restart is performed.
f68108eb90193cfc: Adding `fill="none"` to the `arrowDown` icon template has no effect because the only live usage already applies `fill: none` via CSS, meaning the triangle artifact bug remains unfixed and the change should instead target the scroll pill's styling or path data directly.
38c650805753c0f6: Calling `resolveMatrixInboundRoute()` before the mention gate causes unmentioned or unauthorized messages to inadvertently update `lastActivityAt` on matched bindings, which can prevent ACP/session bindings from expiring even when no message is actually routed to the agent.
751e7881d811e603: Two non-existent auth plugin entries (`google-gemini-cli-auth` and `minimax-portal-auth`) were manually added to the bundled plugin metadata snapshot without corresponding plugin directories, causing a mismatch between the checked-in snapshot (77 entries) and the auto-generated output (75 entries) that will fail the snapshot validation test in CI.
41893f5749ef9557: Adding `gateway.handshakeTimeoutMs` to `FIELD_HELP` without a corresponding entry in `FIELD_LABELS` breaks the required parity between the two maps, causing the UI label to be undefined and the schema quality parity test to fail.
fd06a10a516ba471: The service layer was updated to accept `payload.kind="exec"` for cron jobs, but the gateway RPC schemas and validation logic were not updated to match, making the new payload type silently inaccessible to external callers through the public API.
3ce8b271f40d41ba: The `exec` job timeout configuration is ignored by the outer `resolveCronJobTimeoutMs()` wrapper, causing any exec job intended to run longer than the generic 10-minute ceiling to be incorrectly aborted regardless of its configured `timeoutSeconds`.
a727c93d589c3650: Calling `loadConfig()` inside `getHandshakeTimeoutMs()` on every new WebSocket connection before authentication triggers repeated synchronous disk reads and JSON5 parsing whenever connections are spaced more than 200ms apart, causing unnecessary CPU/disk overhead that can delay handshakes.
78ffd649d129e183: The new `/model status` branch in the TUI short-circuits before reaching the gateway's full implementation, causing TUI users to see only a potentially stale or undefined `modelProvider/model` string instead of the detailed auth/profile/endpoint view produced by the real handler in `directive-handling.model.ts`.
81d8e01c1fc6b980: If a plugin getter throws during initialization, the truncated result gets permanently cached, causing built-in web-search providers to silently disappear for the rest of the process even after the module finishes initializing.
79a90e11fedea681: Replacing the mock-based fault injection with `fs.chmod(..., 0o500)` makes the sweeper failure test non-deterministic on Windows CI, where that chmod call does not reliably deny write access, potentially causing the test to hang instead of hitting the intended error path.
f5b56a24e426b581: Hook script paths are resolved relative to the cron store's parent directory instead of `ocHomeDir`, causing `beforeRun`/`afterRun` hooks to fail with module-not-found errors when `cron.store` is configured outside the default state directory.
5fbffb22650422fc: In `announceScriptOutput`, `job.sessionKey` is not passed to `resolveDeliveryTarget` when resolving `delivery.channel="last"` targets, causing script output to be incorrectly routed to the agent's main session instead of the job's intended thread-specific session.
f9d4c21cf97b6962: Adding `readonly` to the plugin property does not fully fix the lazy-loading issue because `registry.ts` still eagerly maps over `bundledWebSearchPluginRegistrations` at module scope, meaning a plugin caught in a circular-import/TDZ window will still throw during initialization before the intended lazy resolution can occur.
7dfb4c6f66503c67: When all queued items have already been sent, the early-exit `break` skips the dropped-summary cleanup, leaving `droppedCount` non-zero and causing the `finally` block to reschedule the drain indefinitely without ever delivering the overflow summary.
e843dd15402b953c: Appending a uniqueness suffix to `action_id` breaks existing plugin handlers and agent workflows that match on the previously stable `openclaw:reply_button` action ID, since the raw action ID is forwarded downstream into handler payloads and system events without normalization.
b8eba2838c899ff3: When cloning a plugin entry with object spread after a null-returning lazy getter, the spread re-evaluates `entry.plugin` and can re-trigger the same `ReferenceError` from a circular-import/TDZ state, causing the graceful degradation to fail.
788c174bd58b2b49: The new style-suffix parser incorrectly strips trailing "primary" or "danger" segments from button callback values, causing click handlers to silently receive truncated payloads for any existing namespaced values that legitimately end in those keywords.
907c6e8b33579961: The supplementary usage event is emitted after `runEmbeddedPiAgent` has already fired its terminal lifecycle `end`/`error` event, which causes `createAgentEventHandler` to tear down the chat link and run context before the `phase: "usage"` event arrives, resulting in the usage payload being silently dropped for queued follow-up runs.
3755b95b103def1c: The `durationMs` field in the new usage lifecycle event is sourced from `result.meta.durationMs`, which only reflects the final successful model attempt, so runs that exhaust one or more fallback models will report an artificially short duration compared to the true end-to-end elapsed time captured in `startedAt`.
c9e2277e9d7e32f4: Emitting a "usage" lifecycle event after the "end" event causes `server-chat` to recreate an `agentRunSeq` map entry that is never cleaned up, since only "end" and "error" events trigger cleanup, leading to a permanent memory leak per successful run on long-lived gateways.
9b8b3bd66f5840f3: Setting `senderIsOwner` to `false` over-broadly removes all cron tools (including non-mutating ones like `status`, `list`, `run`, `runs`, and `wake`) from isolated cron jobs, when the intent was only to prevent self-mutation via `cron.update`, causing legitimate read-only and trigger operations to fail unnecessarily.
94c70c598d486979: The new `usage` lifecycle phase branch cleans up `agentRunSeq` entries but never calls `clearAgentRunContext`, causing a permanent memory leak in the gateway's run-context map for every late-arriving `phase: "usage"` event.
eb2026bc5c94b686: The newly emitted `usage` event for queued follow-up runs loses its session mapping because `emitAgentEvent` drops the `sessionKey` for non-internal channels and the run context is already cleared by the time the event fires, meaning session-scoped recorders will never receive usage data for queued follow-up completions.
a7f0d4af7b3b38c8: Runtime log files containing sensitive local credentials, file paths, and a Telegram chat ID were accidentally committed to the repository, exposing private environment details to all repo readers and tarball consumers.
2951a06ae35f2bd3: The accumulated token/cost usage summed across all cron turns is incorrectly attributed solely to the final turn's model and provider, meaning multi-turn or multi-model runs will produce inaccurate cost reporting in downstream systems.
3e0487833778129a: The zombie process check on macOS only matches exact strings "Z" and "Z+", but ps state fields can have additional modifier characters appended (e.g., "Zs", "ZE"), causing zombie processes to be incorrectly identified as alive and breaking stale lock cleanup.
efdd98f104f42078: The catch-all Copilot model resolver incorrectly advertises image input support for every unrecognized model ID, causing text-only or mistyped models to receive multimodal payloads and trigger provider-side failures instead of safely falling back to text-only behavior.
6e19d8198389f059: The updated error handler blindly casts exceptions to `NodeJS.ErrnoException` and logs only `err.code`, causing syntax errors and runtime failures during hook import to collapse to "(UNKNOWN)" and lose the original error message that operators need to diagnose broken hooks.
648946bf52fc48ea: The `phase: "usage"` event incorrectly reports only the duration of the final successful model attempt rather than the total end-to-end latency across all fallback retries, causing latency undercounting in dashboards for any auto-reply run that triggers the fallback path.
62c4b6f050b25226: Using `void emitUserTranscriptUpdate()` instead of awaiting it allows the assistant's transcript update to arrive before the user's in session-history subscribers, causing the assistant reply to appear before the user's prompt when image attachments are involved.
5d76aec877d41b4e: Because the Copilot extension registers an empty model list, the `gpt-5.3-codex` forward-compat alias almost always falls through to a generic synthetic model instead of cloning the codex template's metadata, silently losing template-derived behavior.
40bc3efdeb303c55: The refactored catch block in `loadInternalHooks` discards `err.message` in favor of only `err.code`, silently swallowing actionable error details (e.g., syntax errors or failed imports) and collapsing them to `UNKNOWN`, which significantly hampers debugging.
c01daa931141f772: The silent failure detection incorrectly treats intentional `NO_REPLY` outcomes as errors, causing a fallback error message to be sent to users even when the agent is legitimately configured to stay silent.
4e3ada258fbc528e: When an agent directory name doesn't round-trip through normalization, the real on-disk path is discarded in favor of the normalized version, causing session listings to surface transcripts that subsequent detail lookups (key, timeseries, logs) cannot open because they reconstruct the wrong file path.
05db347a6da05df5: Although `sessionTarget` is now correctly propagated to the synthetic cron job, `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn` never actually uses it to resolve the target session, causing all non-`isolated` values like `"main"`, `"current"`, and `"session:<id>"` to behave identically and silently breaking operators who rely on those modes to resume a specific session.
