8a8cd8e3c6ddd36d: The "new installs only" guard is too weak because existing configs missing the `agents.defaults.memorySearch` key will also satisfy the preconditions after `applyAgentDefaults()` runs, causing current users to be unexpectedly opted into experimental session memory, MMR, and temporal decay features upon upgrading.
9c3b305a06e5f76b: A production import in the Telegram extension reaches outside its package boundary via a relative path (`../../../src/...`) instead of using the approved `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` surface, which violates import boundary rules and will cause the ingress chain to silently fail in standalone plugin installs where that path doesn't exist.
e48dc4509d2bf2ef: Inserting `applyMemorySearchDefaults` into the standard config pipeline causes read-only operations like `status` to unexpectedly trigger memory store initialization, creating SQLite files and starting file watchers on disk for users who never explicitly configured memory search.
397333ca23e880e9: The `<tool_call>` tag recovery fallback indiscriminately parses any tag-shaped snippet in plain-text responses, meaning example or explanatory tool-call markup in the model's reply will be silently executed as real tool invocations and stripped from the visible output.
01ab238983fcc972: The `num_ctx` option is extracted from `params.model` and passed to `installToolResultContextGuard()` unconditionally, meaning non-Ollama or OpenAI-compat models with `injectNumCtxForOpenAICompat: false` will have their tool results compacted against an incorrect synthetic context budget, potentially causing premature compaction or overflow on otherwise valid turns.
21f9a624373d7071: Mapping all HTTP 404 responses to `model_not_found` is too broad, causing misconfigured base URLs or route typos to be misclassified as missing models, which can trigger incorrect failover retries and incorrectly cool down healthy credentials.
7cac302692a96892: The OpenAI-completions Ollama compat transport only forwards `num_ctx` from `model.options` instead of the full options record, causing newly supported parameters like `top_k`, `top_p`, and `repeat_penalty` to silently have no effect on compat runs despite working correctly on the native Ollama transport.
1efdd6688b00d647: If a model emits a stray `<tool_call>` opener in explanatory text before a real tool call, the parser incorrectly pairs it with the closing tag of the genuine call, causing JSON parsing to fail and the cursor to advance past the real tool call, resulting in no tool calls being recovered.
c6316cb87d87bc70: The silent-reply suppression logic is only applied inside `finalizeTextDelivery` but is bypassed for threaded delivery targets (e.g., Telegram forum topics), causing the literal "NO_REPLY" token to leak to end users on those code paths.
e00e28f3b5aadac4: Catching and silencing all `resolveHooksConfig()` exceptions sets `hooksConfig` to `null` instead of failing fast, causing the gateway to boot in a broken half-enabled state where hook-adjacent services like Gmail watchers start successfully but inbound hook deliveries are silently dropped because no hook handler is mounted.
bc0c2c52b89c38ac: Changing `delivered` from `true` to `false` for silent reply tokens causes hook-dispatched agent runs to incorrectly post a fallback "NO_REPLY" summary to the main session instead of remaining silent, reintroducing a silent-token leak for shared delivery-contract callers.
f762c90a4cb00f0a: When sending media with a caption, a local media path allowlist violation is only caught after the caption text has already been sent, causing a partial send where retrying will duplicate the caption and leaving callers unable to treat the failure as a clean no-op.
354881ebcec7a894: Removing the `isBusy || props.sending` guard from the Stop button's render condition means the button can appear and remain clickable while disconnected, even though `handleAbortChat()` silently no-ops without a live connection, leaving users with a non-functional abort control and no access to send or new-session actions until the socket reconnects.
7a334ee88e764d40: On Windows, spawning `.cmd`/`.bat` wrapper scripts (e.g., `claude.cmd`, `npx.cmd`) directly via `child_process.spawn()` will fail on modern Node.js, because the code does not apply the existing `cmd.exe` routing logic that the repo already uses elsewhere for such executables.
89824a537be67229: Reading the request body before installing the body limit guard removes byte-limit and timeout protections, allowing oversized or slow-drip requests to exhaust memory and hold connections open on the Slack HTTP endpoint.
1cffcdb23725fc56: Because `applyModelDefaults()` always pre-populates `model.contextWindow` before this code runs, the new `options.num_ctx` user configuration is silently ignored in favor of the default context window value, making the intended override logic unreachable.
b5d32f3e4a9f211d: The `startsWith(trimmedProvider + "/")` check incorrectly treats provider-prefixed model IDs (e.g., `openrouter/hunter-alpha`) as already-qualified refs, causing the resolved server value to diverge from the catalog option key (e.g., `openrouter/openrouter/hunter-alpha`) and breaking model selection in the picker.
80b798bbd59da676: The new `fallbackOnErrors` field is added to the `AgentModelConfig` type but the corresponding Zod schema remains `.strict()` with only `primary` and `fallbacks` defined, meaning any user who attempts to configure this feature will receive a validation error and be unable to use it.
9cfb43fb4e3da51d: The new `DEFAULT_FALLBACK_STATUS_CODES` constant omits HTTP 529 (overloaded), so callers using `fallbackOnErrors: "default"` will silently fail on the first model instead of continuing the fallback chain when an Anthropic/Together-style overload response is received.
62e63a436cdda9a6: The newly added `fallbackOnErrors` parameter in `runWithModelFallback` is never actually read or used in the implementation, making it a no-op that silently fails to change which HTTP error codes trigger a model fallback retry.
f967d3cd8ca8ea9c: Adding `fallbackOnErrors` to the shared `AgentModelConfig` type falsely implies the option is supported for image, PDF, and image generation models, when in reality those code paths ignore the field and silently fall back to the old retry behavior.
d285a94c4f7d587b: The documentation falsely claims that Slack PDF uploads are processed through vision analysis, when the actual code explicitly excludes PDFs from image classification and prevents extracted PDF images from being forwarded to the model.
edc9ef177568b583: When `getStatusCode(err)` returns nothing, `shouldTriggerFallback()` falls back based solely on `reason !== null` regardless of mode, causing custom status allowlists to be silently bypassed for status-less `FailoverError`s (e.g., `model_not_found`), which can unintentionally exhaust alternate models on errors the user never opted into.
8590e47d9ee197d3: The documentation incorrectly marks image-only thread roots as supported for vision pipeline attachment propagation, when in fact the code drops root messages lacking text, meaning attachments from image/PDF-only thread starters will never be passed to subsequent replies.
5155bed373f46ecd: The troubleshooting documentation incorrectly directs users to configure `llm.providers` to resolve vision errors, but the attachment-analysis resolver actually reads from `cfg.agents?.defaults?.imageModel`, meaning users who follow this guidance will update the wrong key and still be unable to use Slack attachment analysis.
7c37fdc2c0a36e46: When a Slack thread starts with a file-only message and media hydration fails, the thread context is left with no body or media, causing replies that reference the parent file to reach the agent with no indication a file ever existed.
dcdfeaa9be16a14e: A capitalization-only edit to CHANGELOG.md inadvertently drops the Telegram/topics bullet point, causing the DM auto-topic-labeling feature from #51502 to be omitted from release notes entirely.
b4c2acc10703dfd8: The `sessions_manage` tool incorrectly forwards an `instructions` field to `sessions.compact`, which rejects it at runtime because the gateway's `SessionsCompactParams` schema only permits `key` and `maxLines` with `additionalProperties: false`, breaking the guided-compaction path entirely.
a01234228f954055: When `current` is a bare alias, the select option value is rewritten to the full `modelId` but the bound `<select>` value in `agents-panels-overview.ts` still uses the raw alias, causing a mismatch where no option is selected and users may unknowingly overwrite their current model.
cfd72ff756fb2ebd: Using `importOriginal` in the `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-runtime` mock inadvertently re-imports the full reply runtime module graph (including the heavy `reply.js` stack), negating the performance isolation the mock was intended to provide and risking OOM failures on low-memory Vitest lanes when only `HEARTBEAT_TOKEN` and a stubbed `getReplyFromConfig` are actually needed.
1ac5c4af2b409274: The self-reset deadlock guard in `sessions-manage-tool.ts` can be bypassed because `resolvedKey` and `effectiveRequesterKey` are compared without normalization, allowing a canonical main session calling `sessions_manage` with the `"main"` alias to slip past the check and still trigger the dangerous `sessions.reset` path.
5c529cb2f5cc05be: The `compact` action in `sessions-manage-tool.ts` incorrectly calls the `sessions.compact` gateway RPC (which only trims transcript lines) instead of the `/compact` runtime compaction flow, meaning it silently discards transcript data rather than performing the documented summarization-aware semantic compaction.
6081287ee1d04f82: Hardcoding `sessionTarget: "main"` in `runAgentAttempt` overrides legitimate non-main session keys (e.g., cron sessions), causing `handleAgentEnd` to misclassify cron-session failures as main-session failures and breaking the provenance tracking this change intended to introduce.
68e3dfe8232426e1: The fallback logic incorrectly classifies any non-cron run with a truthy `sandboxSessionKey` (which can be a synthetic or auto-derived identifier) as a `"main"` session target, causing background and tooling runs to be mislabeled and potentially triggering wrong alerts or remediation paths.
e1ec1deb4dcd4eae: The `formatTransportErrorCopy()` call is gated inside the `ERROR_PREFIX_RE` block, so bare transport errors like `socket hang up` or `connect ECONNREFUSED` that lack an `Error:` prefix are never rewritten into user-facing copy and pass through unchanged to assistant streams and history replies.
21e7d83a39f6dc3a: The `registry.has(normalizedKey)` guard in `buildMediaUnderstandingRegistry` incorrectly prevents `describeImage` from being attached to pre-seeded core providers (e.g., `groq`, `deepgram`), so even when a config declares image-capable models for those providers, the image tool remains unavailable because `describeImage` is never registered.
56b16618c1c0cb42: Removing the `next.totalTokens = undefined` line causes stale token counts to persist when a provider omits a fresh snapshot, leading status and context-report surfaces to display outdated token totals instead of showing `unknown` or the correct updated value.
75ac1d31710726a0: The `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens()` call cannot correctly suppress stale token counts because the `session` object is constructed without the `totalTokensFresh` metadata, causing `/context` commands to display misleading cached token values after output-only turns or model fallbacks.
2ce319c29ffc456d: Removing the `next.totalTokens = undefined` line leaves a stale token count in place when a provider only reports partial usage data, causing the parent-fork guard to incorrectly treat compacted or model-switched sessions as exceeding the fork limit and skip history forking.
a838496e2bf81e32: The early return added when `totalTokensFresh === false` causes `resolveTotalTokens()` to discard valid input/output token totals before reaching the existing fallback calculation, resulting in `subagents_list` reporting no token counts at all for runs where only partial usage data is available.
0332969fbc0530c5: Removing the `next.totalTokens = undefined` line allows stale token counts to persist when only `totalTokensFresh` is set to false, causing subagent UX surfaces that read `totalTokens` directly without checking freshness to display outdated context usage instead of showing it as unknown.
76e7e33d00b0ed2a: The new `totalTokensEstimate` field is not cleared during session resets or compaction, causing stale token counts from previous conversations to persist and be exposed in `openclaw sessions --json` output, which can mislead automation consuming that data.
df7546b2f5bf6bbd: When `totalTokensFresh` is undefined (e.g., on legacy session rows), coercing it to `false` in `/context json` creates an internally contradictory response where a non-null `totalTokens` is paired with `totalTokensFresh: false`, diverging from the `sessions --json` command which treats missing freshness as fresh.
e5e8d89d4402bdeb: When `totalTokensFresh` is false and the provider emits no usage, `resolveTotalTokens()` silently falls back to stale `inputTokens`/`outputTokens` left over from a previous run, causing token counts in `/subagents list` and completion stats to be incorrectly reported as belonging to the current turn.
0155514a305ad54f: When upgrading legacy sessions that have `totalTokens` but no `totalTokensEstimate`, the new code fails to seed `totalTokensEstimate` from the existing cached value because it clears `totalTokens` and only writes `totalTokensEstimate` from the current result, which is `undefined` in the very cases it's meant to preserve — causing those sessions to lose their cached token count until a fresh prompt snapshot is taken.
ce561aa97a477386: The TUI fallback to `entry.totalTokensEstimate` will never display estimated token counts because the upstream status payload producer in `status.summary.ts` never populates `totalTokensEstimate` in the session summary objects, leaving context usage rendering as `?` for zero-usage and output-only cases.
501459eb556b9907: The TUI footer fallback to `totalTokensEstimate` is ineffective because the session state updater in `tui-session-actions.ts` never copies `totalTokensEstimate` from the entry, so the field remains undefined and the footer continues to display incorrect token counts on zero-usage turns.
0598e58c4ab7cb5a: Using `||` instead of `??` causes a legitimate zero token count to be treated as falsy and incorrectly replaced by a stale `totalTokensEstimate`, meaning just-reset or empty sessions will display outdated token totals in `/status`.
16bfc68d481ad7c2: The removal of the `CLLocationManager.locationServicesEnabled()` guard from `currentPermissions()` causes the gateway to incorrectly report location as available even when system-wide Location Services are disabled, leading to failed location requests at runtime.
133698eb17d102cc: Caching `CLLocationManager.locationServicesEnabled()` at initialization instead of calling it live on each snapshot introduces a regression where the published location availability can become stale if system-wide Location Services are toggled without triggering an app-level authorization change.
610d0d67e88810e2: When both `tool_choice` and `toolChoice` exist in the merged `extraParams` object, `resolveAliasedParamValue` always favors the snake_case key regardless of source precedence, allowing a lower-priority default to override a higher-priority agent-specific setting.
3e000fad87ff020f: Changing the install kind to `"pipx"` breaks skill installation because the parser only supports `brew|node|go|uv|download`, causing the install entry to be silently dropped and leaving users without a valid auto-install path for the skill.
5dbd1a1d929edc48: Loading all media upfront in `loadAndClassifyMedia` before any send call means a single failed media URL will abort the entire 2-10 item media group, eliminating the previous partial-delivery behavior where successfully loaded items were still sent.
f0464b39a75f5349: The `deliverMediaGroupReply` function buffers all album attachments in memory simultaneously before making any API calls, which can cause severe memory spikes and potential OOM crashes compared to the previous sequential load/send approach, especially when handling multiple large video files that Telegram supports up to 100MB each.
75067c85d2f0eedb: The new album grouping path passes raw HTTP media URLs directly to Telegram's `sendMediaGroup` instead of fetching and uploading the bytes, causing silent failures for non-public or internally-reachable URLs (e.g., Tailscale or private CDN) that Telegram's servers cannot access, with no per-item fallback.
9351c9b312d07867: When sending an album with inline buttons and a URL caption where `linkPreview` is disabled, the new carrier `sendTelegramText()` call fails to forward `params.linkPreview`, causing Telegram to unexpectedly display a link preview that the caller explicitly requested to suppress.
4bbf9f48182bc75a: The extension-based fast path for groupability checks bypasses MIME validation, causing mislabeled or redirecting media URLs to be incorrectly forced into `sendMediaGroup` as photo/video instead of falling back to the appropriate send method, which can break entire reply delivery.
b79c678377ba5d8b: The fix emits an assistant update for tool-call-only responses, but downstream relays in the gateway and agent stream handlers silently drop assistant events when the delta text is empty, so the tool-call-only signal still never reaches clients unless the emitted event is enriched with tool-call metadata or another non-empty marker.
4ccf613140a416b5: The `standardFontDataUrl` is resolved using `import.meta.url` relative to the source file location, causing the path to incorrectly point to `src/node_modules/...` instead of the repo root when running from source, which will break font loading for image-only PDFs in dev workflows.
65983f5cd9b81c6b: The `isSameOAuthIdentity()` function fails to compare `accountId`, so two different OAuth accounts sharing the same `profileId` but no email can be incorrectly treated as identical, allowing one agent to overwrite another account's tokens and causing agents to authenticate as the wrong user.
43a092f73125d254: The `hasToolCalls` check incorrectly relies on an ID-based extraction helper that returns false for valid tool calls missing an `id` field, meaning tool-call-only assistant responses from providers that omit IDs will still cause `handleMessageEnd` to silently skip emitting the assistant update, leaving the downstream idle/no-progress bug unfixed.
df2f6d263983e408: The OAuth refresh lock path sanitization replaces all non-alphanumeric characters with underscores, causing distinct profile IDs like `openai-codex:work/test` and `openai-codex_work:test` to collide on the same lock file, which can cause unrelated credential refreshes to incorrectly block each other and fail with a timeout.
424beff8d11f164e: In-flight OAuth refresh requests are deduplicated by `profileId` alone, so two concurrent callers with different OAuth identities that share the same `profileId` will incorrectly collapse onto one refresh promise, causing the second caller to receive the wrong account's API key.
ecfd755e86924378: The credential refresh logic only persists updated OAuth tokens when the expiry timestamp increases, but since providers can rotate access and refresh tokens without changing the expiry, the old (potentially invalidated) tokens remain in the store, causing repeated failed refresh attempts with a stale refresh token on subsequent requests.
942598c3e30b1290: When a sub-agent refreshes an OAuth token and writes back to the main auth store, it may use a stale in-memory snapshot instead of the current on-disk file, causing unrelated credential or usage updates made after the snapshot was activated to be silently clobbered.
02725fc123f13956: The `isSameOAuthIdentity` function incorrectly treats credentials with a missing `email` field as matching, allowing cross-agent token write-back to silently overwrite the wrong OAuth account when providers don't populate email.
2395ac50fbb70af6: Because the per-store lock is released before the network refresh completes, `writeCredentialToAgentStore()` can overwrite a newer, legitimately advanced OAuth credential with a stale token pair when a concurrent writer updates the profile in the interim.
6a2aeee3f8a988bd: The write-back logic only propagates refreshed OAuth tokens through the main store, so agents sharing a profile that exists only in non-main `auth-profiles.json` files can still race on token refresh and trigger `refresh_token_reused` errors despite the new global lock.
dec088b919f228db: The OAuth token refresh no longer holds the file-level lock that serializes auth-store mutations, so a concurrent profile deletion or re-authentication can be silently overwritten when the in-flight `performOAuthRefresh()` later persists its stale credentials via `writeCredentialToAgentStore()`.
bbfd1c1c1311a2d8: When a session migrates to the new `totalTokensEstimate` field, a zero-usage or missing-usage run clears `totalTokens` without seeding `totalTokensEstimate` from the legacy value, causing the token count to be lost entirely for pre-existing sessions instead of being preserved.
f9fb753ffafa5c5e: Although `totalTokensEstimate` is now correctly included in the JSON output, the human-readable `status` table still reads only from `sess.totalTokens` (which is `freshTotal ?? null`), so sessions with `totalTokensFresh === false` will continue to display as `unknown` even when a valid estimate is available.
59976be0a2b8554a: The `sessions_list` tool still serializes only `entry.totalTokens` instead of falling back to `totalTokensEstimate`, so sessions on providers that omit usage data will now expose zero context size to agents even though a preserved estimate exists.
5f28f5609d4099e3: The `totalTokensEstimate` fallback added to the TUI footer will never actually display because the estimate field is never stored in `GatewaySessionList` or copied by `applySessionInfo()`, leaving the footer stuck showing "unknown/..." after zero-usage runs.
75b8368aaf612665: Because `readUsageFromSessionLog()` unconditionally sets `totalTokensFresh: true`, the newly added `logUsage.totalTokensFresh` condition always evaluates to true, causing transcript-derived token counts (which may omit cache or override tokens) to replace more accurate stored totals, leading `/status` and `session_status` to under-report context usage.
c5e7b77f463e1fe9: The zero-usage branch in `persistSessionUsageUpdate()` unconditionally clears `totalTokens` and `totalTokensFresh` without backfilling `totalTokensEstimate`, causing sessions migrated from older stores to permanently lose their last known context size and display as "unknown" in status and UI views.
ffe66eb5fd6d0b19: Because `readUsageFromSessionLog()` unconditionally marks all transcript snapshots as fresh, the new `logUsage.totalTokensFresh` condition always fires, causing `/status` to overwrite a higher preserved `totalTokensEstimate` with a smaller, incomplete transcript token count and reintroducing the under-reporting the change was meant to fix.
63eb6a6dff205c28: When a zero-usage run updates a session with a new model or provider but unset legacy totals fields, the code incorrectly copies the previous model's `totalTokens` into `totalTokensEstimate`, causing inaccurate context size displays and potentially misguiding the `parentForkMaxTokens` guard under the new model's identity.
7e8d9fb606145dce: The `handleStdout` function ignores the new `detectCursorKeyMode()` helper and instead uses two independent `includes` checks to update `session.cursorKeyMode`, causing the wrong final mode to be set when both `smkx` and `rmkx` sequences appear in the same PTY chunk because the `rmkx` branch always wins regardless of byte order.
952f695376820b1f: Because PTY stdout is delivered in arbitrary chunks, checking each chunk independently for `\x1b[?1h`/`\x1b[?1l` sequences can miss split sequences or ignore a later `rmkx` in the same chunk, leaving `session.cursorKeyMode` stale and causing `process send-keys` to emit the wrong arrow-key encoding.
630d5ca9651e0481: Removing the `assertSchtasksAvailable` preflight check causes unhandled `execSchtasks` spawn errors (e.g., `ENOENT`) to escape `readScheduledTaskRuntime` before the Startup-folder fallback can be reached, turning graceful fallback/unknown responses into hard failures in environments where `schtasks` is unavailable.
2f433bf48630dc8c: The `totalTokensEstimate` field was added to only one of the two `sessions.changed` broadcast payloads, leaving the second lifecycle-event broadcast (used by subagent create/status events) still omitting the field, which causes WebSocket/SSE clients to continue receiving null token counts until they perform a full refetch.
9f116dd5b64d68cb: The smkx/rmkx detection logic only inspects the current PTY chunk without buffering carry-over bytes, so if the escape sequence `\x1b[?1h` or `\x1b[?1l` is split across two chunks, it will never be recognized and `session.cursorKeyMode` will remain stale for the rest of the session, causing arrow keys to be encoded incorrectly.
42ca16f0ae5c7c19: The workflow triggers a paid Claude API job with write permissions for any user who mentions `@claude` in an issue or comment, allowing untrusted actors on public repositories to consume API budget and cause unintended write-side effects without any collaborator or maintainer authorization check.
04c6b027caeb03ba: When a session is started with `background: true`, `process send-keys` may immediately fall back to normal-mode CSI escape sequences before the PTY application has emitted its `smkx` startup sequence, causing arrow and navigation keys to be misinterpreted in the exact launch-then-interact workflow this change intends to fix.
e6bdf455cd74d870: Once a stream is registered, `playTts()` unconditionally skips the TwiML `<Say>` fallback for calls without telephony TTS configured, causing all audio responses to silently fail on deployments that rely on that fallback path.
5a89adf13fb59879: Moving the `metadata.initialMessage` cleanup to after `await speak(...)` instead of before it creates a race condition where concurrent or duplicate stream connect events can trigger the initial greeting multiple times before the field is cleared.
58cf747c0086d1ee: Because `gateway.handshakeTimeoutMs` is classified as restart-only in the reload plan and hot-reload gateways silently drop restart-required changes, updating this config key at runtime will have no effect until the gateway is manually restarted.
174074189e226c51: The new range validation (1000–120000 ms) silently clamps previously valid `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` environment variable values to the 15s default, breaking existing deployments that relied on out-of-range values without any warning or error.
a1bc24c8a13f2330: The new `sessionsList` options added to the `GatewayConfig` type are not reflected in the Zod runtime validation schema, which uses strict mode, causing config validation to fail and preventing startup when these documented keys are used.
5ea7512590e163d6: The `autoTopicLabel` setting is looked up only from the top-level `channels.telegram.direct[chatId]` config, bypassing account-scoped overrides and the `"*"` wildcard fallback, so `autoTopicLabel: false` can be silently ignored and unexpectedly trigger costly LLM rename calls on first DM messages.
ab8dfba332f2a7e7: The `prewarmSessionUsageCache` function performs significant synchronous work before its first `await`, meaning that even though it's called fire-and-forget, it still blocks the startup call stack and can delay gateway readiness when many sessions exist.
0f10e53fa273d06a: During prewarm, `needsTranscriptUsageFallback` is evaluated without the runtime subagent model (unlike `buildGatewaySessionRow` which uses `subagentRun?.model`), causing restored subagent sessions to be incorrectly skipped during cache warming and re-incurring transcript-read latency on the first real call to `sessions.list`.
ba4768d6bb112007: During prewarm, reading full transcript files concurrently into memory with `fs.promises.readFile` can multiply peak memory usage across up to 64 parallel tasks, risking memory pressure or OOM crashes on large deployments.
50da6feb5932d5cd: The `needsTranscriptUsageFallback` check in prewarm omits the `subagentRun?.model` fallback that `buildGatewaySessionRow` passes to `resolveSessionModelIdentityRef`, causing prewarm to incorrectly skip usage warming for child sessions with no persisted model/cost and forcing the first post-startup `sessions.list` call to perform expensive on-demand transcript reads instead.
e6c253eb4189c04b: The new transport architecture table incorrectly classifies Twitch as using an outbound WebSocket connection when it actually uses IRC/TCP, contradicting the repo's own Twitch documentation, which could cause users to misconfigure firewall rules or debug via the wrong network path.
16886c3a81cccdb5: The Mattermost row in the new connection architecture table incorrectly depicts the channel as outbound-only, omitting the inbound HTTP callback mode required when native slash commands are enabled, which could mislead operators into misconfiguring their firewall rules or reverse proxy setup.
9d13addc1a97de17: The documentation incorrectly implies all Discord and Slack Socket Mode traffic flows over a single WebSocket connection, when in reality outbound messages still use HTTPS APIs, which could lead operators to misconfigure firewall or proxy rules and misdiagnose send failures.
bb332b7bf2c19b6a: The Tlon channel is incorrectly documented as plain "Outbound HTTP" in the connection architecture table, when it actually relies on a long-lived Server-Sent Events (SSE) subscription that requires SSE-friendly streaming egress, which could lead to misconfigured proxies or incorrect idle-timeout settings.
59b73dee27478a5e: The documentation incorrectly groups Twitch and Nostr under a transport mode where outbound replies are sent over HTTPS, when in reality both channels send replies over WebSocket connections, which could mislead operators into configuring incorrect firewall or proxy egress rules.
c55ecd3cb78601b0: In `splitMinimaxToolCalls()`, when MiniMax wraps multiple `<invoke>` elements inside a single `<minimax:tool_call>` block, only the first invocation is captured and processed because the regex matches the entire wrapper once and advances the cursor past it, silently dropping all subsequent tool calls in that wrapper.
6f7180373ff00d80: XML entity sequences like `&`, `<`, and `>` are not unescaped before being parsed into tool call arguments, causing any MiniMax payloads containing characters such as `&`, `<`, or `>` to pass corrupted, escaped literals to the tool instead of the intended values.
42457fc602e3d039: When a `<minimax:tool_call>` block contains both a valid and a malformed `<invoke>` element, the malformed sibling is incorrectly preserved as a plain text block after wrapper removal, causing raw `<invoke>` markup to be surfaced to users instead of being stripped.
837827c8c7962720: The XML parameter parser fails to convert scalar boolean values like "true" and "false" from string to their proper boolean types, causing tools that rely on strict boolean checks to behave incorrectly—treating string "true" as falsy or string "false" as truthy.
e8cf3381b71f56d7: The `prewarmSessionUsageCache` function resolves model identity without passing the runtime subagent model fallback used by `buildGatewaySessionRow`, causing it to incorrectly skip cache warming for restored child sessions and reintroduce the transcript-read latency it was meant to eliminate.
932acc511e2a8eb8: The `parseXmlParameterValue()` function unconditionally trims all parameter values, causing whitespace-sensitive tool arguments (e.g., indented code blocks or text with leading/trailing newlines) to be silently corrupted before being forwarded to tools like `runMessageAction()`.
65de3cd9c422e6e7: The `splitMinimaxToolCalls()` function's regex requires both opening and closing `<minimax:tool_call>` tags, causing malformed MiniMax payloads that only contain a closing wrapper tag to be silently dropped instead of parsed and executed as tool calls.
fa761cfb46fe5d9f: The MiniMax malformed closing-tag recovery path applies a regex over the entire preceding text block, which can incorrectly match and execute tool calls from quoted examples or other prose, causing unintended tools to be dispatched.
96358ece43612bec: The `unescapeXmlEntities` function double-decodes already-escaped XML entities (e.g., `&lt;` becomes `<` instead of `<`) because `&` is replaced before other entity patterns, silently corrupting tool payloads that contain intentionally escaped content.
064e3b554b8fd76e: The `parseXmlParameterValue()` function incorrectly coerces all digit-only string parameters to numbers, breaking tools that expect string identifiers like message routing IDs, Discord snowflakes, and phone numbers, which can cause precision loss or failed/misdirected tool calls.
57eb3e4686d1c09e: The `splitMinimaxToolCalls()` function incorrectly promotes every `<invoke>` block in a message to an executable tool call whenever a `minimax:tool_call` marker is present anywhere, rather than scoping execution only to `<invoke>` blocks nested within valid MiniMax wrapper elements, allowing unintended tool dispatch from quoted or prose examples.
237050b8af50f186: An incorrect index into `closeMatches` causes `extractThinkingFromTaggedStream()` to select the wrong closing `</think>` tag when stray leading close tags are present, leaking raw control-tag text into the reasoning stream instead of properly identifying the last closed `<think>` block.
fa31da30366b1930: The `findCodeRegions()` helper only recognizes triple-backtick fences, so a four-backtick code block is misidentified as an unclosed triple-backtick fence that masks everything through EOF, causing `splitMinimaxToolCalls()` to miss any `<minimax:tool_call>` wrapper that follows and silently drop the tool call instead of executing it.
6ba88d86e57db2d3: When `sessions_spawn(..., thread=true)` is called from a top-level Matrix room with no existing thread, the session incorrectly binds to the entire room instead of creating a dedicated child thread, causing the spawned session to hijack all non-threaded messages in that room.
24059fd040c960e9: When a reply contains inline `<think>` tags with text on both sides (e.g., `Okay.<think>…</think>Done`), splitting them into separate `text` blocks causes `extractAssistantText()` to rejoin them with `"\n"` instead of preserving the original inline sentence, adding spurious line breaks to user-visible responses.
3fc483b57066ce87: The Matrix subagent hooks are registered via a fire-and-forget dynamic `import()`, creating a race condition window after gateway start or plugin reload where `sessions_spawn(..., thread:true)` calls can fail nondeterministically because the plugin loader doesn't await registration and the subagent spawner immediately rejects requests when no hook is present.
fcf5d11007375ca6: When a user resets their model selection to "Default," `rememberResolvedChatModelRecent` is called unconditionally, causing the resolved default model to be incorrectly recorded as an explicit recent choice and pushed to the top of the recents list, displacing the user's actual selections.
90f7a15ad9766f0a: When a Matrix account goes offline, its bindings are dropped from memory but persist on disk, so if a child session is deleted while the account is offline, the `subagent_ended` cleanup never finds and removes the stale binding, causing it to be reloaded from `thread-bindings.json` on the next restart.
71b541b53d3b4988: Bundled providers like `byteplus`, `volcengine`, and `google-gemini-cli` are missing from `BUILTIN_PROVIDER_IDS`, causing `isCustomProvider()` to incorrectly return true for first-party catalog entries and `sortRankedChatModelOptions()` to incorrectly sort them above genuine built-in providers.
47cf7c22338aa473: The `maxAnnounceChars` truncation logic appends an extra suffix string after slicing to the character limit, causing the final output to exceed the configured cap and potentially fail delivery on platforms with strict size limits like Telegram.
0b8e2727ddadf72b: Adding both `"text/xml": ".xml"` and `"application/xml": ".xml"` to `EXT_BY_MIME` causes the reversed `MIME_BY_EXT` map to overwrite the `text/xml` entry with `application/xml`, so any `.xml` file will resolve to the wrong MIME type in `detectMime()`.
2428a0e610f9a57c: Because `loadPluginManifestRegistry()` retains duplicate plugin IDs for both bundled and workspace-overriding plugins, a shadowed bundled web-search plugin (e.g., `google`) is still included in `compatPluginIds`, causing it to be incorrectly auto-allowlisted and preventing the expected "not in allowlist" validation warning from ever triggering.
258ae6f8edd64009: If `manager.persist()` fails after `setBindingRecord()` has already stored the binding in memory, the stale in-memory binding is never cleaned up, causing the room/thread to keep routing messages to a session that never successfully started until the Matrix monitor restarts.
66fb1a164bce7c79: The new `hookContext` hard-codes the transient `messageChannelId` for Discord DMs instead of the stable `user:${author.id}` key used by the inbound path, causing `message_sent` hooks to be attributed to a different conversation than `message_received` events and breaking per-conversation plugins on all Discord direct-message replies.
dc284381bdf7c4f7: The `onDelivered`/`hookContext` fix for `message_sent` emission is opt-in only, leaving several extension reply paths (WhatsApp, MS Teams, BlueBubbles) still bypassing `deliver.ts` without the new hooks, so issue #47472 remains unresolved for those channels.
c154677b3dc876a6: The new `hookContext` passes the raw `chatId` instead of Feishu's canonical `feishuTo` identifier (e.g., `user:${senderOpenId}` for DMs, `chat:${chatId}` for groups), causing `message_sent` hook events to emit a mismatched `conversationId` that won't correlate with preceding `message_received`/`inbound_claim` events, breaking any plugin that relies on conversation-level event matching.
57af51d891ceaf08: The replay loader unconditionally discards persisted queues older than 5 minutes, defeating the purpose of persistence by causing message loss whenever restart downtime exceeds that hardcoded TTL.
8c99a9ee4000551a: After persisting followup queues to disk on shutdown, the in-memory queue is never cleared, causing duplicate message processing when in-process restarts replay both the live in-memory queue and the newly written disk snapshot.
098651a75cdd788a: The `rust_random_bytes` tool incorrectly calls `nativeAddon.randomBytes`, which does not exist in the native addon — the correct exported function is `secureRandom` — causing a `TypeError` at runtime on first use.
634371cc29786fe7: The `rust_url_encode` function calls `nativeAddon.urlEncode`, which does not exist as an export in the Rust addon, causing every invocation to fail at runtime with a `TypeError` due to the missing function.
261ef7c70b07e8dc: The AES-GCM nonce tracker purges entries after 60 seconds, allowing previously used nonces to be reaccepted and potentially reused with the same key, which is a catastrophic cryptographic failure that completely breaks confidentiality and integrity guarantees.
6625d1ddae45f45e: `SharedStateProcessor.clear()` zeroizes the buffer but never truncates the vector, so the buffer appears to still contain data (as zeroed bytes), which can incorrectly block future `addData()` calls and cause `getData()` to return stale zero-filled data.
8619320c08cfa629: The `handle_webhook` function is incomplete — its struct literal, match branch, and function body are never closed — preventing the Rust addon from compiling and blocking any build that cannot rely on a prebuilt binary.
dcb2b2af696e7eaf: The `register()` function resolves the native addon path relative to `src/index.ts`, pointing to a non-existent `extensions/rust-plugin/src/native/index.cjs` file, which causes a runtime throw before registration completes and makes all Rust tools and routes unavailable.
8dfb6c405277ca7b: The fix for resolving built-in channel names from session keys silently falls back to `(unknown)` in production because the `sandbox` command is excluded from plugin loading and `normalizeAnyChannelId` depends on an empty plugin registry at runtime, even though tests pass due to a pre-seeded fake registry in `test/setup.ts`.
32980bc05390a82b: The `prettify_json` function accepts an unbounded caller-supplied `indent` value that drives per-line string expansion via `" ".repeat(...)`, enabling a denial-of-service attack through massive memory allocations even on small JSON payloads, and should be clamped to a safe maximum before use.
f09fe4df0604e448: The dynamic import for the native addon uses a relative path (`./native/index.cjs`) that resolves incorrectly from `src/index.ts`, pointing to a non-existent file instead of the actual loader at `extensions/rust-plugin/native/index.cjs`, causing the plugin to throw an error and fail to initialize before any tools are registered.
1fb2dbd165591333: The `resolveTeamGroupId` function unnecessarily calls `GET /teams/{id}` before fetching messages, requiring extra `Team.ReadBasic.All` permissions that aren't needed for channel message reads, causing silent failures and the feature never activating in tenants that only grant `ChannelMessage.Read.All`.
17f7c36d418f7656: After this patch, `resolveTaskScriptPath()` resolves under `$OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw` instead of the OS home, causing `readScheduledTaskCommand()` to fail to locate pre-existing Windows Scheduled Task scripts and leaving the real gateway running while `stop`/`restart` falsely report success.
669fceb7b6329406: By making `resolveHomeDir()` prefer `OPENCLAW_HOME`, service-manager paths for launchd and systemd are inadvertently redirected away from the real OS home directory, breaking daemon install, status, and management for any user who sets `OPENCLAW_HOME` to a non-home location.
b122027b54b90273: When a provider sends a legitimate zero-token context snapshot, `deriveSessionTotalTokens()` returns `undefined`, causing the code to fall back to the previous run's stale token estimate instead of correctly reflecting the reset session as empty.
9ec3c4ff6f954bf5: Removing the default media extraction path for silent tools breaks automatic delivery of voice and image attachments, because tools like `tts` and `image_generate` rely on tool-result `MEDIA:`/`details.path` data being converted into `payload.mediaUrls` when summaries are off, causing successful calls to produce no outbound message.
53b941e92f015819: When a task environment only contains `OPENCLAW_PROFILE` but is missing `OPENCLAW_HOME` (as in pre-patch installations), the early-return branch discards the caller shell's config root variables and falls back to the default home directory, causing `gateway stop`/`restart` to resolve the wrong `openclaw.json` and potentially leave the Windows scheduled task running despite reporting success.
e943b6fe20c17ff9: When `OPENCLAW_HOME` is changed or unset after installation, `resolveTaskScriptCandidates()` fails to locate the originally installed script path, causing `stop`/`restart` commands and Startup-folder relaunches to target the wrong script and potentially leave the actual gateway process running.
5d55787175c8fd04: The `persistSessionUsageUpdate()` guard incorrectly requires `promptTokens > 0`, so when a provider reports `promptTokens: 0` after a session reset, the function falls through to the stale/clear branch instead of persisting an explicit empty snapshot, causing `/status`, gateway rows, and TUI views to display outdated or `unknown` token counts instead of `0`.
c08dcad9ebf81e45: Changing the `hasNonzeroUsage` predicate from `v > 0` to `v >= 0` causes all-zero usage records to be treated as valid nonzero usage, which can cause `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded()` to latch onto a zero-valued trailing transcript entry instead of the real prior API call's usage snapshot, potentially preventing memory compaction from triggering even when the prompt is near the threshold.
ecbe19547dbeb9af: Returning `0` from `derivePromptTokens()` is ineffective for error paths because `buildErrorAgentMeta()` drops falsy zero values during its spread, causing downstream session writers to retain stale prompt token estimates instead of clearing them.
57804a6e983159ff: Setting `totalTokensFresh = true` before checking `if (totalTokens > 0 || !cronSession.sessionEntry.totalTokensFresh)` means a fresh zero-token snapshot can never overwrite a stale `totalTokensEstimate`, causing session status to incorrectly revert to the old pre-reset context size instead of reflecting the actual zero.
95e3255c5de231ed: Changing the condition from `> 0` to `>= 0` in `derivePromptTokens` causes it to incorrectly return `0` instead of `undefined` when no prompt-side token breakdown is provided, leading to session storage and status displays falsely reporting an empty context for backends that only emit a total token count.
2fa858c07c61d418: Changing the usage predicate from `> 0` to `>= 0` causes `hasNonzeroUsage` to match all-zero delivery-mirror footer entries, which can trick transcript-derived memory-flush logic into reading zeroed token counts and incorrectly skipping context compaction even when the model's context window is near its limit.
9d2b02f851a5c13b: When a provider reports only `promptTokens`, the CLI session update path unconditionally overwrites `inputTokens` and `outputTokens` with `undefined` instead of preserving the previous counters, causing reused CLI and cron sessions to display `?/?` token counts despite the auto-reply path correctly preserving them.
fd5436ee9a498c45: When a run reports zero tokens after a previously active session, the code incorrectly overwrites the new zero value with the old token estimate, causing stale usage data to persist in session listings and status displays until a future positive-token run occurs.
f48e8fc383570df8: When a zero-token snapshot arrives for an upgraded session row, the legacy `totalTokens` field is incorrectly treated as current because `totalTokensFresh` is absent, causing the fallback logic to restore the old token total instead of reflecting the fresh zero and making session size displays revert to stale pre-upgrade values.
933fda196231d486: When a session's model is patched, the stale `totalTokensEstimate` is not cleared alongside `contextTokens`, causing the new token-count fallback logic to surface the old model's token usage against the new model's context window in session listings until fresh usage is written.
9f73c429e4d1acb6: The sessions_list tool serializer omits the `totalTokensFresh` flag, preventing callers from distinguishing reliable token counts from stale fallback estimates and potentially causing agents to make routing or cleanup decisions based on outdated context sizes.
1b0165284d217f8a: When a provider only reports `total_tokens` without an `input`/`output` breakdown, the code incorrectly preserves stale `inputTokens` and `outputTokens` from the previous session entry instead of clearing them to unknown, causing misleading token split data to appear in `/status` and session listings.
1ead1bd6608dd79a: When a session reports zero token usage after a previously nonzero session, the code ignores the freshly-computed `prevFresh` flag and incorrectly restores the old non-zero prompt size instead of persisting the explicit zero, causing stale context counts to be displayed until a positive snapshot arrives.
e7a0d98f7324e173: When the session store entry contains a stale zero token count, the new fallback ordering causes `buildGatewaySessionRow()` to emit `0` instead of the fresher transcript usage total, causing gateway session listings and TUI consumers to display incorrect live context figures.
1f19777faf8c646a: The `usedEstimate` flag is incorrectly derived from the presence of `totalTokensEstimate` rather than the winning branch of `resolveGatewaySessionTotalTokens()`, causing `totalTokensFresh` to be set to `false` even when the fresh transcript total wins, which leads downstream consumers like ACP to drop valid usage snapshots and lose the current context size.
73b9084df8e05142: In `buildStatusMessage()`, the token-count overwrite logic can replace a newer `totalTokensEstimate` (written during compaction) with an older snapshot from `readUsageFromSessionLog()`, causing `session_status` to report a stale pre-compaction prompt size instead of the current stored estimate.
0373ed6c247365aa: The change to allow `tokensAfter >= 0` lets a legitimate zero token count from zero-usage backends be persisted as `totalTokens = 0` with `totalTokensFresh = true`, causing the session to appear empty after compaction instead of falling back to the previously known token size.
9b4384969a8145d6: When `gateway restart` is run after a stop on macOS, the fallback unconditionally returns `null` on any `service.restart()` failure, swallowing actionable launchd bootstrap errors (e.g., GUI-session guidance) and causing headless/SSH/wrong-user invocations to be misdiagnosed as a missing service installation.
207a95076f8982dd: When a new session's first turn fails before startup, the previous session's `skillsSnapshot` is incorrectly persisted, causing subsequent retries to reuse the stale snapshot and potentially miss SKILL.md installs/removals or environment changes that the `/new` session reset was intended to pick up.
c76331e4499aab3e: When a post-`/new` or `/reset` turn fails before `resolvedThinkLevel` is computed, the session entry is written with a fresh ID but retains stale `thinkingLevel` and `verboseLevel` fields, causing the retry to incorrectly inherit the old `/think` or `/verbose` settings instead of starting from defaults.
901fe353a99421a4: After a successful restart re-bootstrap via the `onNotLoaded` fallback path, the service's `loaded` flag is never updated to `true`, causing `gateway restart --json` to incorrectly report `service.loaded: false` and misleading any automation that relies on the JSON status output.
0cd5613871cf1a02: Eagerly persisting the old session entry with an updated timestamp before the new turn completes means that if the turn fails midway, the store will incorrectly treat the stale session as current, causing subsequent retries to resume the old session instead of creating the requested new one.
85fdf90163bf3671: The README setup command uses an incorrect CLI syntax (`openclaw channel add synology-chat`) instead of the valid form with the plural subcommand and named flag (`openclaw channels add --channel synology-chat`), causing users who copy-paste it to immediately hit a CLI error before reaching the setup flow.
62aa7f6bbe5cbaf0: The `download` function for native Feishu documents exports a PDF but provides no reliable way for the agent to read its contents, since the read tool lacks PDF support and the dedicated PDF tool is optional, leaving exported documents effectively inaccessible on default installations.
b340da114480df91: The `requestBrowserApiJson()` wrapper never passes a timeout to `withTrustedWebToolsEndpoint`, so if Bright Data's control-plane API stalls, `brightdata_browser_*` calls can block indefinitely instead of failing within the configured timeout.
52f6b6146ec97c70: Setting `ssrfPolicy: { dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork: false }` on the sandbox browser config blocks loopback (`127.0.0.1`) CDP connections, causing `isChromeReachable()` and `getChromeWebSocketUrl()` to fail before the bridge can connect to Chrome and breaking all sandbox browser sessions.
898d24b3a087861f: The SSRF policy is hardcoded to `dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork: false` rather than being threaded through from user configuration, meaning any SSRF settings defined in `openclaw.json` or `SandboxBrowserSettings` will never reach sandboxed browser navigations.
06931bcee27a2710: The `brightdata_browser_network_requests` tool never clears `session.requests` after click- or submit-driven navigations, causing subsequent network history dumps to include stale traffic from the previous page.
68ee9ff2bcf9cf8c: The configured `webSearch.timeoutSeconds` value is never read, causing `runBrightDataSearch()` to always fall back to a hard-coded 30-second timeout and making the operator-facing config setting effectively non-functional.
b4cc089d5132e9c6: When a full Brave endpoint URL (e.g., `.../res/v1/web/search`) is provided as `baseUrl`, `resolveBraveEndpoint()` incorrectly appends the path segment again, producing a doubled and invalid URL like `.../res/v1/web/search/res/v1/web/search` that results in 404 errors.
ad5e4bd3e54b9504: When an unlocker zone doesn't exist, the bootstrap path automatically creates it with `ub_premium: true`, which silently enables higher-cost billing for premium domains on first use without any user opt-in or warning.
f73cf8e1c52758ec: Navigation metadata (page title and URL) returned directly as trusted tool output allows an attacker-controlled page to inject prompt-manipulation text that steers the model simply by being navigated to, before any content sanitization occurs.
a32a2e1647e7d4c6: Removing the `OPENAI_DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL` import from `embeddings-openai.ts` leaves the constant unresolved, breaking all OpenAI-backed memory embedding functionality at runtime when the module is evaluated.
77c6dfff347baf9e: When a Google search via Bright Data returns a non-JSON HTTP 200 response (e.g., an HTML error page), JSON parse failures are silently swallowed and returned as empty results, causing callers to incorrectly treat a failed request as a legitimate query with zero matches.
0edfd7f27720fbb7: The new `input.signal?.aborted` branch in `runTurn()` incorrectly calls `killProcessTree(child)` on turn cancellation, SIGTERMing the persistent session's owner process that should survive for reuse, causing warm-session loss and potential dead-session recovery on the next turn.
916325ee50e0e315: The `trading-agents` skill specifies a `pip` installer that is not supported by OpenClaw's whitelist of accepted install kinds (`brew`, `node`, `go`, `uv`, `download`), causing the install step to be silently dropped and leaving users unable to install the required `tradingagents` dependency through OpenClaw.
7283ab069dd5fcd3: The new `cdpPort` field for targeting a specific Chrome instance in `existing-session` profiles is not propagated through the profile creation API, `CreateProfileParams`, or the CLI, meaning profiles created via these supported paths will always fall back to auto-discovering an arbitrary Chrome instance instead of using the intended port.
e8e6d2e898541b16: The process-group cleanup via `killProcessTree()` is inadvertently skipped on successful (non-aborted) turn completions because `childStillRunning` is already false by the time the `finally` block runs, allowing detached grandchild subprocesses to leak in production.
aa780ebe08308a32: When `model.baseUrl` is undefined, `isDirectOpenAIBaseUrl` incorrectly returns false, causing `function.strict` to be stripped from native OpenAI requests that rely on the default endpoint, which can break structured tool calling for configurations that should fully support strict mode.
1f851e227e2c09f4: The CLI instructions documented in SKILL.md require navigating into a source checkout directory, but since the skill directs users to install via PyPI (`pip install tradingagents`), the documented CLI flow will fail because no local source tree exists and users should instead be directed to use the `tradingagents` console script entry point exposed by the installed package.
1ddb3b2c5f753189: Wrapping `register(api)` inside an `if (shouldActivate)` block causes non-activating plugin loads to skip registration entirely, resulting in empty registries that break provider resolution, web-search discovery, and runtime hooks for callers like `resolvePluginProviders()` that rely on non-activating loads to populate snapshot registries.
8624d2ff666fe12d: The new fallback logic for `targetStrictMode` incorrectly assigns `false` for direct Azure OpenAI (`*.openai.azure.com`) endpoints, causing `shouldStripOpenAIStrictFlag()` to strip the `strict` field from tool definitions before the Azure allowlist can run, which regresses structured tool calling for Azure users.
18ff196f4026775a: In oneshot ACP sessions, if the direct child process exits with code 0 but leaves a detached subprocess (e.g., Codex/Claude) running, the guard condition that checks for abort or a still-running child will skip `killProcessTree()`, leaking the orphaned process since the "preserve warm owner" rationale doesn't apply when the session is immediately closed after completion.
0a310d2d2685aca8: Unconditionally clearing `contextLookupProvider` for slash transcript model IDs forces model-only lookup in `resolveContextTokensForModel()`, causing `/session-status` to skip provider-configured context windows and incorrectly fall back to the 32k default for sessions where the context window is defined under a provider and bare model ID combination.
7ea6a9125541f925: The `normalizeModelCompat()` path unconditionally sets `supportsStrictMode = false` for all non-native OpenAI base URLs before `hasOpenRouterStrictToolSupport()` exceptions are ever evaluated, causing `tools[].function.strict` to be stripped from OpenRouter and Anthropic structured-output requests that should legitimately support it.
3519c793f4c267d4: `hasStoredLocalOperatorDeviceAuthToken()` does not validate that the stored token matches the current device identity, so a stale token left over from a regenerated `identity/device.json` can incorrectly suppress the normal-closure pairing fallback, causing `devices approve` to fail with `gateway closed (1000)` on loopback restarts.
264cdd360bfe6de0: The normal-closure pairing fallback guard only checks `device-auth.json` and fails to exclude shared-auth sessions (e.g., `--token`/`--password`), allowing a paired operator with limited scopes like `operator.pairing` to approve a pending request requiring elevated scopes such as `operator.admin` via the local `approveDevicePairing()` path, which skips the scope enforcement applied by the gateway RPC path.
b9a567214117f729: The postinstall rewrite logic unconditionally computes `nextProgram` from the npm package path, causing `needsRepair` to always return true for repo-backed LaunchAgents and silently repointing the service away from the local dev checkout during a routine global package install.
f9582958ef4c312b: The `lastReflectionBySession` map grows unboundedly because entries are never evicted in production, risking memory exhaustion in long-lived gateways with many unique sessions.
94aec4bafc13ea89: The refactored code removes the `applyAuthorizationHeaderForUrl` and `safeFetchWithPolicy` guards that previously stripped `Authorization` headers on redirects to non-allowlisted hosts, risking Graph bearer token leakage to arbitrary redirected destinations.
3739f7ba39fa35c8: The fallback logic incorrectly suppresses the normal closure pairing fallback for operators who have a stale shared/config token but a valid paired device token, because `resolveMutatingPairingFallbackAuth()` never accounts for the `AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH` device-token retry path in `GatewayClient`, leaving those users vulnerable to the restart failure the patch intended to fix.
b6b380f8f6b39ae2: The `handleFeedbackInvoke` function uses the raw `activity.conversation.id` without stripping the `;messageid=...` suffix that Teams can append, causing feedback events to be routed to the wrong session when the suffixed form is present, unlike the message handler which normalizes this ID correctly.
34563a27f1132652: During LaunchAgent repair for Bun-runtime installations, the code discards the valid Bun binary path already stored in the plist and attempts to re-resolve it via `which bun` in the npm lifecycle's restricted PATH, which can cause repairs to fail even when the original Bun binary is still accessible.
4501069df4eee69a: Making `appPrincipal` required in the Zod schema will break existing valid Google Chat deployments that omit it, even though the `app-url` and `project-number` webhook verification paths don't require it at runtime.
677833dfc17b5b17: Adding a `postinstall` script referencing `postinstall.mjs` to `package.json` will cause Docker builds following the documented steps to fail with `Cannot find module '/app/postinstall.mjs'` because the existing `COPY` instructions in the Docker documentation don't include `postinstall.mjs` before `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` is run.
000a3edea5ab87d5: Adding `docs/zh-CN/README.md` outside the established translation pipeline means future updates to the canonical root `README.md` will never be automatically propagated to this file, causing the translation to drift out of sync almost immediately.
1bf87c5c1f780d58: Making `appPrincipal` required in the Zod schema without updating the guided setup flow to collect it will cause the Google Chat configuration to fail validation and prevent saving during the setup wizard.
34ed508b4feea72b: The function returns an ambiguous raw model value prematurely when it encounters the first provider conflict, instead of continuing to scan the full catalog for a later entry that matches the preferred provider.
3c670a42af0a22d8: The Chinese README's navigation links use `../...` relative paths that escape the `docs/zh-CN/` directory and resolve to English documentation pages instead of the existing zh-CN equivalents, breaking the localized onboarding experience for Chinese readers.
1899df533fcf1299: The newly added `zh-CN/README.md` translation is orphaned because no existing navigation configs or entry points reference it, making it unreachable to users browsing the Chinese documentation.
992502bae5d1bd50: The `postCreateCommand` in the devcontainer config runs `npm install` instead of `pnpm install`, which in a pnpm workspace will skip all workspace packages and leave the environment in a broken state where build and test commands fail due to missing dependencies.
fa86db34cb96318e: The channel-level `replyToMode` override is applied in `prepare.ts` but not propagated to `buildSlackThreadingToolContext`, causing tool replies to still be sent in-thread instead of the channel root when a channel overrides an account-level `replyToMode: "all"` setting with `"off"`.
40d94fb90ada005a: The per-channel `replyToMode` override is never passed to the deferred reply resolver, causing queued followups and other deferred Slack replies in channels with a channel-specific `replyToMode` config to incorrectly fall back to the account-level threading mode instead.
5ebc40cc826c87d1: The new `z.preprocess` silently converts large numeric Discord snowflakes to incorrect string values due to JavaScript's floating-point precision limits, persisting corrupted IDs instead of rejecting them as the previous validation did.
86b86dc674fc00dd: When a turn is aborted via `input.signal`, the new code incorrectly kills the entire `prompt` process tree, destroying the persistent ACPX session that `cancelSession()` expects to remain alive and reusable, causing subsequent turns to lose conversation state.
7138ab7c82c086e7: The new `DiscordIdSchema` coercion accepts any JavaScript number (including floats, negatives, and exponent-form values) rather than only valid integer snowflakes, so invalid IDs like `-1` or `1.5` silently pass validation and are stored as strings that will never match in downstream Discord allowlist checks instead of surfacing a config error.
ed507a133ccd8d4b: The `prunePollParamsForNonPollAction` guard only exempts the `"poll"` action but not `"poll-vote"`, causing required fields like `pollId` and vote option identifiers to be deleted before plugin dispatch and breaking real poll-vote flows.
9c8206b6ba182dc6: When resolving a provider-qualified alias (e.g., `vercel-ai-gateway/sonnet`), the code returns the alias's target model ID verbatim without re-normalizing it through `parseModelRef`, causing the constructed provider/model key to skip required normalization steps and potentially produce an invalid or unrecognized model ID for providers like Vercel AI Gateway that expect a compound path format.
b31eec42c9b52986: The devcontainer's `postCreateCommand` runs `npm install` instead of `pnpm install`, immediately bootstrapping developers into a broken state that the project's own doctor script explicitly warns against, since this is a pnpm workspace that requires pnpm for a valid install.
f0081fb98c885602: On Windows, `resolveVersionFromPackage()` follows `realpathSync` on `.cmd` shims but never reaches the actual `acpx/package.json`, causing the fallback to return `null` and leaving the original `execution-failed` error unrecovered.
469a6d8ccce75964: The metadata-based version fallback triggers on any spawn error (e.g., EACCES, bad shebang, missing Node interpreter), causing `checkAcpxVersion()` to incorrectly return `ok: true` and skip the repair install, leaving the ACP backend permanently broken instead of self-healing.
64c17855ff65cdb8: Exporting `./plugin-sdk/telegram` and `./plugin-sdk/telegram-core` as package subpaths exposes Telegram-private implementation details as public API, creating an unintended long-term compatibility obligation that contradicts the repo's own test suite, which explicitly excludes these paths from the supported public subpath list.
5a2204c39b8d703b: When normalizing Gemini usage, `promptTokenCount` (which includes cached tokens) is mapped entirely to `input` without extracting `cachedContentTokenCount` into `cacheRead`, causing `estimateUsageCost()` to price cached tokens at the full input rate and report zero cache hits for any request using Gemini context caching.
ad8b1f98478b81b6: The import path rewrite from a relative `../../src/plugin-sdk/telegram.js` to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/telegram` will cause the plugin-sdk boundary test suite to fail because the associated guardrail baselines and fixture allowlists still reference the old relative import path and were not updated in tandem.
dfeaec8441133f8b: The new try/catch around `resolveMediaList()` doesn't fix the intended Discord CDN 404 failures (which are already handled internally per-attachment) and instead silently drops the entire media list when unexpected resolver bugs or malformed payloads occur.
d0aca42e17706062: The test mocks the entire `resolveMediaList()` helper to reject rather than testing the real fetch-failure path, meaning it only validates that an outer catch swallows exceptions and could pass even if actual Discord attachment fetch failures are mishandled deeper in the production code.
ce4a57980acc2670: The new try/catch around `resolveMediaList` silently swallows unexpected resolver errors as verbose-only log entries instead of surfacing them as worker failure signals, causing malformed payloads or future regressions to silently drop media in non-verbose deployments rather than triggering the existing error-handling infrastructure.
55fecf1c3ddd15eb: The workspace override is only applied to the run environment but not to memory operations, causing `memory_search`/`memory_get` to still resolve against the agent's default workspace directory, which breaks context isolation and can leak notes from the wrong project.
bf337bc65078e978: Unconditionally clearing `workspaceOverride` in `resolveConfiguredBindingRoute()` causes ACP rebinds for the same agent to silently drop a previously configured workspace override, falling back to the agent's default workspace on every turn after the first.
d38993f376cae668: The `isLikelyVisionModel` heuristic incorrectly flags all model IDs containing "gpt-5" as image-capable, which will cause text-only GPT-5-family models (e.g., `openai-gpt-52`) to advertise image support and send `input_image` payloads that the upstream model cannot handle.
3c571776d297650c: When `readAuthStoreMtimeMs` returns `null` (i.e., the auth file is absent), the cache is incorrectly populated with an empty store and all subsequent calls short-circuit before syncing external CLI credentials, leaving auth resolution permanently stale until a process restart.
a3e0f428011da327: The patch for `@mariozechner/pi-ai` is only applied in the local pnpm workspace and is not included in the published npm package, meaning users who install `openclaw` globally via npm will still be affected by the signature-rejection bug.
ce204c484e904df6: Re-running the custom onboard flow unconditionally overwrites a user's intentional text-only input configuration with `["text", "image"]` based on model ID heuristics, which can cause unsupported image payloads to be sent to proxies that only accept text.
49b8ba593fe75dee: Forwarding `workspaceOverride` into cross-chat `message()` sends causes Discord interactive components to persist and restore the source conversation's workspace instead of the destination's, reintroducing the workspace bleed the feature was designed to prevent.
a9f6ec6bc34f590e: By aggregating routes from a global registry pool instead of scoping to the current `params.registry`, the gateway HTTP handler inadvertently exposes plugin routes from other agents' workspaces, allowing foreign or stale webhook endpoints to leak across workspace boundaries.
fe570ace147e47e1: The new route matcher can find routes from pooled registries, but the gateway auth precheck only consults the original registry, causing requests to pooled routes requiring gateway auth to be incorrectly rejected and fall through to 404 handling instead of being authenticated and routed to the plugin.
fe98a39142a62e10: Adding the push-only `build-artifacts` job as a dependency of `build-smoke` causes `build-smoke` to be silently skipped on pull requests, removing previously enforced build and smoke test coverage for PRs.
293351d06961a1b3: Removing the `secrets` CI job eliminates all enforcement of private key detection, workflow security auditing, and production dependency vulnerability scanning, allowing PRs and pushes with committed secrets or vulnerable dependencies to merge without being blocked.
b2787ccd716481fd: The auto-thread fallback unconditionally copies `toolContext.currentThreadTs` into `replyToId` regardless of whether the destination Mattermost channel matches the originating thread's channel, causing cross-context sends to use a mismatched `rootId` and breaking posts to other Mattermost targets.
c44176f1f3be35f7: The new thread context inheritance fallback ignores `replyToMode` and `hasRepliedRef`, causing messages sent under a `replyToMode="first"` session to incorrectly continue threading all replies instead of only the first one.
61ff4ef3ca88beae: The newly added `headingAware` chunking option is read by `mergeConfig()` but is missing from the Zod runtime schema, so any config that attempts to enable it will be rejected at load time and the feature can never be activated through normal configuration.
f0c0e96849eb985e: The `isHeading()` function incorrectly identifies `#` characters inside fenced or indented code blocks as Markdown headings, causing `headingAware` chunking to split documents in the middle of code examples rather than at true section boundaries.
ae435a8b6d658874: When syncing a fresh auth profile credential to sibling agent directories, cooldown/disabled state is not cleared for those siblings, causing them to remain blocked from using the newly propagated credential until the cooldown window expires.
ff52764c7ced0550: The `mergeConfig` function now returns the raw `chunking.overlap` value instead of the previously computed clamped value, meaning configs where `overlap >= tokens` will no longer be normalized to `tokens - 1`, causing `chunkMarkdown()` to produce ever-growing chunks that duplicate text and can exceed embedding size limits.
1e8b04f9545986f7: Making the `node` parameter optional for the `describe` action while reusing a canvas-only default node resolver means that non-canvas nodes will still throw an error when no node is explicitly specified, even though `node.describe` supports any connected node type.
e9cfd6aba27d8fd1: The thread-inheritance fallback silently fails for manual or embedded Mattermost runs because `to` is normalized to a canonical form (e.g., `channel:<id>`) before `handleAction()` runs while `currentChannelId` retains the raw original value (e.g., `#channel` or a bare ID), causing the exact equality check to never match even when both refer to the same conversation.
3344120f1a28f94f: If a Telegram polling session gets stuck before completing its first successful `getUpdates` call, the channel health snapshot remains in a state (`connected: undefined`, `lastEventAt: null`) that bypasses the stale-socket check entirely, causing `evaluateChannelHealth()` to incorrectly report healthy even after the grace period expires and preventing the liveness monitor from restarting the hung session.
f744b6673ca10e5e: Setting `connected: false` unconditionally in the `finally` block during Telegram poll retries can cause the gateway to report as fully unready during recoverable outages, because the `disconnected` status is not gracefully ignored by the readiness check the way other transient states are.
66fd0ba945824961: The preview path emits `emptyAllowlistWarnings` without sanitization, allowing crafted config keys containing control characters or ANSI escapes to inject forged content into terminal output, breaking the existing log sanitization guardrail.
d01896b4c4949516: When syncing a new auth profile credential to sibling agent directories, the helper fails to update each sibling's `store.order`, causing the newly synced profile to be invisible on any sibling that has a local auth-order override, since `resolveAuthProfileOrder()` prioritizes `store.order` over config.
ca5997c59cab43db: Marking the `openclaw` peer dependency as optional in `peerDependenciesMeta` bypasses npm's version compatibility enforcement during plugin installation, allowing users on incompatible older OpenClaw releases to install the plugin without error and only encounter broken `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` imports at runtime.
5415376e92af34eb: The Twitch extension now imports from the new `openclaw/plugin-sdk/twitch` subpath, but no minimum `openclaw` peer dependency version is declared, allowing older hosts to install the package and fail at load time when their loader cannot resolve the new subpath alias.
f3edaf38dd1a163d: Replacing `normalizeCommandBody` with `normalizeCommandBodyLite` in `buildCommandContext` breaks alias canonicalization, causing alias commands like `/id` in group mentions to fall through unhandled instead of being mapped to their canonical equivalents like `/whoami`.
047942eb6a669594: Adding `bad-barnacle` to the stale exemption list has no effect because PRs are actually auto-closed by a separate workflow (`auto-response.yml`) that triggers on the `dirty` label, meaning the intended protection against Barnacle closures is never applied.
e3598b87d7cdfb41: The startup migration deletes per-agent `sessions.json` files that post-migration discovery logic still depends on, causing migrated session stores to become invisible to combined-store callers immediately after the first migrating boot.
56703224f6ce1510: After a session store is migrated to the `sessions.d` directory format, the subagent depth checker continues reading the old `sessions.json` file, causing spawn depth to always resolve to 1 and allowing over-limit subagents to keep spawning unchecked.
c846ab86fc337de3: Setting `overflow: clip` on the usage card container clips absolutely-positioned dropdown menus used by filters and export controls, causing options that extend beyond the card bounds to be cut off and potentially unreachable, which is a functional regression in the usage filtering and export workflow.
1118e80c7533ed13: When `preflightDiscordMessage()` returns `null` due to transient failures (e.g., REST hydration errors), the message ID is not cleared from `recentInboundMessages`, causing legitimate redeliveries of the same message to be incorrectly dropped for up to 5 minutes.
37f3abda3ab9fb60: The deduplication check drops redelivered messages that arrive while the first attempt is still in-flight, and since the error handler only clears the cache key for future deliveries without replaying the already-dropped retry, a message can be permanently lost if the first attempt fails before Discord sends another copy.
3d576a4abef4bad3: Replacing the responsive `max-width: min(900px, calc(100% - 60px))` with a hard-coded `max-width: 50%` on `.chat-group-messages` forces message bubbles into a half-width column at all screen sizes, causing excessive text wrapping and poor readability on small/mobile screens.
f4e09005be8bf2b8: Setting `overflow-x: auto` on `.daily-chart-bars` causes vertically-positioned bar tooltips to be clipped and unreadable, since browsers treat overflow as hidden in all directions when any overflow axis is set to `auto`.
23285be131b39df2: When `compaction` is set to `false`, the preemptive context overflow signal is never raised, causing over-budget prompts to be submitted to the provider anyway and triggering a costly failed prefill before session compaction kicks in — defeating the purpose of the flag.
b02c216a94029c9c: Setting `deliver: false` when `turnSourceChannel` or `turnSourceTo` is absent breaks session-fallback delivery for exec follow-ups, causing approved background executions on outbound channels like Telegram and WhatsApp to silently drop their completion messages instead of routing through the gateway's existing `session.lastChannel/lastTo` fallback.
928ca25f05151df4: When an inline query times out, the in-flight `llmPromise` is never cancelled, causing expensive abandoned LLM runs and unnecessary session writes to accumulate silently in the background.
edd12a60dbb95a60: When `dispatchReplyWithBufferedBlockDispatcher` rejects in the inline research callback handler, `spinnerInterval` and `deferTimer` are never cleared in the error path, causing those timers to leak and indefinitely fire repeated Telegram API edit attempts.
25635c917f62aa4a: When `selectedToActive` baking is enabled, the script incorrectly deselects all objects and reselects only the target mesh, leaving no source objects selected and causing the high-to-low poly bake path to fail or produce incorrect results.
9852ec89460c7e11: The inline query handler skips DM policy enforcement because `isSenderAllowed` treats an empty allowlist as permitted, allowing any Telegram user to trigger the LLM path even when `dmPolicy` is set to `"pairing"` or `"disabled"`.
9043e512843d8c40: The 10-minute inline query cleanup deletes the map entry but never resolves or rejects `inlineMessageIdPromise`, leaving the `Promise.all` chain permanently pending and leaking captured state for every timed-out query, causing unbounded memory growth under normal inline typing usage.
c3e1f6c9248a346d: A raw API key from `skills.entries["nano-banana-pro"].apiKey` is being interpolated directly into LLM prompt text during inline image research, exposing the credential to the model, its output, and any downstream logs or history instead of being passed through secure secret/environment plumbing.
